Triggers Level 7 Abberath's Fury when Equipped +(20-30) to Strength 15% increased Movement Speed (6-10)% chance to Ignite When you Kill an Ignited Enemy, inflict an equivalent Ignite on each nearby Enemy 1% increased Fire Damage per 20 Strength Burning Hoofprints. Abberath's Hooves | Description | Abberath's Hooves is a pair of unique Goathide Boots.
Abberath's Hooves | Skill functions and interactions | Abberath's Fury : can be supported by socketed support gems.
Abberath's Hooves | Modifiers | Area of effect : The on-kill effect has a range of 2 meters. Modifiers to area of effect do not affect this. [1]
Abberath's Hooves | Modifiers | Equivalent Ignite : Ignites inflicted by this effect are 'equivalent' in DPS and remaining duration , [2] but they are not inflicted by the skill that applied the original ignite. Accordingly, they cannot apply Combustion Support 's fire resistance penalty or benefit from Item Rarity Support.
Abberath's Hooves | Modifiers | Explosions : While the on-kill ignite effect does not strictly require enemies to become corpses, it is prevented by most explosion effects. [3]
Abberath's Hooves | Modifiers | Proxies and minions : Because kills by ignite are always credited to the player, it's possible for the on-kill effect to spread ignite inflicted by proxies such as totems , and even by minions. As these ignites have a dps value equivalent to the original ignite, they may deal damage regardless of Ancestral Bond.
Abberath's Hooves | Modifiers | On-ignite effects : The on-kill effect works with when you Ignite an Enemy effects, as it applies ignite to each individual enemy. (Unlike proliferation.)
Abberath's Hooves | Skills | Abberath's Fury

Abhorrent Interrogation | Description | Abhorrent Interrogation is a pair of unique Ambush Mitts.
Abhorrent Interrogation | Mechanics | Enemies take 4% increased Elemental Damage from your Hits for each Withered you have inflicted on them : This modifier increases enemies' damage taken from hits, based on how many Withered debuffs were inflicted by the hitting entity. [1]
Abhorrent Interrogation | Mechanics | With the maximum Withered limit of 15, a total of 60% increased elemental damage taken can be applied to enemies. This does not scale with #% increased/reduced Effect of Withered. It is in addition to the regular increased chaos damage taken , not a replacement.
Abhorrent Interrogation | Mechanics | Your Hits cannot Penetrate or ignore Elemental Resistances : This modifier prevents elemental penetration , such as Lightning Penetration Support , as well as effects that would cause resistance to be ignored entirely, such as Inquisitor 's Inevitable Judgement.
Abhorrent Interrogation | Mechanics | Examples of resistance modification that can be combined with this unique:
Abhorrent Interrogation | Mechanics | Effects that invert resistances (Ex. Hits have 15% chance to treat Enemy Monster Elemental Resistance values as inverted Elemental Mastery , Rakiata's Dance , Wild Strike of Extremes ) are also unaffected.

Actum | Description | Actum is a unique Butcher Axe.
Actum | Mechanics | Critical Strike Chance is (30-40)% for Hits with this Weapon : This modifier affects the final critical strike chance for hits with this weapon. It is not affected by effects that increase, decrease, or modify critical strike chance, including Marylene's Fallacy and Overwhelm. It is still affected by effects that guarantee or disable critical strikes entirely, such as Resolute Technique and Diamond Shrines.
Actum | Mechanics | You have no Intelligence : Your intelligence is set to 0.

Alberon's Warpath | Description | Alberon's Warpath is a pair of unique Soldier Boots.
Alberon's Warpath | Mechanics | Summoned Skeleton Warriors are Permanent and Follow you : Skeleton Warriors summoned with Summon Skeletons no longer have a limited duration and follow the character around like other permanent minions. These Skeleton Warriors can also be recalled using Convocation. Skeleton Warriors summoned with Vaal Summon Skeletons do not become permanent and follow the character.
Alberon's Warpath | Mechanics | Summon Skeletons cannot Summon more than 1 Skeleton Warrior : This causes the Summon Skeleton skill to summon only one Skeleton Warrior per cast. It does not change the maximum number of skeletons that the character can summon. Skeleton Warriors summoned with Vaal Summon Skeletons are not affected by this.
Alberon's Warpath | Mechanics | #% increased Strength : The character's strength is increased by a percentage.
Alberon's Warpath | Item acquisition | Alberon's Warpath can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Alpha's Howl | Infobox | Evasion: (664-850). Requires Level 64 , 138 Dex. +2 to Level of Socketed Aura Gems (80-100)% increased Evasion Rating +(20-30)% to Cold Resistance 25% chance to Avoid being Chilled Cannot be Frozen 16% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills. Alpha's Howl | Description | Alpha's Howl is a unique Sinner Tricorne.
Alpha's Howl | Mechanics | 16% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills : This modifier applies to all skills, not just those that are socketed in Alpha's Howl. The effect is additive with increased (Mana) Reservation Efficiency modifiers from passives and other items. However, the mana reservation efficiency itself has a diminishing marginal return on the actual mana reserved so that the reserved mana never reach zero, even stacking the efficiency to positive infinite. Read Reservation § Reducing reservation for details.
Alpha's Howl | Item acquisition | Alpha's Howl can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Alpha's Howl | Recipes | Alpha's Howl can be created from the following recipes:

Alpha's Howl | Build of the Week | Alpha's Howl was featured in Season 2 Episode 10 , Episode 13 , and Episode 14 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.
Alpha's Howl | Design attribution | Alpha's Howl was created by supporter TrueOmega. [1]
Altered Distant Memory | Infobox | Map Level: 81 Map Tier: 11 Item Quantity: +100% Monster Pack Size: +25%. 100% increased Experience gain Monster Level: 81 Map has (3-5) additional random Modifiers Map has (2-4) additional Synthesis Global Modifiers. Corrupted. The winters of my youth were cold and harsh. We had nothing, yet to my mother's credit, I wanted for so little. Travel to this Map by using it in a personal Map Device. Altered Distant Memory | Description | Altered Distant Memory is a unique Synthesised Map. The base map is T10, but the area level of the unique map is overridden as 81.
Altered Distant Memory | Description | Altered Distant Memory is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map. However, this map can be witnessed by the Maven as part of the prerequisites of Maven's Invitation: The Forgotten.
Altered Distant Memory | Description | Despite not on the atlas, this map can still be affected by atlas passive skill that affects Synthesised Map. Note that atlas passive skill cannot affect maps from Kirac's atlas mission.
Altered Distant Memory | Modifiers | The map has two pools of map modifiers, one draw from "synthesis global" pool (mod domain = 19), one from "synthesis local" pool (mod domain = 18). Synthesis map modifiers are not visible on the map itself, and these modifiers are only revealed upon entering the map.
Altered Distant Memory | Layout | The map uses the tileset similar to the Siege Map , which is based on Act 10 areas The Canals and The Feeding Trough , but without the blood canals part. The area contains several bridges and blockades along the road. The boundaries of the map are surrounded in blue memory decay typical of a Synthesis zone, but cannot be directly interacted with. A portal to the boss room is located at the top left.
Altered Distant Memory | Boss | Altered Synthete
Altered Distant Memory | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Altered Distant Memory | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Altered Distant Memory | Item acquisition | Altered Distant Memory has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from T14+ Map Bosses, and sometimes sold by Kirac or Tujen.
Altered Distant Memory | Area restrictions | This item can be acquired in the following areas:
Altered Distant Memory | Recipes | Altered Distant Memory can be created from the following recipes:
Altered Distant Memory | Legacy variants | Altered Distant Memory has legacy variants due to changes in basetype: map series, tier and etc.
Altered Distant Memory | Legacy variants | The map is dropped as a corrupted item since version 3.17.0. Even using a non-corrupted item from older map series, the vendor recipe would exchange the old map with a new Altered Distant Memory unique Synthesised Map (of the current map series), but corrupted.

Ancestral Vision | Description | Ancestral Vision is a unique Viridian Jewel.
Ancestral Vision | Mechanics | Modifiers to Chance to Suppress Spell Damage also apply to Chance to Avoid Elemental Ailments at 50% of their Value : This modifier grants characters 1% chance to avoid elemental ailments for every 2% chance to spell suppression. Modifiers that grant spell suppression conditionally also grant ailment avoidance conditionally.
Ancestral Vision | Mechanics | Ancestral Vision is unaffected by Acrobatics 's Modifiers to Chance to Suppress Spell Damage instead apply to Chance to Dodge Spell Hits at 50% of their value : avoidance may still be gained from spell suppression modifiers. Because both stats use 50% of the original modifier value, reaching the cap of 75% Chance to Dodge Spell Hits would also give 75% Chance to Avoid Elemental Ailments.
Ancestral Vision | Item acquisition | Ancestral Vision can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Angler's Plait | Description | Angler's Plait is a unique Unset Ring.
Angler's Plait | Modifiers | Angler's Plait is always obtained as a corrupted item. The implicit Has 1 Socket is always replaced by You can catch Corrupted Fish. A Tainted Catalyst must be used to reroll a random catalyst quality as well as a roll from (0-20)% quality.
Angler's Plait | Item acquisition | Angler's Plait has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Extremely rarely drops from Rare enemies while the player has a Corrupted Slitherpinch equipped.
Angler's Plait | Recipes | Angler's Plait can be created from the following recipes:
Angler's Plait | Recipes | Angler's Plait can only be obtained corruped and is not in the core drop pool, therefore divination cards such as The Penitent , Hubris , Blind Venture , and Jack in the Box cannot reward an Angler's Plait. Baited Expectations appears to circumvent this restriction by using a custom item list. [1] [2]
Angler's Plait | Recipes | Angler's Plait can only drop from a Rare monster while the player has a corrupted Slitherpinch equipped. It is a deterministic drop that is unaffected by IIR or IIQ. [3]
Angler's Plait | Recipes | The original requirements for this item to drop were shared by user /u/poorFishwife on May 6, 2020 [4]. It was originally speculated that the player having a means of inflicting fleeing (e.g. Chance to Flee Support or Blackheart ) was also a requirement. However, this was disproven in April 2025, where three drops were documented by user Surr on a character with no method of inflicting fleeing including ignite (or any fire damage) or minions. [5] [6] This was further corroborated by other sources. [7]. In October 2025 the same user demonstrated the outcome of the recipe to vendor 60 Angler's Plait rings. The result was a Loreweave, the normal outcome for this type of recipe. [8].
Angler's Plait | Status as an Easter egg | The inclusion of Angler's Plait in Path of Exile is an Easter egg in reference to a running joke by Grinding Gear Games about the existence of secret gameplay features related to fishing. It has so far not been confirmed that any such gameplay features legitimately exist.
Angler's Plait | Design attribution | Angler's Plait was created by supporter SauronSatan. [9] [10] [ better source needed ]

Asenath's Chant | Infobox | Energy Shield: (112-135). Requires Level 45 , 23 Int. Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Attack with a Bow , with a 0.3 second Cooldown (10-15)% increased Attack Speed (10-15)% increased Cast Speed +(100-120) to maximum Energy Shield (30-40)% increased Stun and Block Recovery 30% increased Mana Regeneration Rate 5% increased Movement Speed. Vendor Offer 2x Alchemy Shard 12x Alteration Shard
Asenath's Chant | Description | Asenath's Chant is a unique Iron Circlet.
Asenath's Chant | Mechanics | Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Attack with a Bow : Each time you attack with a bow , a socketed spell will be cast. Each spell has a separate 0.3 second cooldown. The triggered spells are targeted where the attack was targeted.
Asenath's Chant | Mechanics | Note that the game usually prevent a skill to link with two trigger mechanics. For example, you CANNOT socket a spell in Asenath's Chant which is support by Hextouch and another curse skill (which curse skills are spells). That would make the curse is supported by both Hextouch and the mod Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Attack with a Bow , and making the curse unable to be triggered.
Asenath's Chant | Item acquisition | Asenath's Chant is drop disabled.
Asenath's Chant | Legacy variants | Asenath's Chant has a legacy variant.
Asenath's Chant | Legacy variants | According to the patch notes the legacy mod value is cosmetic only. The actual chance is 100%.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Infobox | Energy Shield: (18-23). Requires Level 25 , 39 Int. +(20-30) to Intelligence +(60-80) to maximum Life +(60-80) to maximum Mana Curse Enemies with Temporal Chains on Hit Non- Aura Curses you inflict are not removed from Dying Enemies Enemies near corpses affected by your Curses are Blinded Enemies Killed near corpses affected by your Curses explode, dealing 3% of their Life as Physical Damage. Cool the head and cool the blade. Acquisition Drop level: 75 League-specific • Legion. Vendor Offer 6x Alchemy Shard 7x Alteration Shard
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Description | Asenath's Gentle Touch is a pair of unique Silk Gloves.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Mechanics | Asenath's Gentle Touch inflicts a Temporal Chains debuff on hit, with values equivalent to a level 1 Temporal Chains skill. Hits from reflected damage or items like Scold's Bridle will not apply the curse to yourself.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Mechanics | Corrupting (such as vaaling ) the gloves may add a corruption implicit , including a second Curse Enemies on Hit modifier. If the player can apply multiple curses, such as with Whispers of Doom , Unholy Authority , or Cospri's Will , both curses may be applied by one hit. If the character's curse limit is 1, the curse applied will be determined by the curse priority.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Item acquisition | Asenath's Gentle Touch can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Asenath's Gentle Touch belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Recipes | Asenath's Gentle Touch can be created from the following recipes:
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Legacy variants | Asenath's Gentle Touch has a legacy variant.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Legacy variants | pre-3.7 and pre-2.6 variants are used to be different. But in version 3.20.0 , all post-2.6 variants lost the "with 40% increased Effect" in the curse mod text. The two legacy variants use different mod filters in the official trade site, as well as show up different default internal values (10 verse 100) [1]
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Legacy variants | A Divine Orb cannot add the mods Non-Aura Curses you inflict are not removed from Dying Enemies , Enemies near Corpses affected by your Curses are Blinded and Enemies killed near Corpses affected by your Curses explode, dealing 3% of their Life as Physical Damage to the item.
Asenath's Gentle Touch | Lore | Asenath's Gentle Touch was associated with the Maraketh legion as a Legion mechanic drop-restricted (now league-specific ) unique. The item itself references Sekhema Asenath.

Asenath's Mark | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Asenath's Mark | Legacy variants | Asenath's Mark has legacy variants.
Asenath's Mark | Legacy variants | Note that using a Divine Orb on this item will not grant Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Attack with a Bow, with a 0.3 second Cooldown and 30-60% increased Spell Damage.
Asenath's Mark | Legacy variants | Unlike some other items with legacy variants, the legacy mods 5% increased Movement Speed , 10-15% increased Stun and Block Recovery and 10-15% increased Cast Speed are not removed with a Divine Orb. However, mod values such as the legacy +(50) to maximum Energy Shield roll may change. Always use advanced mod display mode (PC keyboard default bind: Alt button) to check the mod value range before using the orb.

Ashcaller | Description | Ashcaller is a unique Goat's Horn.
Ashcaller | Mechanics | " Trigger Level 10 Summon Raging Spirit on Kill " can only be triggered with kills by the player character. Kills by the character's Totems or Traps do not trigger the skill. Minion Support gems like Minion Damage Support support the Summon Raging Spirit trigger skill, they do not need to be linked, just needed to be socketed in this wand.
Ashcaller | Item acquisition | Ashcaller can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Ashcaller | Recipes | Ashcaller can be created from the following recipes:
Ashcaller | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Ashcaller | Legacy variants | Ashcaller has legacy variants.
Ashcaller | Legacy variants | The pre-3.8 double legacy variant use the same local Adds # to # Fire Damage mod as the current variant, so that the double legacy variant can be divined to update the mod values at the cost of the price of the orb and lower #% increased Burning Damage mod value. However, using the orb cannot gain the new 10% chance to Cover Enemies in Ash on Hit mod.

Ashes of the Sun | Description | Ashes of the Sun is a unique Driftwood Club. It always comes with three linked white sockets.
Ashes of the Sun | Mechanics | The 500% increased Ignite Duration on Enemies and 30% chance to Ignite modifiers can be deceivingly useful for enabling certain Ignite builds. This Unique was in combination with Infernal Cry and Dynamite to create extremely long-lasting proliferating Ignites that can clear Delve nodes even while the player is dead. [1]

Atziri's Reflection | Description | Atziri's Reflection is a unique Golden Buckler.
Atziri's Reflection | Modifiers | Unaffected by Curses : The mod is not equal to curse immunity. This shield can interact with item such as Coward's Chains / Coward's Legacy , since you still have the curse on you, despite the curse does not have any debuff effect applied. The help text is Debuffs you are Unaffected by can still be placed on you, but will not actually apply their effect.
Atziri's Reflection | Modifiers | When an enemy inflicts a Hex curse on a character equipped with Atziri's Mirror, Hex Reflection causes that Hex curse to be inflicted on that enemy as well.
Atziri's Reflection | Item acquisition | Atziri's Reflection has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Only drops from Uber Atziri.
Atziri's Reflection | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Atziri's Reflection | Recipes | Atziri's Reflection can be created from the following recipes:
Atziri's Reflection | Legacy variants | Atziri's Reflection has a quasi- legacy variant. Legacy modifiers and base items were retained when upgrading unique to their fated counterparts. So because Atziri's Mirror had legacy variants, Atziri's Reflection effectively did as well. Since version 3.17.0 , however, due to the removal of prophecy system, Atziri's Mirror cannot be upgraded to Atziri's Reflection. There is no reason to do so by using legacy Atziri's Mirror anyway, since the mods were inferior than the post-3.0 variant. Note that since Atziri's Reflection is a boss drop, it cannot be an influenced item anymore.
Atziri's Reflection | Legacy variants | The current version of Atziri's Reflection is not considered fated and drops generically.

Atziri went to great lengths to ensure her immortality, but nothing is eternal. Place into an allocated Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Right click to remove from the Socket. Atziri's Rule | Description | Atziri's Rule is a unique Judgement Staff.
Atziri's Rule | Mechanics | Queen's Demand is a skill that triggers Flames of Judgement or Storm of Judgement , alternating with each cast. Because you cannot use a trigger on another trigger, Queen's Demand can't be supported with trigger support gems.
Atziri's Rule | Support Gems | Atziri's Rule can not be supported by the following gems. [1]
Atziri's Rule | Support Gems | Queen's Demand can be supported by Spell Echo Support or Unleash Support , which will cause Queen's Demand to repeat, triggering both Flames of Judgement and Storm of Judgement. The triggered spells can not be supported by spell echo or unleash, and will therefore not receive less spell damage from the support gem.

Augmented Distant Memory | Infobox | Map Level: 81 Map Tier: 11 Item Quantity: +100% Monster Pack Size: +25%. 100% increased Experience gain Monster Level: 81 Map has (3-5) additional random Modifiers Map has (2-4) additional Synthesis Global Modifiers. Acquisition Drop restricted Drop level: 81 The Maven's Crucible Drops from T14+ Map Bosses, and sometimes sold by Kirac or Tujen. Vendor Offer 12x Alchemy Shard
Augmented Distant Memory | Description | Augmented Distant Memory is a unique Synthesised Map. The base map may have variable tier, but the area level of the unique map is override as 81.
Augmented Distant Memory | Description | Augmented Distant Memory is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map. However, this map can be witnessed by the Maven as part of the prerequisites of Maven's Invitation: The Forgotten.
Augmented Distant Memory | Description | Despite not being on the atlas, this map can still be affected by atlas passive skills that affect Synthesised Maps. Note that atlas passive skills cannot affect maps from Kirac's atlas missions.
Augmented Distant Memory | Modifiers | The map has two pools of map modifiers, one draw from "synthesis global" pool (mod domain = 19), one from "synthesis local" pool (mod domain = 18). Synthesis map modifiers are not visible on the map itself, and these modifiers are only revealed upon entering the map.
Augmented Distant Memory | Layout | The map uses The Torched Courts tileset. The area contains several bookshelves that block paths and separate rooms. Some bookshelves contain holes which indicate that they can be teleported through using Blink Arrow or Flame Dash or other similar movement skills. The boundaries of the map are surrounded in blue memory decay typical of a Synthesis zone, but cannot be directly interacted with. A portal to the boss room is located at the top left.

Augmented Distant Memory | Legacy variants | Augmented Distant Memory has legacy variants.
Augmented Distant Memory | Legacy variants | The map is dropped as a corrupted item since version 3.17.0. Even using a non-corrupted item from older map series, the vendor recipe would exchange the old map with a new Augmented Distant Memory unique Synthesised Map (of the current map series), but corrupted.

Aukuna's Will | Description | Aukuna's Will is a pair of unique Clasped Mitts. The item is named after Aukuna, the Black Sekhema , the general of Maraketh legion encounter.
Aukuna's Will | Mechanics | Your Raised Zombies count as corpses : This modifier allows your Raise Zombie minions to be used as corpses , including being consumed or detonated by corpse explosions. Note this also allows enemies to detonate your zombies.
Aukuna's Will | Item acquisition | Aukuna's Will can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Aukuna's Will belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Berek's Respite | Description | Berek's Respite is a unique ruby and topaz Two-Stone Ring.
Berek's Respite | Mechanics and interactions | Area of effect : The on-kill effects have a range of 2 meters. Modifiers to area of effect do not affect this. [1]
Berek's Respite | Mechanics and interactions | Equivalent Shock/Ignite : Ailments inflicted by this effect are 'equivalent' in effect/DPS and remaining duration , [2] but they are not inflicted by any skill. Accordingly, they cannot apply Combustion Support 's fire resistance penalty or benefit from Item Rarity Support.
Berek's Respite | Mechanics and interactions | Explosions : While the on-kill effect does not strictly require enemies to become corpses, it is prevented by most explosion effects. [3]
Berek's Respite | Mechanics and interactions | Proxies and minions : Because kills by ignite are always credited to the player, it's possible for this ring to spread ailments inflicted by proxies such as totems , and even by minions. As these ignites have a dps value equivalent to the original ignite, they may deal damage regardless of Ancestral Bond.
Berek's Respite | Mechanics and interactions | On-ignite/shock effects : This ring's on-kill effects work with when you Ignite/Shock an Enemy effects, as they apply ailments to each individual enemy. (Unlike proliferation.)
Berek's Respite | Item acquisition | Berek's Respite can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Berek's Respite belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Bitter Instinct | Description | Bitter Instinct is a unique Spiny Round Shield.
Bitter Instinct | Mechanics | Damaging Retaliation Skills become Usable every 4 seconds : This modifier allows damaging retribution skills to be used without needing to activate their condition (e.g. blocking a hit). This mod does not affect Vengeful Cry , as it is not considered a damaging retaliation skill, even with Corrupting Cry Support. This mod does not prevent activating conditions from making retaliation skills available. This mod is not affected by Expert Retaliation Support.

Blackflame | Mechanics | Enemies Ignited by you take Chaos Damage instead of Fire Damage from Ignite : This modifier causes enemies ignited by you or your skills to treat damage taken from ignite as being defensively chaos damage rather than fire damage. Blood of the Karui | Description | Blood of the Karui is a unique Sanctified Life Flask.
Blood of the Karui | Mechanics | It first recovers the life as a regular flask would do, and at the end of the flask duration - if you are still missing life - it will recover your entire missing life pool (meaning that if the initial recovery heals you to your max life, the secondary effect will not take place at the end of the flask duration).
Blood of the Karui | Item acquisition | Blood of the Karui can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Blood of the Karui belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.
Blood of the Karui | Recipes | Blood of the Karui can be created from the following recipes:
Blood of the Karui | Legacy variants | Blood of the Karui has legacy variants.
Blood of the Karui | Legacy variants | Moreover, during version 3.13.X, Fateful Meeting can grant a random double-influenced league-specific unique item including flask. This bug has been fixed in version 3.14.0, so that influenced version of Blood of the Karui are legacy too. There is no actual in-game benefit to use influenced flask, however. Player cannot use Influenced Exalted Orb to create an influenced flask base either.
Blood of the Karui | Alternate artwork | The flask also have Voidborn foil variants.

Blood Price | Mechanics | Nearby Enemy Monsters have at least 8% of Life Reserved : Reserving an enemy's life causes it to start with and be unable to recover above the unreserved total. This modifier does not stack. [1] This modifier works on unique enemies, but does not work on non- monsters , such as Exiles in PvP areas like Hall of Grandmasters. Modifiers to Reservation Efficiency have no effect on this mod.

Bound By Destiny | Description | Bound by Destiny is a unique Prismatic Jewel.

Bound Fate | Description | Bound Fate is a unique Cloth Belt.
Bound Fate | Mechanics | Every five seconds, the character gains one of six stat bonuses randomly.
Bound Fate | Mechanics | Mechanically, Bound Fate changes which stat bonus it grants on a fixed interval. It does not grant the character temporary effects , which means that there is no effect duration that can be modified in any way.
Bound Fate | Mechanics | It can be difficult to discern which of the random stat bonuses is applied to the character at any given time. There is no effect icon to indicate it, and these stats are not shown on the Character screen or in skill tooltips. [1]
Bound Fate | Item acquisition | Bound Fate can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Breach item | Description | Breach items are a category of unique items associated with Breach.
Breach item | Description | Breach uniques can be birthed from an Ancient Wombgift using the Genesis Tree , dropped from Grasping Coffers found in a Hive Fortress or Hive Colony , or obtained as a boss drop from the bosses It That Was Esh and It That Was Tul in a Hive Colony. Certain passives on the Genesis Tree grant increased chance for birthed uniques to be a Breach unique. Breach uniques can be transformed into their alternate counterparts using a File:Flesh of Xesht inventory icon.png.
Breach item | List of breach items | Note that although Uul-Netol's Vow , which can drop from any Breachlord in a Flawless Breachstone , is both Breach-exclusive and a unique item, it is not considered to be part of the category of Breach items. It was not part of the Blessing upgrade system and is considered an additional drop in permanent leagues. In Keepers league, it can drop exclusively from It That Was Tul and It That Was Esh.

Breathstealer | Description | Breathstealer is a pair of unique Hydrascale Gauntlets.
Breathstealer | Mechanics | Breathstealer can be anointed by Sister Cassia or in the Blight Stash Tab. Anointments are retained if the item is corrupted into a rare Hydrascale Gauntlets , allowing the use of a Tainted Mythic Orb to potentially create an anointed Gravebind.
Breathstealer | Mechanics | Create a Blighted Spore when your Skills or Minions Kill a Rare Monster : Blighted Spores are allies with a random Aura , affecting the player and other nearby allies. Random auras can include those that come from monster modifiers. Blighted spores do not move, can be damaged and killed, and have an unmodifiable 10 second duration.
Breathstealer | Mechanics | Breathstealer does not create Blighted Spores when equipped by Animate Guardian or Animate Guardian of Smiting.
Breathstealer | Item acquisition | Breathstealer has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
Breathstealer | Item acquisition | Can drop after completing a Blight encounter in a Blighted map.

Brutal Restraint | Description | Brutal Restraint is a unique Timeless Jewel.
Brutal Restraint | Mechanics | Brutal Restraint adds +2 Dexterity to all travel passives in its radius (+10 to Dexterity / Intelligence / Strength ), +4 Dexterity to all other small passives and adds random modifiers to notable passives. It transforms Keystone passives into exclusive Keystone passives not normally available to the passive skill tree.

However, even the jewel was obtained before version 3.11.0 , using a Divine Orb , the player cannot obtain Deshret variant anymore. Moreover, using a Divine Orb on legacy jewel Deshret variant, will delete the "Deshret" mod value forever.

Cadigan's Authority | Mechanics | Battlemage
Cadigan's Authority | Mechanics | You cannot have more than 2 Summoned Totems of the same type : "Totems of the same type" refers to the type of totem itself, not the skill it uses. For example, two individual skills linked to individual Spell Totem Supports are considered the same type, as both are "Spell Totems".

Cadigan's Crown | Description | Cadigan's Crown is a unique Runic Crown.
Cadigan's Crown | Mechanics | As the helmet prevents critical strikes, it cannot be combined with crit builds or Elemental Overload. This can be fine for builds such as Ignite builds with Shaper of Flames and low hit rate (therefore having difficulty proccing EO), or Poison builds with Inextricable Fate or Beacon of Madness , and a weapon with three high-tier flat Elemental Damage rolls.

Cameria's Avarice | Recipes | [1]
Cameria's Avarice | Legacy variants | Since Cameria's Maul has a legacy variant (item with legacy 40(45)% increased Stun Duration on Enemies [a] implicit), Cameria's Avarice carried over the legacy mod when doing the fated prophecy (until the prophecy mechanic is removed in version 3.17.0 ). However, this is considered as a quasi-legacy version, as Cameria's Avarice is released after the change in the implicit so that it can only able to do so in the permanent leagues, and apart from the legacy implicit, the item's explicit mods are identical with the current variant. In fact, players does not need a legacy Cameria's Maul, but a legacy Gavel , and then chance a "legacy" Cameria's Maul, and then fulfilling the Cold Greed prophecy.
Cameria's Avarice | Legacy variants | It is not confirmed that when using the new vendor recipe that introduced in version 3.19.0 , the legacy implicit mod carry over or not.

Cameria's Maul | Design attribution | Cameria's Maul was created by supporter RichMoney (RichJMoney). [1] [2]
Cameria's Maul | Design attribution | The name "Cameria's Maul" is a word play on Mall of America , a famous tourist attraction located in RichMoney's home state of Minnesota. RichMoney stated that he wanted to create a unique weapon with the Gavel base type as a " troll " or prank, so that when an unidentified unique Gavel drops, some players will be disappointed when they see that it is Cameria's Maul instead of Mjölner. [1] Because of this, Cameria's Maul is sometimes called "Trollner". [2]
Cameria's Maul | Design attribution | RichMoney also created Cameria's Avarice and The Thaumaturgist.

Cane of Unravelling | Description | Cane of Unravelling is a unique Ezomyte Staff.
Cane of Unravelling | Mechanics | You get one power charge per button press and the game carries the leftover charges to the next skill if you used more than 200 mana in one go. So if you cast an 800 mana skill you get one charge, but if you then cast three zero mana skills afterwards you get one charge each time.

Ceaseless Feast | Description | Ceaseless Feast is a pair of unique Spiked Gloves.
Ceaseless Feast | Mechanics | (20–30)% chance to inflict Corrosion on Hit with Attacks : Corrosion is a stacking debuff that causes -5000 to Armour and -1000 to Evasion Rating per stack. Corrosion lasts for 4 seconds and inflicting a stack refreshes the duration of all existing Corrosion stacks.

Ceinture of Benevolence | Infobox | Requires Level 40. (15-25)% increased Stun and Block Recovery. +(20-40) to Dexterity +(40-60) to maximum Mana (10-7)% reduced Flask Charges used Non-Unique Utility Flasks you Use apply to Linked Targets. "Put your faith in me. Our god demands it." - High Templar Maxarius. Vendor Offer 6x Alchemy Shard 5x Alteration Shard
Ceinture of Benevolence | Description | Ceinture of Benevolence is a unique Cloth Belt.
Ceinture of Benevolence | Mechanics | Non-Unique Utility Flasks you Use apply to Linked Targets : This modifier causes the effects of consumed non-unique utility flasks to also apply to linked targets. These flasks, including their affixes, will benefit the recipient the same as if the recipient had the flasks equipped themselves. In the case that there are multiple of the same flask or flask suffix, only the higher numerical value will apply.
Ceinture of Benevolence | Mechanics | Flask effect modifiers local to the flask such as the increased effect enchant from an Enkindling Orb will benefit both the linker and the link recipient since the flask is being applied to both players. Modifiers that cause flasks applied to you to have increased effect such as the Natural Remedies notable will only increase flask effect on the character that has that modifier.
Ceinture of Benevolence | Item acquisition | Ceinture of Benevolence can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Ceinture of Benevolence | Recipes | Ceinture of Benevolence can be created from the following recipes:

Chains of Command | Description | Chains of Command is a unique Saintly Chainmail.
Chains of Command | Mechanics | Animate Guardian's Weapon creates an animated weapon copy of the animated guardian's main-hand weapon. These animated weapons last for a base duration of 20 seconds. Animate Guardian's Weapon has a 0.05 second cooldown, which means that at most one animated weapon can be created every 0.05 seconds, no matter how many enemies are killed at once.
Chains of Command | Mechanics | You cannot have Non-Animated, Non-Manifested Minions : While Chains of Command is equipped, the character can only have the following types of minions:
Chains of Command | Mechanics | Other types of minions are unsummoned when Chains of Command is equipped, which includes those that resemble weapons such as raised spectres of Dancing Sword. Skills that summon these other types of minions are disabled.

Chains of Emancipation | Description | Chains of Emancipation is a unique Chain Belt.
Chains of Emancipation | Mechanics | Temporal Chains is inflicted for five seconds on a hit against you. The hit must be from an enemy or environmental effect; you cannot curse yourself by hitting yourself through self-damage e.g. Boneshatter or Scold's Bridle. Rotblood Promise is an alternative way to apply and remove Temporal Chains to yourself that doesn't require being hit.
Chains of Emancipation | Mechanics | A flask of Warding of Warding Suffix Removes Curses on use is a simple and reliable way to lose Temporal Chains quickly. However, it cannot remove the map curse of Temporal Chains of Temporal Chains Suffix 13% increased Quantity of Items found in this Area 8% increased Rarity of Items found in this Area 5% increased Pack size Players are Cursed with Temporal Chains , and that persistent curse will prevent you from receiving the temporary one from a hit. In this scenario, the only way to trigger the Rage gain is to temporarily become immune to the curse using e.g. Holy Flame Totem or Kiara's Determination.
Chains of Emancipation | Mechanics | Shackles of the Wretched as a method to curse yourself with Temporal Chains then another curse like Despair (e.g. socketed with Herald of Thunder Hextouch Support or Cast when Damage Taken Support Temporal Chains with Heartbound Loop ) can be used to automate maximizing your rage after reaching 25 rage. [1] In this scenario your curse limit must be 1.

Chober Chaber | Trivia | Brother Laz , author of the famous Median XL mod for Diablo 2: Lord of Destruction, played a role in the design of Chober Chaber. [1]
Chober Chaber | Trivia | The name of the item -- as hinted by the flavor text -- comes from the beginning of Deuteronomy 18:11: "or casts spells, or who is a medium or spiritist or who consults the dead". The first expression, "[one who] casts spells" in Hebrew is חָ֑בֶר וְחֹבֵ֖ר which is transliterated as wə-ḥō-ḇêr ḥā-ḇer.
Chober Chaber | Build of the Week | Chober Chaber was featured in Season 2 Episode 14 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.

Cinderswallow Urn | Veiled modifiers | Unveiling a Catarina's Veiled prefix draws from a pool of both exclusive veiled prefixes ( Catarina's ) and generic veiled prefixes. The generic veiled prefixes compatible with flasks are incorrectly named Of the Order. [a]

Cinderswallow Urn | Ruthless mode | Although veiled rare items cannot be found in Ruthless mode , this item will still drop with a veiled modifier and can be unveiled.
Cinderswallow Urn | Ruthless mode | Utility flasks cannot gain charges while active in Ruthless mode. This flask also has a flask duration of 4.00 seconds instead.
Cinderswallow Urn | Legacy variants | Cinderswallow Urn has legacy variants :
Cinderswallow Urn | Legacy variants | Legacy variants (pre-3.14.0) of this flask use crafted mods which have a different color than normal mods. These crafted mods can be removed from the unique item but are not able to be crafted back via the Crafting Bench , effectively bricking the item. The mod text and mod value are the same between the crafted mod and the new, 3.14 introduced unveil mod.
Cinderswallow Urn | Legacy variants | The version dropped during version 3.5.0 ( Betrayal league ) has a lower required character level to equip (40). The new versions require character level 48, as the level of the crafted/unveil mods are level 60 (60 times 0.8 = 48).
Cinderswallow Urn | Legacy variants | Players can create a quasi-legacy variant by obtaining a veiled legacy Cinderswallow Urn, and unveiling a currently obtainable mod on the flask.

Clear Mind | Description | Clear Mind is a unique Cobalt Jewel.
Clear Mind | Mechanics | Blood Magic : When the Blood Magic keystone is allocated, the spell damage bonus from Clear Mind is always applied because having no mana implies that no mana is reserved.
Clear Mind | Mechanics | Life reservation : If an aura is supported with Arrogance Support to reserve life, then it is not reserving mana and the spell damage bonus from Clear Mind applies as expected.
Clear Mind | Mechanics | Another way to use an aura and not reserve mana is to use the unique ring Essence Worm , which causes the socketed skill gem to not reserve mana.
Clear Mind | Item acquisition | Clear Mind is drop disabled.

Cloak of Flame | Description | Cloak of Flame is a unique Scholar's Robe.
Cloak of Flame | Modifiers | 40% of Physical Damage taken as Fire Damage [1] : Cloak of Flame has a rare ' Damage taken as ' property. This applies generically to both Physical Hits and Damage over Time , e.g. Bleed and Corrupted Blood. (Unlike Lightning Coil or the influenced mod on rare/magic body armour, which applies to damage from hits only) Functionally, it acts as a 30-36% Physical Damage Reduction (depending on the Maximum Fire Resistance from 75% to 90%). It is best used in combination with Dawnbreaker , which provides an additional 20% damage shift.

Coiling Whisper | Description | Coiling Whisper is a unique Amethyst Ring.
Coiling Whisper | Mechanics | Power : Normal Monsters have 1 Power, Magic Monsters 2, Rare Monsters 10, Unique Monsters 20, and Players 5
Coiling Whisper | Mechanics | When 90% of your Hex's Duration Expires on an Enemy, Eat 1 Soul per Enemy Power : When a hex inflicted by the character on an enemy has elapsed 90% of its total duration , the character will gain a number of stacks of Soul Eater equal to that enemy's power. Hexproof does not prevent this interaction.
Coiling Whisper | Mechanics | The main issue with triggering soul gain is that the target may die or the hex may be overwritten before 90% of its duration elapses. Certain solutions, some of which can be combined, include:
Coiling Whisper | Mechanics | Despite counting as your hexes, hexes from the modifiers of Ylfeban's Trickery and Sentari's Answer do not grant Souls on curse expiry. Targets are Unaffected by your Hexes still applies, however.
Coiling Whisper | Mechanics | Note that this ring only disables the effect of hex curses. Marks still apply their full effect against affected enemies.

Cold Iron Point | Trivia | "Cold iron" is a poetic term for a bladed melee weapon made of iron, similar to the modern usage of "cold steel". It is also a reference to how metals at room temperature feel cold to the touch. [1] [2]
Cold Steel | Infobox | Radius: Large. Increases and Reductions to Physical Damage in Radius are Transformed to apply to Cold Damage Increases and Reductions to Cold Damage in Radius are Transformed to apply to Physical Damage. Winter provides us with her own weapons, hanging from the eaves of our homes. Place into an allocated Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Right click to remove from the Socket. Acquisition Drop disabled. Vendor Offer 4x Alchemy Shard
Cold Steel | Description | Cold Steel is a unique Viridian Jewel.
Cold Steel | Mechanics | Cold Steel will only transform nodes that explicitly state Cold Damage or Physical Damage. It will not apply to Elemental damage, nor Attack Damage, Melee Damage, etc., unless they also use the words Cold or Physical. All other restrictions on damage and weapon types will remain. A few examples are:
Cold Steel | Strategy | Global Physical Damage is hard to come by on the skill tree, and Physical Spell Damage does not exist at all. The Jewel can therefore be used to turn Cold Damage nodes into Physical damage nodes for Ethereal Knives , Shockwave Totem and Bear Trap.
Cold Steel | Strategy | If the player is dealing a lot of additive cold damage with any kind of weapon, the Jewel will allow them to convert physical nodes in the southern half of the tree to apply to that cold damage instead. However, as these bonuses will no longer apply to physical output, the only unique weapons with enough added cold damage to justify this strategy (i.e. achieve overall increased damage) is Dyadus when held in the off hand or Debeon's Dirge. As there is no noteworthy main hand weapon with similar amounts of additive cold damage, and the only skills that will not alternate between hands when dual wielding with an axe in the off hand are Heavy Strike and Infernal Blow (each when wielding a dagger or claw in the main hand), this is an unlikely scenario.
Cold Steel | Strategy | Skills with Damage conversion to Cold Damage, such as Frost Blades or Ice Crash , will usually not profit from turning increased Physical Damage nodes to increased Cold Damage nodes, as Physical Damage nodes will scale all of the damage, whereas Cold Damage nodes will only scale the cold portion of damage. See Damage Conversion for more information.
Cold Steel | Item acquisition | Cold Steel is drop disabled.

Corrupted Energy | Description | Corrupted Energy is a unique Cobalt Jewel.
Corrupted Energy | Mechanics | With 5 corrupted items equipped, (not counting corrupted jewels or gems): 50% of chaos damage does not bypass energy shield and 50% of physical damage bypasses energy shield. If the character has insufficient energy shield, then the remainder of the non-bypass damage will still be taken from life.
Corrupted Energy | Mechanics | Though the description of damage bypassing is similar to Shavronne's Wrappings or Solaris Lorica , Corrupted Energy functions differently. Corrupted Energy requires the character to be hit for the effect to take place. For this reason, it has no effect with damage over time. [ citation needed ]
Corrupted Energy | Mechanics | For example, if the character is bleeding (a damage over time), energy shield will absorb the physical damage over time caused by bleed before it is applied on life.
Corrupted Energy | Mechanics | If the character is taking chaos damage over time (eg. poisoned , positioned on desecrated ground or caustic ground ), the damage will be applied directly on life.
Corrupted Energy | Item acquisition | Corrupted Energy is drop disabled.

Cortex | Description | Cortex is a unique Synthesised Map. The base map is T11 but the unique map has a fixed area level (83).
Cortex | Description | Cortex is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map. However, this map can be witnessed by the Maven as part of the prerequisites of Maven's Invitation: The Feared.
Cortex | Description | Despite not being part of the Atlas, this map can still be affected by atlas passive skill that affects Synthesised Map. Note that atlas passive skill cannot affect maps from Kirac's atlas mission.
Cortex | Modifiers | The map has two pools of map modifiers: "synthesis local" pool (mod domain = 18) or map modifiers, and "synthesis global " pool (mod domain = 19) or reward modifiers.
Cortex | Layout | The area was known as Fractured Laboratory during the initial release ( Synthesis league ), [1] [2] but known as Cortex in the Memory Nexus Map UI. [3] When this area is re-introduced as unique map in version 3.8, it is known as Cortex only.
Cortex | Layout | The map uses the same tileset as Laboratory Map , Relic Chambers Map and The Reliquary.

Cospri's Malice | Description | Cospri's Malice is a unique Jewelled Foil.
Cospri's Malice | Mechanics | When an attack with a melee skill made with Cospri's Malice hits an enemy and deals a critical strike , socketed cold spells will be cast, similar to a trigger gem. The cooldown of this triggering effect is 250ms.
Cospri's Malice | Mechanics | Multiple socketed cold spells will trigger sequentially, starting with the socket on the top left. The order of the triggered spells is preserved even if a previous spell is already off cooldown. Multiple copies of the same spell share a cooldown. NOTE! This applies on multiple copies of the same spell when dual-wielding, even if they are in different Cospri's.
Cospri's Malice | Mechanics | Dual wielding: While dual wielding , socketed cold spells will only cast when a melee critical strike is made with the Cospri's they are socketed in.
Cospri's Malice | Item acquisition | Cospri's Malice can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Coward's Chains | Description | Coward's Chains is a unique Chain Belt. [1]
Coward's Chains | Mechanics | You count as on Full Life while you are Cursed with Vulnerability : This modifier will cause conditional effects that require full life to always be active. This is not equal to always having your life be at 100%; if you have any amount of life below maximum, you will be considered to be "on full life" and "not on full life" at the same time. Similarly, you can be on low life and full life at the same time if your actual life is below the low life threshold.
Coward's Chains | Item acquisition | Coward's Chains has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Coward's Chains can only be obtained from a chest inside Hybridisation Chamber , a tier 3 incursion room.

Cowards' Wail | Mechanics | Spectral Totems continue to use skills as they normally would. They cannot be hit , targeted at, or damaged. Enemies and allies will pass through them. They always have maximum 1 Life and cannot be taunted. They do not count as summoned totems, but other modifiers that apply to totems will apply to spectral totems. Maximum totem limit is independent from maximum spectral totem limit.

Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Description | Cowl of the Ceraunophile is a unique Solaris Circlet.
Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Mechanics | Cowl of the Ceraunophile can be anointed by Sister Cassia or in the Blight Stash Tab.
Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Mechanics | Can have a second Enchantment Modifier : This modifier allows up to two anointment to be applied. Copies in permanent leagues from before version 3.23.0 can have a combination of two anointments, two labyrinth enchantments , or one of each. Adding a new enchantment replaces one of the existing enchantments at random. Both enchantments are retained if the item is bricked into a rare Solaris Circlet , allowing the use of a Tainted Mythic Orb to have a chance to become a double-enchanted Wilma's Requital , Flamesight , Galesight , or Thundersight.
Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Mechanics | (#)% reduced (element) Resistance : This modifier is calculated differently than this one: -(#)% to (element) resistance
Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Mechanics | Lightning Resistance is 75% - This modifier over-rides all other flat and increased/reduced lightning resistance modifiers, ignoring them completely.
Cowl of the Ceraunophile | Interactions | Damage taken as can allow characters to skip the downside of needing to gear for additional resistances.

Crest of Perandus | Legacy variants | Crest of Perandus has legacy variants.
Crest of Perandus | Build of the Week | Crest of Perandus was featured in Season 2 Episode 3 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.
Crest of Perandus | Historical trivia | Before Patch 1.3.0, having 40% chance to block (30% from basetype), Crest of Perandus had the highest block chance of any shield. Even after the basetype block chance nerf, with 36%, the legacy Crest of Perandus held the title of "the highest block chance shield" until version 3.0.
Crest of Perandus | Historical trivia | After patch 3.0, Crest of Perandus has been dethroned by Unfailing Unfailing Prefix (31-36)% increased Armour +3% Chance to Block Shagreen Tower Shield of the Essence of the Essence Suffix +(7-8)% Chance to Block , which has 37% block chance. That shield was dethroned again circa version 3.16.0.

Crown of Eyes | Description | Crown of Eyes is a unique Hubris Circlet.
Crown of Eyes | Mechanics | Only increases and reductions to Spell Damage are applied to attacks. More and less modifiers do not apply to attacks.
Crown of Eyes | Interactions | Kinetic Bolt , the Wand Mastery , and other sources of Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attacks at XXX% of their value do not stack with each other. Only the highest value is applied.
Crown of Eyes | Interactions | Iron Will : allows Strength's damage bonus to apply to spell damage, which Crown of Eyes can reapply to attack damage with greater effect. This does not double-dip; modifiers can never apply twice to the same damage. [1] Every 5 points of Strength will grant a total of 1.5% increased Attack Damage globally. This interaction is effective for builds that want to apply Strength's damage bonus to attack damage that isn't melee or (converted from) physical, such as Molten Strike with added damage from Brutus' Lead Sprinkler or Replica Alberon's Warpath.
Crown of Eyes | Interactions | The Iron Fortress : improves Strength's damage bonus. Combined with Iron Will and Crown of Eyes, the bonus can be raised to 4.5% increased damage per 10 Strength.
Crown of Eyes | Interactions | Iron Grip : because the combination of Iron Will and Crown of Eyes already applies Strength's damage bonus to all attack damage, Iron Grip has no additional effect.

Darkness Enthroned | Modifiers | The (50-100)% increased Effect of Socketed Jewels increases the values of each of the two socketed jewels and rounds the increased values down.
Darkness Enthroned | Modifiers | This modifier does not increase the cap of the stats granted from the unique abyss jewels. For example, socketing an Amanamu's Gaze will increase the amount of DoT multiplier granted per Ghastly Eye Jewel, but it still caps out at +30% DoT multi.

Darkscorn | Description | Darkscorn is a unique Assassin Bow.
Darkscorn | Modifiers | Darkscorn converts a percentage of physical damage to chaos damage.
Darkscorn | Modifiers | It also causes a percentage of physical damage from enemies to be taken as chaos damage. This can become an issue for characters with poor Chaos resistance, but can also be a defensive benefit for those with high resistance, or who take the Chaos Inoculation keystone , the latter simply resulting in those characters taking 25% less Physical damage.
Darkscorn | Modifiers | The modifier "20% chance for Poisons inflicted with this weapon to deal 300% more damage" ends up being the same as a "60% chance for Poisons inflicted with this Weapon to deal 100% more Damage" modifier in terms of average damage. Or put another way, on average, poison with this weapon deals 60% more damage.
Darkscorn | Item acquisition | Darkscorn can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Darkscorn belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Darkscorn | Legacy variants | Darkscorn has legacy variants.

Dawnbreaker | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Dawnbreaker | Trivia | Dawnbreaker has the highest block chance among all shield in-game (48%).
Dawnbreaker | Trivia | In temporary leagues, Vaal Buckler or Spiny Round Shield plus of the Citadel of the Citadel Suffix +(14-15)% Chance to Block plus synthesis implicit modifier (4-5)% Chance to Block Attack Damage plus corruption implicit +(4-5)% Chance to Block may have the highest theoretical block chance from a rare shield (27%+15%+5%+5%=52%), but it has a very low chance to successfully craft it.
Dawnbreaker | Trivia | In permanent leagues, using Armour Recombinators can transfer the legacy mod Unfailing Unfailing Prefix (31-36)% increased Armour +3% Chance to Block from a donor item, so that +3% to aforementioned chance, but it still needed to be successfully corrupt and synthesis the item in order to have more than 48%+5% chance to block, given Dawnbreaker itself can be corrupted.
Dawnbreaker | Trivia | However, Dawnbreaker has a downside mod -1% Chance to Block Attack Damage for every 200 Fire Damage taken from Hits Recently while the rare shield and T1 block suffix combo has no conditional mod to lower the block chance.
Dawnbreaker | Trivia | This calculation also excludes any conditional mod that increase the block chance from a rare shield, or any scourge modifier that is not available to craft on item anymore.

Death and Taxes | Description | Death and Taxes is a unique Necropolis Map.
Death and Taxes | Description | Death and Taxes is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Death and Taxes | Layout | Map type: Indoor
Death and Taxes | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Death and Taxes | Mechanics | Death and Taxes is reminiscent of a raid boss in classic MMORPGs. [1] The map is initially empty. Players must run past three orange portals, which are surrounded by elemental effects similar to those of certain Shrines, to the end of the map where a final fourth orange portal can be found. Upon reaching the end, the boss is activated and blue portals throughout the map will spawn hostile animated weapons.
Death and Taxes | Mechanics | The map has no native monsters other than the boss, which has 4 forms, though portals will spawn animated weapons after the boss has been engaged. Once each form is killed, the boss will enter an "in-between stage", becoming immune to damage until it is lured to the next orange portal. Players need to kill every form to complete the map. Each form drops items on death, with the final form dropping a large amount of currency items.
Death and Taxes | Animated weapons | Once the first portal is activated, these are spawned continuously until the last boss's form is dead. Caveat : animated weapons can't be hit with Spark. Spark is ground projectile, and animated weapons are flying.
Death and Taxes | Avatar of Ash | Once the first portal is activated, Avatar of Ash will spawn, which uses Flameblast , Summon Raging Spirit and Fireball supported by Lesser Multiple Projectiles. Flameblast is by far the most dangerous of these, especially since all of the action tends to make the expanding circle difficult to see until it is too late, especially in the presence of stun and desync. You need to pre-emptively move every few seconds in order not to get hit.
Death and Taxes | Avatar of Winter | Upon reaching the next portal, the boss will take form of Avatar of Winter, which uses a huge AoE Ice Nova , long range Glacial Cascade and spawns ice versions of Firestorm.
Death and Taxes | Avatar of Silence | Upon reaching the next portal, the boss takes form of Avatar of Silence, which casts Shock Nova , Ball Lightning , and Storm Call. As with Avatar of Ash's Flameblasts, it is unwise to stand still; while one Storm Call hit probably won't kill you, an entire salvo may do so.
Death and Taxes | Avatar of Apocalypse | Upon entering the final portal, Avatar of Apocalypse spawns, which uses all previous spells combined. This is the boss' final form; Avatar of Apocalypse will not respawn once killed.
Death and Taxes | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Death and Taxes | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Death and Taxes | Item acquisition | Death and Taxes can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Death and Taxes | Recipes | Death and Taxes can be created from the following recipes:
Death and Taxes | Legacy variants | Death and Taxes has legacy variants.
Death and Taxes | Design attribution | Death and Taxes was created by an anonymous supporter.
Death Rush | Infobox | Requires Level 30. +(17-23)% to Chaos Resistance. Recover (3-5)% of Life on Kill Gain Adrenaline for (1-3) seconds on Kill. To truly appreciate life you must be there when it ends. Acquisition Drop level: 46 League-specific • Onslaught. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard
Death Rush | Description | Death Rush is a unique Amethyst Ring.
Death Rush | Mechanics | Adrenaline cannot be gained or refreshed while you already have Adrenaline.
Death Rush | Mechanics | The gained Adrenaline duration stacks additively with other similar stats; for example, equipping 2 copies of Death Rush will grant Adrenaline for 2-6 seconds on kill instead.
Death Rush | Item acquisition | Death Rush can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Death Rush belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Death Rush | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Death Rush | Legacy variants | Death Rush has legacy variants.
Death Rush | Alternate artwork | Foil Death Rush can also be obtained from Voidborn Reliquary ( Amethyst , Ruby and Sunset colors, according to trade site result), as well as Valdo's Map such as "Intense Gallery" and "Grim View" (both grant Celestial Amethyst color)

Death's Oath | Description | Death's Oath is a unique Astral Plate.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | Grants Death Aura.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | The Death Aura skill triggered by Death's Oath is affected by any relevant support gem that is also socketed in Death's Oath. It does not require the socket to be linked.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | Since the skill is triggered by the item rather than a skill gem , it is not affected by Empower Support , Enlighten Support , or Enhance Support. As the aura is activated by trigger, it cannot be supported by Blessing skills.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | As a red (strength) gem socket is not the desired colour for a Death Aura build, and because Death's Oath has a high strength requirement (which yield more red gem socket when using Chromatic Orb ), it is recommended to use Crafting Bench 's "at least one/two/three X color craft" (e.g. Green / Blue) as well as add-socket / remove-socket method to recolour the socket of this item.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | Self damage from the debuff granted by Death's Oath does not stack.
Death's Oath | Mechanics | Gore Footprints ItemBloodFootstepsUniqueBodyStr3 Intrinsic Gore Footprints : Death's Oath has the Gore Footprints Effect applied to it. This is purely cosmetic.
Death's Oath | Item acquisition | Death's Oath can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Death's Oath | Recipes | Death's Oath can be created from the following recipes:
Death's Oath | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Death's Oath | Legacy variants | Death's Oath has legacy variants.
Death's Oath | Legacy variants | The legacy versions do not grant Death Aura skill, but instead grant a debuff that have AOE: "Death's Oath" (internal id: "unique_deaths_oath_aura"). It has a radius of 3 metres (30 units). [b] It also uses the same visual icon as Chaos Degeneration Aura , and have the status icon text: "You are taking Chaos Damage over time".
Death's Oath | Design attribution | Death's Oath was created by supporter isildria. [ citation needed ]

The item was used to be created by completing fated unique prophecy The Bowstring's Music , and legacy modifiers and basetypes are retained when upgrading unique to their fated counterparts. So because Death's Harp has legacy variants, Death's Opus effectively does as well.
Death's Opus | Legacy variants | Due to version 3.19.0 changes to Death's Harp , Death's Opus can be Divined to have Bow Attacks fire 3 additional Arrows (displayed as Bow Attacks fire 2 additional Arrows and Bow Attacks fire an additional Arrow in advanced modifier descriptions view when pressing PC default keybind alt ). However, by doing so, the other legacy mods' (physical damage and crit multi) values from pre-3.17 variants will worsen.

Defiance of Destiny | Description | Defiance of Destiny is a unique Paua Amulet.
Defiance of Destiny | Mechanics | Gain #% of Missing Unreserved Life before being hit by an Enemy : This modifier instantly recovers a percentage of missing life immediately after the wearer fails to evade or dodge a hit , before that hit deals damage.
Defiance of Destiny | Mechanics | This effect will prevent dying to hits that deal less damage than the numerical roll's percentage of (maximum unreserved life - 1). Its timing also means the recovery occurs before on-hit effects, such as culling strike ; in this case, the life threshold for cull can be directly subtracted from the maximum hit a Defiance of Destiny can out-heal. Recovering before damage calculation may prevent benefiting from defensive modifiers with a Low Life condition, such as Lori's Lantern.
Defiance of Destiny | Item acquisition | Defiance of Destiny can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Defiance of Destiny | Legacy variants | Defiance of Destiny has a legacy variant.

Demigod unique | Description | A Demigod unique , or simply Demigod , refers to a series of race -exclusive event prizes.
Demigod unique | Mechanics | Demigod uniques are exclusive items obtained as rewards to top players during race events , akin to an itemized trophy. They can be traded to and from other players, but cannot be obtained through any other methods.
Demigod unique | Mechanics | All Demigod items can be converted into a microtransaction through the /convertracereward command regardless of whether they are original or alternate art; this process is permanent and will destroy the item. The microtransaction will retain any special visual effects from the original item, though it will not grant any stats.

Dissolution of the Flesh | Infobox | Limited to: 1. Removes all Energy Shield Life that would be lost by taking Damage is instead Reserved until you take no Damage to Life for 2 seconds (20-30)% more Maximum Life. Dissolution of the Flesh | Description | Dissolution of the Flesh is a unique Crimson Jewel.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Mechanics | This item sets your maximum energy shield to zero.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Mechanics | A character that reserves 100% or greater of their life will die. After Life becomes unreserved, your current life does not replenish to full automatically; however, the missing life can still be reserved on top of. If a player is reserving life through skills (e.g. auras supported by Arrogance Support ), the player will not die upon reaching 100% reservation; reserved skills will be turned off automatically to free up life to reserve through Dissolution of the Flesh's modifier effect instead. [1] Reservation effects from Midnight Bargain and Blood Sacrament cannot be cancelled and so will cause the player to die early. Reservation from Mines is cancellable and so will not cause the player to die earlier than usual, however the mines will not be removed when the reservation is cancelled.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Mechanics | Life that would be lost by taking Damage is instead Reserved until you take no damage to Life for 2 seconds is unaffected by any currently existing modifiers to Reservation Efficiency, as it is not a skill [2]. Modifiers to Skill Effect Duration or Temporal Chains do not affect the 2 second recovery window for the same reason. The reservation is unaffected by Temporal Rift 's rewind effect. Modifiers to Life Recovery Rate will have no effect on the reservation. It is not a debuff and the 2 second interval cannot be modified.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | Damage from hits and damage over time will reserve life, but effects that cause you to spend, lose, or sacrifice life (such as Rite of Ruin , Dark Pact or Rathpith Globe ) will not. Effects that remove life this way allows a character to maintain Low Life , permitting Pain Attunement and other low life-related modifiers to function. Items and skills that hit the player (e.g. Forbidden Rite ) will continually reserve life, preventing the player from being able to unreserve their life.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | The player is still considered as taking damage from hits while life is being reserved instead, for the purposes of Aspect of the Crab.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | Stats which track the amount of damage taken such as Cast when Damage Taken Support or Dawnbreaker will continue to function normally because Dissolution of the Flesh does not interact with damage taken, but instead changes life loss from damage taken to become life reservation instead.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | Solutions for damage over time is critical when using Dissolution of the Flesh as they will prevent the timer to reset the reservation from starting, and in particular ailments , specifically Bleeding , Poison , and Ignite.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | It is generally advised to have several sources of damage avoidance (e.g. block , spell block, Elusive , evasion) or damage mitigation (e.g. Armour , Max Resistances, spell suppression) to mitigate the rate at which life is reserved and to increase the window between taking damage from consecutive hits. Ward also synergizes well with this playstyle, especially if using Olroth's Resolve.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | Sacrifice of Blood from Chaos Bloodline 's Corruption's Embrace will mitigate damage before reserving life.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Taking damage | Dissolution of the Flesh has synergies with Eternal Youth and Petrified Blood , providing powerful build archetypes to an otherwise awkward mechanic.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Eternal Youth | Eternal Youth allows Life to recharge instead of Energy Shield, and as with Energy Shield recharge, any damage taken which causes life loss will cause it to be interrupted. A character with Dissolution of the Flesh does not lose life from taking damage directly but reserves life equal to damage taken instead. However, reserving life can still make you lose life if your maximum unreserved life drops below your current life value. When this happens, you are losing life from taking damage, which is the condition that inhibits Recharge. [3]
Dissolution of the Flesh | Eternal Youth | There are two workarounds for this issue:
Dissolution of the Flesh | Eternal Youth | Life Recharge from Eternal Youth recovers a significant amount of Life per second (33.3% by default, 20% with Wicked Ward). This value can be modified by modifiers to Energy Shield Recharge Rate (specifically Blightwell ) and Life Recovery Rate. This Recharge can either allows sustain of high Life Costs or Sacrificed Life, making it synergistic with Dark Pact , Lifetap Support , Sacrifice Support , and Rathpith Globe , or serves a defensive purpose through the sustain of Petrified Blood.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Petrified Blood | Petrified Blood provides a buff that says When taking Damage from Hits, 40% of Life loss below half Life is Prevented, then (100-81)% of Life loss prevented this way is lost over 4 seconds. As the player will always be considered below half life due to PB, this effect will apply to the entirety of incoming hit damage. Effectively, the player will only take 60% of the hit's damage immediately as a reservation, then a percentage of the other 40% is spread out as life loss (not damage) over 4 seconds. As long as the player has no other Life reservations, the Life reservation from Dissolution of the Flesh starts from the player's maximum Life, even when using Petrified Blood , effectively increasing the total effective health pool of the character by spreading the damage over one and two thirds health bars.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Petrified Blood | Additionally, having enough sources of damage taken recouped as Life to fully counteract the life loss from the Petrified Blood effect will prevent the life loss from being able to kill the player other than reserving 100% of the player's Life, even when the actual Life value is at 1. For example, at 20/20, Petrified Blood will cause the player to take 76% of the 40% of the hit as life loss; a player would theoretically need at least 30.4% Life recoup in this scenario to counteract the loss. Modifiers to life recovery rate will affect the needed Recoup value.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Petrified Blood | Strength of Blood from a Lethal Pride can also provide a significant boost to physical damage reduction, synergizing well with Vaal Pact from the Keystone or Atziri's Acuity. As long as the player does not reserve over half of their life, Petrified Blood enables over-leech, allowing consistent uptime of the damage reduction. Eternal Youth may be skipped if attempting this method, as it cuts the maximum total recovery from leech (and therefore the damage reduction) by half.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Item acquisition | Dissolution of the Flesh has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
Dissolution of the Flesh | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:

Doedre's Damning | Legacy variants | Since the items dropped before version 3.19.0 have mod values within the current ranges. It is not possible to tell it is an item dropped before or after that patch. The chance of dropping an item that have the similar mod values before that patch is: 1/16 * 1/16 * 6/16 = 3/2048 = 0.146%. Due to sheer amount of items generated in the new leagues, such chance is not that low. Also, take into account that the item has not changed since the last server data wipe in version 0.10.0 , there is 10 years' worth of loot in the permanent leagues. Therefore, beware of scammers that offer this item and claim it is a legacy item.

Doedre's Skin | Description | Doedre's Skin is a unique Widowsilk Robe.
Doedre's Skin | Description | The function of this unique item is similar to the boss encounter of Doedre in Act 4 or Act 9.
Doedre's Skin | Mechanics | The body armour grants Summon Doedre's Effigy , as well as supports socketed hex curse skill gems (ie. excluding Bane and Hexblast ) with SupportCursePillarTriggerCurses (from the mod Socketed Hex Curse Skills are Triggered by Doedre's Effigy when Summoned and a hidden mod ).
Doedre's Skin | Mechanics | In addition, all socketed hex curse skill gems (cannot support any Mark curse skills) are supported by Blasphemy Support. All support stats are accounted for, including the cost and reservation multiplier.
Doedre's Skin | Mechanics | Even though curses socketed into the body armour do not have to be linked to be cast by the Effigy, there is a benefit to linking sockets. Both Enhance Support and Empower Support will work on all curses that are linked together, increasing their effects accordingly. The same applies to area of effect modifiers from supports such as Increased Area of Effect Support or its Awakened variant if one wishes to vastly increase the area of effect of curses.
Doedre's Skin | Mechanics | Additionally, all hex area of effect modifiers will work on the AoE of the Effigy. This includes all passive tree modifiers and the ones from items, like the ones found on Windscream , Star of Wraeclast , or Dying Breath.
Doedre's Skin | Mechanics | The Effigy's survivability cannot be scaled by your stats, as the construct is not a minion or totem. The Effigy can be affected by your auras, for example standing next to it and activating Discipline will grant it additional energy shield.
Doedre's Skin | Item acquisition | Doedre's Skin can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Doedre's Skin | Recipes | Doedre's Skin can be created from the following recipes:
Doedre's Skin | Legacy variants | Doedre's Skin has legacy variants.
Doedre's Skin | Legacy variants | The legacy versions actually uses a different trigger support: SupportCursePillarTriggerCursesLessCurseEffect [1] as legacy items does not have the global 20% less Effect of your Curses.
Doedre's Skin | Legacy variants | However, the pre-3.5 variant also has the legacy mod (33-25)% reduced Effect of your Curses which is global and multiplicative with the less effect of curse from 20% less Effect of Curses from Socketed Hex Skills (which actually granted from the support as the mod was a text-only mod [2] ). A Divine Orb can remove this mod. However, a post-3.5 pre 3.16 legacy Doedre's Skin, may still worth less than a Divine Orb in the permanent trade leagues, so that buying one may cheaper than update the mod of pre-3.5 double legacy variant.
Doedre's Skin | Legacy variants | A Divine Orb cannot change 20% less Effect of Curses from Socketed Hex Skills to 20% less Effect of your Curses.

Doon Cuebiyari | Description | Doon Cuebiyari is a unique Vaal Sceptre.
Doon Cuebiyari | Modifiers | Gems socketed in it are supported by a level 30 Iron Will Support , which grants 58% increased spell damage as well as 1% increased spell damage per 8 strength. All support stats are accounted for, including the cost and reservation multiplier.
Doon Cuebiyari | Modifiers | When wearing a Doon Cuebiyari in your main hand, any spell that is also supported by Iron Will (e.g. because it is socketed into the sceptre itself) will gain a total of 13% increased damage per 40 strength or 0.325% increased damage per 1 strength (in addition to the direct damage modifier by a leveled iron will gem).
Doon Cuebiyari | Modifiers | 1% increased Damage per 8 Strength when in Main Hand can be improved by using a Tempering Orb to obtain any enchantment with 25% reduced Explicit Damage Modifier magnitudes ; as only the "per 8 Strength" can be scaled numerically, you will gain 1% increased Damage per 6 Strength when in Main Hand instead.
Doon Cuebiyari | Item acquisition | Doon Cuebiyari can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
