Doryani's Machinarium | Description | Doryani's Machinarium is a unique Maze Map.
Doryani's Machinarium | Description | Doryani's Machinarium is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map.
Doryani's Machinarium | Layout | When you enter Doryani's Machinarium, there are three paths you can travel and a staircase that leads to an empty boss room. Each path has a payload you'll need to transfer and is connected to one of the boss's arms. When you reach the end of each path, you are presented with three buttons.
Doryani's Machinarium | Layout | Each button augments that arm with an element: fire, cold or lightning. After you've completed each path and you have augmented each arm, you can then fight the boss you have created. The reward you'll receive is based on the options you have chosen. [1]
Doryani's Machinarium | Boss | The Apex Assembly will always drop Doryani's Delusion. Your choice for the buff to minions determines the base of the item (fire will yield the armour base, cold will yield the evasion base and lightning will yield the energy shield base). Your choice for the buff to claw determines the element damage to attacks/spells. Your choice for the buff to Bludgeon determines the element of the purity.
Doryani's Machinarium | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Doryani's Machinarium | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Doryani's Machinarium | Item acquisition | Doryani's Machinarium has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Can be found as a Red tier Kirac Mission when using a Comprehensive Scouting Report.
Doryani's Machinarium | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Doryani's Machinarium | Legacy variants | Doryani's Machinarium has legacy variants.

Dreadbeak | Description | Dreadbeak is a unique Rusted Sword.
Dreadbeak | Mechanics | 100% increased Damage when on Low Life – While on low life , Dreadbeak grants 100% increased damage. This is a global damage modifier, which applies to all damage inflicted by the character's skills , including damage from ailments , debuffs , spells , traps , mines and totems.
Dreadbeak | Item acquisition | Dreadbeak is drop disabled.
Dreadbeak | Legacy variants | Dreadbeak has legacy variants.
Dreadbeak | Legacy variants | Moreover, player could use a legacy Redbeak to create a quasi-legacy Dreadbeak, as the legacy implicit 18% increased Global Accuracy Rating would carry over to the fated item, and the item retained the current explicit roll. However, since prophecy system is removed from the game. It could not be created anymore. It can also be "created" by intentionally using Divine Orb on true pre-2.6 Dreadbeak. However, since this will decrease the physical damage mod value, so that it has no point to do so.

Duskblight | Legacy variants | Legacy modifiers and base items are retained when upgrading uniques to their fated counterparts. So because Dusktoe has legacy variants, Duskblight effectively does as well.
Duskblight | Legacy variants | It was not stated in the patch notes, but the legacy mod Socketed Gems are Supported by Level 15 Added Chaos Damage can be divined to level 25.
Duskblight | Legacy variants | Before the removal of the prophecy mechanic in version 3.17.0 , the player could use a legacy Dusktoe (either Leatherscale Boots or Ironscale Boots variants) as an ingredient to obtain a hybrid fated version that has the current post-3.11 mods Trigger Level 1 Stalking Pustule on Kill and Adds (30-40) to (50-60) Chaos Damage to Spells and Attacks during any Flask Effect , as well as the legacy mod +(10-20) to maximum Mana. This could only be done in permanent leagues by acquiring a legacy Dusktoe. By the same method, up to the version 3.11 patch, players can also create a Duskblight on Leatherscale Boots base and have a combination of pre-3.11 legacy mods.

Dyadian Dawn | Description | Dyadian Dawn is a unique Heavy Belt.
Dyadian Dawn | Mechanics | Ignites you inflict with Attacks deal Damage 35% faster – Enemies ignited by an attack burn 35% faster with Dyadian Dawn equipped. This means ignites deal 35% more fire damage per second and have ~26% less duration. Expressed arithmetically, fire damage over time from ignites is multiplied by ( 100 + 35 ) / 100 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(100+35)/100} , while ignite duration is multiplied by 100 / ( 100 + 35 ) {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}100/(100+35)}. The total fire damage inflicted by one instance of ignite remains the same after these adjustments, but the duration is shortened. [1]
Dyadian Dawn | Mechanics | 1% of Attack Damage Leeched as Life against Chilled Enemies : Unlike other similar conditional leech modifiers, a one-shot hit that applies chill will cause the conditional leech to apply. [2] [3]
Dyadian Dawn | Item acquisition | Dyadian Dawn can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Dyadian Dawn | Recipes | Dyadian Dawn can be created from the following recipes:

Dyadian Dawn | Legacy variants | Dyadian Dawn has a legacy variant.

Dyadus | Description | Dyadus is a unique Infernal Axe.
Dyadus | Modifiers | Chill Enemies for 1 second on Hit with this Weapon when in Off Hand – When Dyadus is equipped in the off hand, hits with it will always chill the target for 1 second, even if no cold damage is inflicted by the hit. This guaranteed chill effect reduces the target's action speed by 10%.
Dyadus | Modifiers | Adds (255-285) to (300-330) Cold Damage in Off Hand – Adds (255-285) to (300-330) Fire Damage in Main Hand These modifiers are a very rare case of ones that are Local, despite being conditional. Note however that despite the base values making it look like both values apply; only one of them will be counted, depending on which hand Dyadus is in. This is true even for things such as Spellblade Support ; ie, Spellblade will only add either the cold damage or the fire damage to spells with one Dyadus equipped.

Emberwake | Description | Emberwake is a unique Ruby Ring.
Emberwake | Modifiers | You can inflict an additional Ignite on each Enemy - by default, multiple ignites can be applied to an enemy, however, only the strongest one actually deals damage. Emberwake allows for two ignites to deal damage simultaneously, or three with two Emberwakes.
Emberwake | Modifiers | 40% less Burning Damage is multiplicative with each other when two Emberwakes are equipped. [1]
Emberwake | Legacy Mod | Ignited Enemies Burn (50-65)% slower - Ignited enemies burn slower with legacy Emberwake equipped. This means ignites have more duration and deal less fire damage per second.
Emberwake | Legacy Mod | Expressed arithmetically, ignite duration is multiplied by ( 100 + x ) / 100 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(100+x)/100} , while fire damage over time from ignites is multiplied by 100 / ( 100 + x ) {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}100/(100+x)} , where x varies from 50 to 65 depending on the roll (or 80 for double legacy Emberwake ). The total fire damage inflicted by one instance of ignite remains the same after these adjustments, but the duration is extended. [2]
Emberwake | Legacy Mod | Under ideal conditions two active ignites burning 50% slower can be interpreted as 33% more damage per second by the formula: 2 ∗ 100 / ( 100 + 50 ) = 4 / 3 = 1.33 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}2*100/(100+50)=4/3=1.33}
Emberwake | Legacy Mod | Under worst conditions one active ignite burning 65% slower can be interpreted as 40% less damage per second by the formula: 1 ∗ 100 / ( 100 + 65 ) = 0.60 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}1*100/(100+65)=0.60}

Emperor's Vigilance | Description | Emperor's Vigilance is a unique Steel Kite Shield.
Emperor's Vigilance | Modifiers | The Glancing Blows modifier has the same effect as the keystone, Glancing Blows. Taking the keystone in addition to equipping this item will impart no additional benefit.
Emperor's Vigilance | Modifiers | Its extremely high increased Armour and Energy Shield values for its level make it a strong Unique for Shield Skills like Shield Crush , Shield Charge , or Spectral Shield Throw , on par with crafted high-end rare shields. It scales extremely well with modifiers that can further improve its modifiers, such as the Crucible Passive Skill Tree and added maximum quality from its Foulborn version, allowing it to surpass crafted shields in raw defence values.
Emperor's Vigilance | Modifiers | The modifier, Damage taken from Blocked Hits cannot bypass Energy Shield , overrides the default behaviour of chaos damage bypassing energy shield. Blocked chaos damage from hits will not bypass a character's energy shield.
Emperor's Vigilance | Interactions | Stats that cause damage to bypass energy shield, such as the Divine Flesh keystone from a Glorious Vanity unique Timeless Jewel and #% of Non-Chaos Damage taken bypasses Energy Shield from The Burden of Truth or Corrupted Soul take precedence over the modifier Damage taken from Blocked Hits cannot bypass Energy Shield. For instance, a character with the Divine Flesh keystone allocated and Emperor's Vigilance equipped will have all incoming damage bypass their energy shield, regardless of whether it was blocked or not, while a character which has Corrupted Soul or The Burden of Truth will take #% of the Blocked Non-Chaos Damage hit to their Life, while blocked chaos damage does not Bypass Energy shield. [1]
Emperor's Vigilance | Interactions | Damage taken from Unblocked hits always bypasses Energy Shield does override Chaos Damage taken does not bypass Energy Shield (e.g. Shavronne's Wrappings ).
Emperor's Vigilance | Interactions | Crucible Passive Skill Tree mods with +x to Armour/maximum Energy Shields , x% increased Armour, Evasion and Energy Shield , or x% increased Explicit Defence Modifier magnitudes are extremely powerful on this shield for shield attack builds, especially when combined with Seething Fury , as these modifiers increase the shield's base defence values which get multiplied by the explicit mod. With it, Emperor's Vigilance can reach Armour/ES values no other shields in the game can compete with. Note that Crucible has not been added to the core game, so items with Crucible mods can only be found in the permanent leagues as legacy content; crafting a new Emperor's Vigilance with Crucible mods requires an existing Steel Kite Shield base that has Crucible mods (crafted during Crucible league ) and then chancing it (can be guaranteed with Omen of Fortune ), so the supply is low.
Emperor's Vigilance | Item acquisition | Emperor's Vigilance can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Emperor's Vigilance belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Essence Worm | Infobox | Requires Level 38. Has 1 Socket. +2 to Level of Socketed Aura Gems Socketed Gems have no Reservation Your Blessing Skills are Disabled 80% reduced Reservation Efficiency of Skills. "This thing is not a pet. Vendor Offer 18x Alchemy Shard
Essence Worm | Description | Essence Worm is a unique Unset Ring.
Essence Worm | Mechanics | The skill gem (such as aura , banner , herald , stance , etc) socketed in this ring reserves no mana. If the skill gem has the Aura gem tag, it receives +2 to gem level.
Essence Worm | Mechanics | Essence Worm's 80% reduced Reservation Efficiency of Skills mod affects the reservation of other skills which reserve mana or life, such as aura, banner, herald, stance, aspect skills, Spellslinger etc. This mod is additive to other increased/reduced reservation efficiency modifiers from item mods and passive skills. This modifier is also independent from the other two modifiers of the ring. Regardless of whether there is a socketed gem or not in this ring, the reservation is increased once this ring is equipped.
Essence Worm | Mechanics | Note that under one Essence Worm:
Essence Worm | Mechanics | Unless the player has stacked #% increased (Mana or Life if applicable) Reservation Efficiency mods, equipping this ring effectively rules out using any other reservation skills other than the one socketed in this ring.
Essence Worm | Mechanics | The player can use two Essence Worms, which would total 160% reduced Reservation Efficiency, making it even more difficult to equip any other reservation skills.
Essence Worm | Mechanics | The mod 80% reduced Reservation Efficiency of Skills does not have mana mod tag, so that it is not affected by Fertile Catalyst 's quality, and thus cannot be used the increased mana reservation in niche build such as Relic of the Pact 's Blood Sacrament skill.
Essence Worm | Item acquisition | Essence Worm can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Essence Worm | Recipes | Essence Worm can be created from the following recipes:
Essence Worm | Legacy variants | Essence Worm has a legacy variant.
Essence Worm | Legacy variants | When pressing Alt in PC (bound to other button in controller mode), the legacy mod 80% reduced Reservation Efficiency of Skills [1] will show it has mana mod tag, which can be affected by Fertile Catalyst 's quality, making it 96% reduced Reservation Efficiency and in turn drastically increase mana/life reservation for a niche of builds that build around on scaling damage with reservation. By assuming there is no other #% reduced Reservation Efficiency , a value of 96% means the default reservation value is multiplied by 25 times (1/(1-0.96))
Essence Worm | Legacy variants | The official trade site's mod filter, #% increased Reservation Efficiency of Skills (explicit mod), now applies to the current and legacy items, so that it cannot be used as "NOT condition" in the query to filter out current variant. However, only the current item will show the default mod value is [-80], while the default mod value of legacy item will not be shown.
Essence Worm | Design attribution | Essence Worm was created by supporter retruber. [2] The supporter also designed The Penitent and Witchfire Brew.

Expedition's End | Mechanics | All Damage can Freeze : This mod allows non-cold damage to be able to contribute to freeze ; it does not grant any chance to freeze itself. This mod allows builds using Avatar of Fire or Original Sin to be able to freeze despite not being able to deal cold damage.
Expedition's End | Mechanics | Nearby Enemies are Chilled : This stat causes the player to chill enemies within a distance of 3 meters, with a base effect of 10%. As a chill from the player, its effect scales with ailment effect modifiers, it can apply chilled by you stats, counts as having chilled recently , cannot chill with Cannot Chill , and can be reflected by The Fulcrum. It is not a chilling area.

Eye of Malice | Description | Eye of Malice is a unique Callous Mask.
Eye of Malice | Mechanics | At first glance the mod Nearby Enemies have 50% increased Fire and Cold Resistances may sound unhelpful; however, it also multiplies negative resistances. [1]
Eye of Malice | Mechanics | For example, if a monster's Cold Resistance was lowered to -30% via sources such as Exposure , Frostbite , Void Beacon , and other methods, Eye of Malice's effect would cause that monster to have -45% instead. Note that this modifier affects the actual resistance (or tooltip resistance) of the monster; it does not scale on player side by how many -#% to monster resistances with any given method (i.e. the scaling is calculated on monster side). Conversely, a monster with 30% Cold Resistance after applying any player's aura/curse/other #% res effect, will have 45% Cold Resistance instead. Therefore, it is important to stack lots of sources of Resistance stripping to get the monster negative.
Eye of Malice | Mechanics | Note that Penetration applies after this step; the Penetration value itself will not increase. Read Receiving damage and Resistance penetration for details.
Eye of Malice | Mechanics | Alternatively, you can use this item with effects that invert enemy resistances like Rakiata's Dance and Wild Strike of Extremes , though it has some slight anti-synergy with its exposure effect. For this, you need to avoid getting effects that give reduced resistances or increased exposure effect.
Eye of Malice | Mechanics | The Nearby radius is 3 metre.
Eye of Malice | Item acquisition | Eye of Malice can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Eye of Malice | Recipes | Eye of Malice can be created from the following recipes:

Facebreaker | Design attribution | Brother Laz , author of the famous Median XL mod for Diablo 2: Lord of Destruction, played a role in the design of Facebreaker. [1] [2]

Facebreaker | Trivia | Although there is an in-game enemy called the Rhoa , there is none called "Storm Crow". The name "Storm Crow" may be a reference to the Magic the Gathering card of the same name.
Facebreaker | Trivia | The flavour text may also be a reference to the famous Mohamed Ali quote "Float like a butterfly sting like a bee".
Facebreaker | Trivia | During Closed Beta , Facebreaker was featured in Week #9 of the original "Build of the Week" series released by Grinding Gear Games.
Facebreaker | Trivia | During version 1.1.3b , Facebreaker was declared as an uncommon unique drop. [3] See Guide:Analysis of unique item tiers for the latest community analysis of unique items rarity tiers.

Fairgraves' Tricorne | Description | Fairgraves' Tricorne is a unique Tricorne. It is associated with Captain Fairgraves and, according to its flavour text, is the very hat that both his NPC model and the unique monster bearing his name wear.
Fairgraves' Tricorne | Mechanics | Unique monsters have a chance to drop a Fishing Rod while you have Fairgraves' Tricorne equipped.
Fairgraves' Tricorne | Mechanics | Cannot be Shocked : This modifier prevents you from being Shocked , including shocks from Shocked Ground and other non-hit source of shock, but does not remove shocks you already have on you.
Fairgraves' Tricorne | Mechanics | You can be touched by Tormented Spirits : This stat enables players to be Touched by a Tormented Spirit after moving near one, which grants two buffs typically exclusive to monsters. These buff do not stack and lasts indefinitely until you exit the area or remove the helmet. A wild Tormented Spirit cannot touch you if you are already touched (e.g. via Ghost of the Deep ). [1]
Fairgraves' Tricorne | Mechanics | Both buffs do stack with, and are independent from, the similar buffs from the Speaker of the Dead keystone.
Fairgraves' Tricorne | Tormented Spirit modifiers | Note: information is based on inferences from metadata and may not be 100% accurate.

(map, scarab or unique item) The Betrayer and Heretic Touched monsters also have a chance to drop a specific item (scarab or map depending on the spirit). The chance is supposedly 10%, following the metadata which may not be accurate. The scarabs dropped this way doesn't seem to be affected by modifiers on the atlas tree, as notable nodes such as Loved by the Sun doesn't prevent abyss scarabs to drop.

Fated End | Infobox | Requires Level 38. +(20-30) to maximum Mana. +(20-40) to Intelligence Curse Skills have (8-12)% increased Cast Speed Non- Aura Hexes gain 20% increased Effect per second Non-Aura Hexes expire upon reaching (180-220)% of base Effect. Vendor Offer 16x Alchemy Shard 3x Alteration Shard
Fated End | Description | Fated End is a unique Paua Ring.
Fated End | Item acquisition | Fated End has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
Fated End | Item acquisition | Fated End can be obtained from Curio Displays in the final reward rooms of any Grand Heist.
Fated End | Mechanics | Non-Aura Hexes expire upon reaching #% of base Effect : [a] This takes into account all modifiers to curse effect that apply to the curse, not just the increased effect from Non-Aura Hexes gain 20% increased Effect per second. Thus a curse with less effect or reduced effect will take longer to reach the required fraction of base effect to expire, while curses that have a high enough curse effect modifier from other sources will expire immediately.
Fated End | Mechanics | Removing Fated End after applying a hex will not change the behavior of the existing curse - it will continue to gain increased effect over time and will expire once it reaches maximum effect.
Fated unique item | Description | Originally part of the prophecy system, fated unique items were improved versions of other unique items. Various unique items could be upgraded to their fated forms by fulfilling the prophecy that involved slaying a specific unique monster while holding that item. Once the prophecy was fulfilled, the item would be given a new name and additional stats.
Fated unique item | Description | Fated unique items started to be phased out in version 3.17.0 and were fully removed in version 3.19.0.
Fated unique item | Original design | Fated unique items were originally part of the prophecy system, first introduced in Prophecy league. A number of prophecies could be fulfilled by defeating a specific unique monster while holding a specific unique item. As a result of fulfilling the prophecy, the unique item would be transformed into its fated form, giving it additional stats, a new name and new flavor text. One such prophecy, From The Void , required the character to kill Bameth while holding Blackheart in order to turn it into Voidheart.
Fated unique item | Original design | The first iteration of the upgrade process required the character to have the unique item equipped as the monster was killed in order to fulfill the prophecy. In version 3.1.0 , this restriction was loosened; the player could either equip the item or simply place it in the character's inventory while defeating the monster. In all cases, the prophecy would remain unfulfilled if the unique item was corrupted , or it was an alternate art or foil variant.
Fated unique item | Original design | When a unique item was upgraded into its fated form, its name and flavor text would change, and it would gain (and sometimes lose) one or more modifiers, depending on the item. Apart from that, it would retain all of its original properties. Its appearance, quality value, item level , and base stats would stay the same. The number, color and link configuration of its sockets would remain unchanged, along with any gems placed into those sockets. Any modifiers that carried over to the fated version would keep the same values that they had originally. For example, when Kaltenhalt was transformed into Kaltensoul , it would gain the modifier 20% of Fire Damage from Hits taken as Cold Damage FireDamageTakenAsColdUnique___1 Intrinsic 20% of Fire Damage from Hits taken as Cold Damage ; all other modifiers would remain unchanged.
Fated unique item | List of fated unique item prophecies | Before the removal of the prophecy system in version 3.17.0 , the following prophecies could be fulfilled to transform a certain unique item into its fated form. Prophecies no longer exist in the game.
Fated unique item | Phasing out period | With the removal of the prophecy system in version 3.17.0 , Grinding Gear Games began to phase out fated unique items. Over half of them became unobtainable outside of trading with other players in permanent leagues. At the same time, some unique items were reworked to be equivalent or similar to their old fated forms.
Fated unique item | Phasing out period | The fated unique items that remained during the phasing out period formed a special pool of drop-restricted unique items. Map bosses were given a small chance to drop a random fated unique item, already identified, in addition to their other loot drops. During this time, random fated unique items could also be acquired by turning in sets of Akil's Prophecy and The Transformation.
Fated unique item | Phasing out period | The pool of fated unique items included the following:
Fated unique item | Removal | In version 3.19.0 , all but one of the remaining fated unique items became drop disabled. Cameria's Avarice can be acquired using a vendor recipe ; it is no longer considered to be a fated unique item. Existing fated unique items in permanent leagues can still be obtained by trading with other players.

Fireborn | Infobox | Radius: Medium. Increases and Reductions to other Damage Types in Radius are Transformed to apply to Fire Damage. Leaders are not born in times of peace. Leaders are born in ruins and flames. Place into an allocated Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Right click to remove from the Socket. Acquisition Drop disabled. Vendor Offer 6x Alchemy Shard
Fireborn | Description | Fireborn is a unique Crimson Jewel.
Fireborn | Mechanics | Increases and Reductions to other Damage Types in Radius are Transformed to apply to Fire Damage : Passive skills that grant increases or reductions to one or more damage types within the jewel's radius are transformed into the equivalent magnitude increases or reductions to fire damage. For example, a passive skill that grants 10% increased elemental damage becomes a 10% increased fire damage bonus. Whereas a passive skill that grants 8% increased lightning damage and 8% increased physical damage becomes a 16% increased fire damage bonus.
Fireborn | Item acquisition | Fireborn is drop disabled.

Firesong | Description | Firesong is a unique Crimson Jewel.
Firesong | Mechanics | Modifiers to Ignite Duration on you apply to all Elemental Ailments causes all increased/reduced and more/less modifiers to Ignite Duration on you apply to other elemental ailments. Note that "less duration" is multiplicative and will not stack with reduced: 50% less + 50% reduced is a 75% overall reduction, not 100% reduced.
Firesong | Mechanics | Reaching 100% reduced ignite duration will result in effective immunity to elemental ailments that have a duration (as a duration of 0 seconds prevents application). This does not include ailments from ground effects or auras. Additionally, some enemies may inflict ailments with increased duration, in which case >100% ignite duration reduction is required to maintain immunity.

Flesh-Eater | Description | Flesh-Eater is a unique Dream Mace.
Flesh-Eater | Mechanics | The weapon's 1% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life and chance to cause bleeding are local, while the 3% of Attack Damage Leeched as Life against Bleeding Enemies mod is global. Because of the weapon's low dps value, for players attempting difficult content, it is recommended to find a way to utilise the global leech mod without having to actively attack with the weapon. One such method is to equip another non-mace weapon in addition to this one, then use an attack that cannot use maces (or can only use the main hand weapon), while occasionally applying bleed with an attack that can use maces, or getting chance to cause bleeding from another source. Alternatively, if the player already has sufficient damage but desires the leech to be active every attack, an attack utilizing both weapons (such as Dual Strike ) can be used.
Flesh-Eater | Item acquisition | Flesh-Eater can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Flesh-Eater | Recipes | Flesh-Eater can be created from the following recipes:
Flesh-Eater | Legacy variants | Flesh-Eater has a legacy variant.
Fleshcrafter | Infobox | Energy Shield: (246-367) Movement Speed: -3%. Requires Level 59 , 160 Int. (100-150)% increased Energy Shield Minions Convert 2% of their Maximum Life to Maximum Energy Shield per 1% Chaos Resistance they have Chaos Damage taken does not bypass Minions ' Energy Shield Minions have (50-100)% faster start of Energy Shield Recharge While Minions have Energy Shield , their Hits Ignore Monster Elemental Resistances. Imbue the body with stolen spirit, hold the leash tight. Vendor Offer 20x Alchemy Shard 3x Alteration Shard
Fleshcrafter | Description | Fleshcrafter is a unique Necromancer Silks.
Fleshcrafter | Mechanics | Minions Convert 2% of their Maximum Life to Maximum Energy Shield per 1% Chaos Resistance they have. Since Chaos Golem has a base 60% chaos resistance, [1] Fleshcrafter will convert 100% life of Chaos Golem to Energy Shield, thus the golem will die immediately after summoning.
Fleshcrafter | Mechanics | Other minions such as specific spectred monsters may have base chaos resistance as well. Thus, if the player stacks minion chaos resistance to 50% or above (passive skills such as Grave Intentions , United in Dream , Necromantic Aegis + Victario's Charity , etc.) the mod also converts 100% life of the minions to energy shield, this will result in the minion dying immediately. [2] If using this item, one must be careful not to stack too much minion chaos resistance.
Fleshcrafter | Mechanics | Additionally, Fungal Ground 's effect Allies on your Fungal Ground gain +25% to Chaos Resistance can cause minions affected by Fleshcrafter to die if they stand on the Fungal Ground.
Fleshcrafter | Item acquisition | Fleshcrafter can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Fleshcrafter | Recipes | Fleshcrafter can be created from the following recipes:
Fleshcrafter | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.

Font of Thunder | Description | Font of Thunder is a unique Mirrored Spiked Shield.
Font of Thunder | Mechanics | #% of Cold Damage taken as Lightning Damage; #% of Fire Damage taken as Lightning Damage : Combined with (10-20)% of Fire and Cold Damage taken as Lightning Damage while affected by Purity of Lightning from Watcher's Eye and Divine Flesh , it is possible to fully take Fire and Cold Damage as Lightning or Chaos Damage (both hits and DoT), removing the need to obtain any Fire or Cold Resistance. A minimum roll on the Watcher's Eye modifier is preferred to prevent taking more base damage than would have originally been taken due to the mechanics of damage taken as modifiers. This also allows players to fully utilize the benefits of Replica Nebulis without negatively impacting their defences.
Font of Thunder | Mechanics | Alternatively, same goal can be reached by combining with 30% of Fire and Cold Damage taken as Lightning Damage while affected by Purity of Lightning from Sublime Vision , (10-20)% of Fire and Cold Damage taken as Lightning Damage while affected by Purity of Lightning from Watcher's Eye and (5–10)% of Elemental taken as Chaos Damage if 4 Hunter Items are Equipped from Bound By Destiny , which allows to utilise Chaos Inoculation unlike previous method.

Forbidden Flame | Description | Forbidden Flame is a unique Crimson Jewel.
Forbidden Flame | Mechanics | The randomly rolled Ascendancy Notable will always match the required Class; however, it may be from any Ascendancy from that Class. For example, a Deadeye could still allocate Master Surgeon , a Pathfinder notable passive. The Scion version allocates one of the Ascendant class notables e.g Juggernaut ; this can be used even if it's the same Class as a Notable you've already allocated, which isn't otherwise possible. Reliquarian passives cannot roll on Forbidden Flame.
Forbidden Flame | Mechanics | Allocating a Notable that's already allocated has no further effect, so the Forbidden jewels can't be used to "double up" on a Notable.
Forbidden Flame | Mechanics | Forbidden Flame drops corrupted , so the Notable can't be changed with a Divine Orb.
Forbidden Flame | Hidden Ascendancy Notable variant | Uber Searing Exarch can drop a variant of this item that grants one of seven hidden Ascendancy Notables instead of the regular Ascendancy Notables. This variant still requires the appropriate Class and a matching Forbidden Flesh.

Fortress Covenant | Legacy variants | Not corrupted Fortress Covenant are all legacy items and only be obtained from trading with other players in the permanent leagues. Also, corrupted Fortress Covenant that have corruption implicit are also legacy.
Foulborn unique item | Description | A Foulborn unique item is a mutated version of an existing unique item that has one or more of its modifiers replaced with different ones. Foulborn unique items are "birthed" from the Genesis Tree using an Ancient Wombgift. They are exclusive to Keepers league.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | Foulborn uniques have the prefix "Foulborn" in front of the unique item's name. They also gains icons on the item's name plate and the 2D artwork gains a purple effect, somewhat similar to Replica unique items. However, Foulborn unique do not have their own slots in Unique Collection Tab , and official trade site treats Foulborn unique as the same item as the original, with the exception of the "Is Foulborn" filter.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | Foulborn uniques will have one or more of their modifiers replaced by Foulborn modifiers (also known as "mutated modifiers"), listed in purple. The Foulborn modifier that is added is directly correlated with the original modifier that was replaced. For example, if Quill Rain 's (50-100)% increased Projectile Speed is replaced, it will always result in the Foulborn modifier (40-60)% increased Area of Effect.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | Foulborn items are otherwise functionally identical to their regular versions. If an Ancient Orb is used, it will result in a non-Foulborn unique item outcome.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | As of 3.27.0c, it is no longer possible to use the 3-to-1 vendor recipe to obtain a new copy with randomized roll values. Previously, doing so would remove the "Foulborn" text in the item's name, the name plate icons, and the purple 2D art texture. The Foulborn modifier itself would still be intact and functional. Foulborn uniques are not interchangeable for their regular versions with any recipes.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | Only a select number of unique items have Foulborn variants. In general, drop-restricted uniques, such as most boss uniques, cannot be Foulborn [1] , with the exception of Breach-specific unique items.
Foulborn unique item | Mechanics | Some but not all Breach items share the same regular modifier stats and ID between their "regular" and "upgraded" versions, allowing the Foulborn modifier(s) to be found on both versions. For example, Gain (6-10)% of Fire Damage as Extra Chaos Damage found on The Red Dream and The Red Nightmare can become either +4% to Chaos Resistance per Endurance Charge (Jewel85=The Red Dream's Foulborn mod) or Fire Damage with Hits is Lucky if you've Blocked an Attack Recently (Jewel86=The Red Nightmare's Foulborn mod).
Foulborn unique item | Acquisition | Foulborn uniques can be obtained through the following methods in Keepers league :

Frostbreath | Description | Frostbreath is a unique Ornate Mace.
Frostbreath | Mechanics | Dual Wielding : When using a skill that deals damage with both weapons (such as Dual Strike ), weapons calculate damage separately, then the results are summed together. So against a chilled enemy, each hand's damage would independently be doubled, then added together to get the combined hit value for that attack. [1]

Fury Valve | Infobox | Requires Level 40. +(16-24) to Dexterity and Intelligence. Fury Valve | Description | Fury Valve is a unique Turquoise Amulet.
Fury Valve | Mechanics | Fury Valve causes projectile skills to fire their base number of projectile (s), which can then split on colliding with an enemy, toward a number of other enemies (or directions) based on the skill's number of additional projectiles. Additional arrow modifiers are considered additional projectile modifiers for bow attacks , and will add targets to split toward, but they will not affect the Splits toward # additional targets counter in skill information.
Fury Valve | Mechanics | Because split has the highest priority among projectile behaviours , Fury Valve can override default projectile behaviours from skills, such as skills that always pierce.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Rain of Arrows will shoot one projectile and split zero arrows.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Spectral Throw will split upon hitting an enemy, automatically targeting nearby enemies with its additional projectiles. Split projectiles then pierce as normal.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Volcanic Fissure will always generate only one molten projectile upon reaching its target location, which does not split further.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Scourge Arrow will split into additional projectiles, but all of the projectiles retain the maximum spore pod cap equal to channelling stages. The new projectiles cannot not create more total spore pods than the original arrow would have. For example, with five additional projectiles, if the original arrow has created one spore pod before hitting the monster, only four spore pods can be created by all of the split projectiles, and one of the new arrows will not spawn a pod.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Power Siphon will no longer apply its own Modifiers to number of Projectiles instead apply to number of targets fired at , prioritising Fury Valve. [1]
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Split Arrow & Barrage will fire their listed number of base projectiles before splitting off into the base two projectiles plus any Additional Projectiles gained from gems, passives, and/or equipped items.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Because Split Arrow of Splitting already makes use of the splitting mechanic, Fury Valve will only add an additional two arrows to the total number of split arrows.
Fury Valve | Specific skill interactions | Frost Blades will fire its listed number of base projectiles, each of which will split into any amount of Additional Projectiles gained from gems, passives, and/or equipped items.
Fury Valve | Item acquisition | Fury Valve can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Fury Valve | Recipes | Fury Valve can be created from the following recipes:

Garb of the Ephemeral | Description | Garb of the Ephemeral is a synthesised unique Savant's Robe. It always drops with one to three random synthesised implicit modifiers.
Garb of the Ephemeral | Mechanics | Nearby Allies' Action Speed cannot be modified to below Base Value : Allies within 5 metres of the character cannot have their action speed modified to below base value.
Garb of the Ephemeral | Mechanics | Nearby Enemies cannot deal Critical Strikes : Enemies within 6 metres of the character cannot deal critical strikes.
Garb of the Ephemeral | Divinity | Divinity : You are larger, deal more Elemental Damage, take less Elemental Damage and are immune to Curses.
Garb of the Ephemeral | Divinity | Divinity is a buff that is granted upon reaching 10 Divine Charges. The buff grants 75% more Elemental Damage, 25% less Elemental Damage taken, and Immune to Curses, and lasts 10 seconds.
Garb of the Ephemeral | Divine Charge | Divine Charge - You do not gain Divine Charges while Divinity is active on you. You can gain maximum of 1 charge per second. Once the maximum number of divine charges is gained, Divinity is granted for 10 seconds. Divine charges cannot be gained during Divinity. Garb of the Ephemeral is the only source of divine charges.

Ghostwrithe | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Ghostwrithe | Foulborn modifiers | Your Maximum Energy Shield is Equal to 40% of Your Maximum Life : This modifier overrides your final energy shield value and sets it at 40% of your maximum life. The energy shield value cannot be further scaled, though the percentage can be modified using a Tailoring Orb enchantment affecting defence modifiers. Notably, this stat will override the Buff grants 50% less Maximum Energy Shield stat on Energy Blade. Stats that set maximum energy shield to zero (e.g. Dissolution of the Flesh ) have priority over this stat.

Gifts from Above | Description | Gifts from Above is a unique Diamond Ring.
Gifts from Above | Mechanics | Gifts from Above triggers a level 10 Consecrate skill whenever the player deals a critical strike. The consecrated ground is placed directly under the player, and has a duration of 7.5 seconds, with a cooldown of 5 seconds.
Gifts from Above | Mechanics | If Gifts from Above is used in conjunction with an ability providing culling strike , the increased item rarity will still apply if the last hit is a critical strike. [1]
Gifts from Above | Item acquisition | Gifts from Above can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Gifts from Above belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Glimpse of Chaos | Description | Glimpse of Chaos is a unique Vaal Mask.
Glimpse of Chaos | Mechanics | Glimpse of Chaos has special Vaal Orb outcomes which replace the usual outcomes:
Glimpse of Chaos | Mechanics | If double corrupted at the Locus of Corruption , it instead has these outcomes:
Glimpse of Chaos | Mechanics | Using Vaal Orbs after it gains two implicit will cause it to randomly replace one implicit, but will retain a total of two implicits. This makes double corrupting an ideal strategy before trying to brick it into another Unique or Rare helmet. The only way to gain two implicits is to use double corruption. Using a Vaal Orb on an item that only has one implicit will result in any new implicits to overwrite the existing single implicit.
Glimpse of Chaos | Mechanics | A Tainted Mythic Orb can be used on the rare bricked Vaal Mask to have a chance to obtain a Fractal Thoughts with the special modifiers.
Glimpse of Chaos | List of possible special implicit modifiers | According to poedb , the implicit outcomes are weighted. Regular Vaal implicit outcomes cannot be rolled.

Glorious Vanity | Description | Glorious Vanity is a unique Timeless Jewel.
Glorious Vanity | Mechanics | Glorious Vanity replaces all minor and notable passive skill tree nodes in its radius randomly with the random seed roll on the jewel, making it the most unpredictable of the Timeless Jewels. Two Glorious Vanity jewels that share the same number will give the same passive augmentations.
Glorious Vanity | List of passive skills | The following variants of Glorious Vanity will transform any Keystones within the radius into the following new Keystones:
Glorious Vanity | Additive nodes | Some notable passives are changed into Might of the Vaal or Legacy of the Vaal , which grant 3-4 random stats.
Glorious Vanity | Item acquisition | Glorious Vanity has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Can drop from the Vaal legion in the Domain of Timeless Conflict. Extremely rarely drops from Viper Napuatzi in zones.

Gruthkul's Pelt | Description | Gruthkul's Pelt is a unique Wyrmscale Doublet.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Description | It is named after the in-game boss Gruthkul, Mother of Despair. However, the item was released before the actual boss fight. The item is not an exclusive drop of the boss.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Mechanics | Your Spells are disabled : This modifier disables the use of all spells ; they cannot be cast or triggered. Not being able to use spells imposes a significant handicap, since nearly all non-attack skills are spells, which includes every reservation skill, banner skills, and buff skills like Berserk and Blood Rage. Warcries are not spells and can be used normally or triggered with The Eternal Apple , but Autoexertion is disabled. Curse skills are disabled, but non-skill curses such as Curse Enemies with Vulnerability on Hit may still be applied.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Mechanics | Spell Skills deal no Damage : This serves as a redundancy with Your Spells are disabled by ensuring that no damage can be dealt by spells cast before equipping Gruthkul's Pelt. [1]
Gruthkul's Pelt | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Item acquisition | Gruthkul's Pelt can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Recipes | Gruthkul's Pelt can be created from the following recipes:
Gruthkul's Pelt | Legacy variants | Gruthkul's Pelt has legacy variants.
Gruthkul's Pelt | Legacy variants | When using a Divine Orb , the legacy mod +(20-40)% to Cold Resistance is retained; this behavior is per-item name basis and is not consistent among all other kinds of unique items. The orb CANNOT make this item gain the mod (300-400)% increased Armour and Evasion , which is consistent among legacy unique items. Even though it cannot gain the new mod, using the orb can improve the life mods, so it may be worth it to use one.

Hale Negator | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Hale Negator | Trivia | Initially, during development, Hale Negator was intended to have an effect that drained mana in exchange for life. However, technically limitations prevented such modifiers from appearing on an item with energy shield , and the item couldn't exist unless it disabled energy shield. For this reason, the item was redesigned before the release of Delve. Later, during the design of Timeless Jewel -exclusive keystones for Legion , the mana-draining life-replenishing mechanic returned the The Agnostic. [1]

Hall of Grandmasters | Infobox | Map Level: 73 Map Tier: 6 Guild Character : Ù. Contains the Immortalised Grandmasters PvP damage scaling in effect. The grandest and greatest ever to fight, Divine the champions stand tall. But match their power, best their might, And even the immortal may fall. Hall of Grandmasters | Description | Hall of Grandmasters is a unique Promenade Map.
Hall of Grandmasters | Description | The Hall of Grandmasters is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map.
Hall of Grandmasters | Layout | Instead of containing randomly spawned monsters as in most other maps, the Hall of Grandmasters includes other exiles based on characters of players who purchased a Grandmaster Supporter Pack and used a Grandmaster Export.
Hall of Grandmasters | Layout | The map consists of a foyer with several large doorways. Between each door is a hall of 50 grandmasters, separated by forcefields so you fight waves of 5 at a time. Currently there are 4 halls (Hall of the Heroes, Hall of the Eternal, Hall of the Fallen, and Hall of the Twisted), making 200 Grandmasters or 40 waves in the map. The waves are randomly [1] organised by approximate difficulty and get progressively harder.
Hall of Grandmasters | Layout | At the end of each hall there is a unique Strongbox called Grandmaster's Cache that does not spawn monsters when opened.
Hall of Grandmasters | Layout | According to the development plan, "Grandmasters using Vaal Skills will gain one Vaal Soul per skill every five seconds. It's currently possible to encounter Tormented Spirits in the map. You will not encounter Strongboxes (other than those at the end of each hall) or Rogue Exiles." [2] However currently Grandmasters generate 2 Vaal souls every second.
Hall of Grandmasters | Layout | See List of Grandmasters.
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | Some of the Grandmasters have combinations of legacy gear and very heavy defences, making them hard to kill for a lot of builds. In general these can be categorized by high recovery (life regen, ES regen), high avoidance mechanics (Block, Dodge, Evasion), high damage reduction (reduced elemental damage taken, reduced physical damage taken) and Immunities ( Chaos Inoculation , Gluttony of Elements , legacy Immortal Call ).
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | Dealing with avoidance mechanics: Block Chance Reduction Support is a must for hit based builds. If you cannot replace one of your links then you can link a fast hitting attack/spell with it to quickly reduce Grandmasters defences. Note that the "Monsters cannot Block your Attacks" Attack Mastery will not work on Grandmasters. A suggestion is | Holy Flame Totem - Multiple Totems - Faster Casting - Block Chance Reduction | which will reduce Grandmasters block chance to zero in about a second.
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | Dealing with recovery: Frost Bomb solves most recovery problems and is currently the easiest way to deal with this.
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | Dealing with damage reduction: Some Grandmasters have very high physical reduction, if you struggle with this consider converting some of your damage to elemental. As with players, reducing (not recommended due to overcapped resistances) and penetrations is a massive increase to the damage taken.
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | Dealing with Immunities: Chaos skills are NOT RECOMMENDED as there are several characters with Chaos Inoculation which makes completion impossible. All other forms of immunities, including legacy Immortal Call are time based or can be solved by converting damage or dealing a mix of elemental and physical damage.
Hall of Grandmasters | Strategy | (!)Minions: Due to the way PvP damage scaling works, minions such as Spectres and Animate Guardian are extremely vulnerable in this map. They can be instantly killed by some grandmasters even with 50000+ Life and multiple defensive links such as Minion Life Support, Meat Shield Support, and Elemental Army support.
Hall of Grandmasters | Item acquisition | Hall of Grandmasters can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Hall of Grandmasters | Recipes | Hall of Grandmasters can be created from the following recipes:
Hall of Grandmasters | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Hall of Grandmasters | Legacy variants | Hall of Grandmasters has legacy variants due to the base map came from different map series. Note that there is a vendor recipe to convert map to the current series.

Hallowed Ground | Description | Hallowed Ground is a unique Cemetery Map.
Hallowed Ground | Description | Hallowed Ground is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Hallowed Ground | Layout | The map uses the Crypt tile set. [1] A stretch of empty rooms separated by short corridors leads to a central area with the main boss, Maker of Mires. It is immune to damage until the other four occupants of the map are eliminated.
Hallowed Ground | Layout | The central area splits into four different directions which end in separate areas in which to fight the lesser bosses. The map contains no other monsters except for the five bosses. The volume of the music increases as a warning when you approach any of the bosses.

Hands of the High Templar | Description | Hands of the High Templar is a pair of unique Crusader Gloves.
Hands of the High Templar | Mechanics | Can be modified while Corrupted : This modifier allows the item to have crafting currencies and methods like crafting benches that normally can't be used on corrupted items to be allowed. Any crafting methods that require corrupted items like tainted currency items can also be used on it while it's corrupted.
Hands of the High Templar | Mechanics | Can have up to 5 Implicit Modifiers while Item has this Modifier : This modifier allows the item to accumulate up to 5 Vaal implicit mods. If the item gains another implicit mod while having 5 implicits, one of them will be replaced. [1] If the Hands of the High Templar "bricks" into a Rare item, it will retain only one of its implicits, if any.

Hazardous Research | Description | Hazardous Research is a unique Cobalt Jewel. It is a threshold jewel that enhances Spark and its variants.
Hazardous Research | Mechanics | As a threshold jewel, the full effect of Hazardous Research can be gained only when it is placed in a jewel socket that meets the threshold requirement.
Hazardous Research | Mechanics | Spark of the Nova may also benefit from this jewel, however, since the gem already has its nova behaviour, it can be benefited from the mod (10-15)% increased Lightning Damage only.
Hazardous Research | Item acquisition | Hazardous Research is drop disabled.

Headhunter | Infobox | Requires Level 40. +(25-40) to maximum Life. +(40-55) to Strength +(40-55) to Dexterity +(50-60) to maximum Life (20-30)% increased Damage with Hits against Rare monsters When you Kill a Rare monster , you gain its Modifiers for 60 seconds. Acquisition Drop level: 75 League-specific • Nemesis. Vendor Offer 10x Alchemy Shard 9x Alteration Shard
Headhunter | Description | Headhunter is a unique Leather Belt. It canonically belongs to King Kaom , who wore Lioneye 's severed head on this belt after Lioneye's defeat at Lioneye's Watch.
Headhunter | Description | Due to its powerful effects and extremely low drop rate as a tier 0 unique [1] [2] , it is typically one of the most expensive items in each league, traded amongst players for many Divine Orbs. Its rarity, desirability, and status as one of the most iconic items in Path of Exile means that it is also commonly used as a status symbol.
Headhunter | Mechanics | Upon killing a rare monster, the player gains all of its explicit monster modifiers for 60 seconds. This on-kill effect applies even if a minion or proxy kills a rare monster; this exception is likely made due to the status of this unique item. Because stolen modifiers cannot stack, [3] there is no benefit to combining Headhunter with similar effects, like Inspired Learning or Behead Support.
Headhunter | Mechanics | Stolen monster modifiers are not inherently buffs , as indicated by their lack of green-bordered status icon. Some can however grant buff auras (e.g. Precision Aura ), which do count as buffs, and interact with buff-specific stats like Mantra of Flames accordingly. [4] Other stolen modifiers (e.g. of the Marksman of the Marksman Suffix 100% increased Global Accuracy Rating (Hidden) ) do not.
Headhunter | Mechanics | Additional icons may appear if a stolen modifier grants other effects to the character.
Headhunter | Mechanics | Due to a number of Headhunter effects that grant physical gained as extra elemental damage, Headhunter may lead to accidental deaths to elemental reflect without Brutality Support or Void Manipulation Support on skills that deal non-negligible physical damage (e.g. Leap Slam or Shield Charge ).
Headhunter | Mechanics | Phasing or a travel / blink skill is recommended to move through created objects such as frost walls.
Headhunter | Mechanics | The Ambushes monster modifier, previously known as Shroud Walker , is usually considered a detrimental modifier by most players due to its unpredictable teleporting of the character into potentially dangerous or undesirable locations near monsters and may contribute to accidental deaths. The only methods to remove it are to unequip Headhunter (thus removing all current Headhunter buff stacks) or by letting the buff expire. It is unofficially considered to be a feature/quirk of Headhunter that "balances" the unique through Shroud Walker's various iterations since the item's release [5].
Headhunter | Item acquisition | Headhunter can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Headhunter belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.
Headhunter | Recipes | Headhunter can be created from the following recipes:
Headhunter | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Headhunter | Item skins | The following item skins apply only to Headhunter:

Hinekora's Sight | Legacy variants | Hinekora's Sight has legacy variants.
Hinekora's Sight | Legacy variants | The pre-3.16 legacy item has two +(6-10)% chance to Suppress Spell Damage mods, as it used to be a spell dodge and an attack dodge mod respectively. Since the combined (12-20)% value is from the two mods, to obtain the perfect roll (or the worst roll) for the combined mods with a Divine Orb , the chance would be 1/5*1/5 = 1/25, instead of flat 1/9 chance. The probability distribution is similar to the probability distribution of two dices. However, the Divine Orb would most likely decrease the value of +1000 to Accuracy Rating , since it was changed from a fixed 1000 into a variable roll (600-1000).

Hrimburn | Description | Hrimburn is a unique pair of Goathide Gloves.
Hrimburn | Mechanics | Cold Damage can Ignite: Hrimburn allows the player's cold damage to ignite enemies, in addition to freezing and chilling. The burning damage from ignites caused by cold damage is cold damage that has been converted to fire, so it will follow the usual rules for damage conversion and can be affected by both cold and fire modifiers (along with other usual modifiers to ignite). [1]
Hrimburn | Item acquisition | Hrimburn is drop disabled.
Hrimburn | Legacy variants | Legacy modifiers and base items are retained when upgrading uniques to their fated counterparts. So because Hrimsorrow has legacy variants, Hrimburn effectively does as well.
Hrimburn | Legacy variants | Despite the item is drop disabled in version 3.19.0, since the same patch also introduced new mod values for some mods of Hrimsorrow , and in turn shares with Hrimburn , making Hrimburn has two legacy variants. The infobox on the right is showing the "current" variant that can't be obtained but from using a Divine Orb on legacy Hrimburn. Note that the double legacy item can also get the new value on damage conversion, evasion rating and reflected damage, but not gaining Adds (5-7) to (13-15) Cold Damage to Spells and Attacks by using the Orb.

Hungry Abyss | Description | Hungry Abyss is a unique Viridian Jewel.
Hungry Abyss | Mechanics | Hungry Abyss does not change the source of leech. [1] For example attack leech will not apply to spells with this jewel, a character using a spell such as Incinerate linked to Added Chaos Damage Support will not be able to make use of leech modifiers such as #% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life.

Hyperboreus | Description | Hyperboreus is a unique Leather Belt.
Hyperboreus | Mechanics | Focus
Hyperboreus | Veiled modifiers | Hyperboreus has a veiled modifier :
Hyperboreus | Veiled modifiers | Unveiling an of the Veil suffix draws from a pool of generic veiled suffixes ( of the Order ).
Hyperboreus | Veiled modifiers | Note that the (13-16)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate veiled modifier is in the same mod group as one of the unique modifiers on this item ( Focus has (30-50)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate ), preventing it from being able to be chosen as a potential unveil option. However, Your Critical Strike Chance is Lucky while Focused Focus has (5-8)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate is a separate mod group, and the two modifiers to Focus cooldown recovery will stack additively.
Hyperboreus | Item acquisition | Hyperboreus has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from executed Safehouse leaders in Transportation Safehouses. Only obtainable from Safehouses of area level 78 or higher.
Hyperboreus | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Hyperboreus | Ruthless mode | Although veiled rare items cannot be found in Ruthless mode , this item will still drop with a veiled modifier and can be unveiled.
Hyperboreus | Legacy variants | Hyperboreus has legacy variants.
Hyperboreus | Legacy variants | The un-veiled modifier on the pre-3.14 legacy variants are crafted modifiers. Crafted modifiers can be removed from the item by using the crafting bench. However, player CANNOT craft any modifier back on to the item, effectively bricking it.
Hyperboreus | Legacy variants | The crafted modifiers unveiled during Betrayal league have more tiers than the current variant, as well as mods that no longer exist after Betrayal went core as one of the Master atlas mission.
Hyperboreus | Legacy variants | The current non-Crafting Bench versions of veiled modifiers have a mod value range similar or better to the pre-3.14 version of top tier crafted unveiled modifier. Instead, the top tier crafted unveiled mod has numeric change (mostly nerf and some buff). Using Divine Orb on legacy variants, may reduce (or sometimes increase) the mod value to the current range. For Hyperboreus, the hybrid Strength/ Dexterity /Intelligence mod as well as Regenerate # Energy Shield per second while a Rare or Unique Enemy is Nearby all received an upwards adjustment, while other mod unchanged.
Hyperboreus | Legacy variants | Post 3.14 / pre-3.17 non-Crafting Bench un-veiled modded Hyperboreus has one more legacy mod that not exist in other versions: Flasks applied to you have (15-18)% increased Effect 33% reduced Flask Charges gained. There is another legacy mod Regenerate 3% of Life per second during any Flask Effect which has crafted mod equivalent in the pre-3.14 legacy items. This mod is removed in 3.16 but not documented in the patch notes.
Hyperboreus | pre-3.14 veiled modifier | Note that the mod value range is an indication only, player should consider to check the legacy range in the page history of the datacard, and check the mod value range in-game by using advanced mod description by pressing Alt button in PC (other game versions may have different keybinding)
Hyperboreus | pre-3.6 veiled modifier | Note that the mod value range is an indication only, player should consider to check the legacy range in the page history of the datacard, and check the mod value range in-game by using advanced mod description by pressing Alt button in PC (other game versions may have different keybinding)

Hyrri's Truth | Description | Hyrri's Truth is a unique Jade Amulet.
Hyrri's Truth | Skills | Grants Level 30 Precision Skill has the same function as Precision.
Hyrri's Truth | Skills | Level 30 Precision reserves 255 mana. However, due to the unique mod Precision has 100% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency , the Precision skill granted from this item instead reserves 255 / ( 100 % + 100 % ) = 128 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}255/(100\%+100\%)=128 mana. If the player character has a Precision skill gem socketed elsewhere, this modifier will also affect that gem as well.
Hyrri's Truth | Skills | The increased Mana Reservation Efficiency mod is additive to other increases or reductions of (Mana) Reservation Efficiency mods. It can also affected by Fertile Catalyst to become 120% increased.
Hyrri's Truth | Item acquisition | Hyrri's Truth can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Hyrri's Truth belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.
Hyrri's Truth | Recipes | Hyrri's Truth can be created from the following recipes:
Hyrri's Truth | Legacy variants | Hyrri's Truth has legacy variants.
Hyrri's Truth | Legacy variants | Hyrri's Truth that dropped before version 3.16.0 would show the value of the mod Precision has 100% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency is 1, instead of 100 in the trade site. This is because the legacy item uses a legacy mod. The legacy item functions mostly the same as the current variant, just CANNOT be affected by Fertile Catalyst (as a value of "1.2" for the internal stat "precision_mana_reservation_efficiency_+100%" means nothing).
Hyrri's Truth | Legacy variants | Despite it displayed as Grants Level 22 Precision Skill , this is cosmetic only. The legacy item still grants level 30 skill likes the current variant, so that no need to use a Divine Orb to gain the skill gem level.
Hyrri's Truth | Legacy variants | Note that the devs also specifically set new default values of zero for the mod Adds # to # Physical Damage to Attacks, Adds # to # Cold Damage to Attacks, #% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life , so that there is no benefit to use the currency at all, as 3 legacy mods would be deleted but the mod Bow Attacks have Culling Strike would not be added.

Icefang Orbit | Description | Icefang Orbit is a unique Iron Ring.
Icefang Orbit | Mechanics | Non-Chilled Enemies you Poison are Chilled causes your poison debuffs to inflict a base 10% chill , affected by chill and ailment modifiers. This can be interesting in combination with The Golden Rule 's poison reflection and Winterweave 's chill reversion. Other useful combinations in this setup include:
Icefang Orbit | Mechanics | Note that a chill from reflected poison can still carry undesirable effects, such as Snowforged 's -5% to Elemental Resistances. This is not reversed. Additionally, because The Golden Rule and Icefang Orbit apply to skills, but Winterweave 's The Effect of Chill on you is reversed does not, proxies like totems and Mirage Archer Support may significantly slow themselves if they poison.
Icefang Orbit | Mechanics | Poisoned Enemies you Kill with Hits Shatter will not shatter enemies that die to damage over time effects such as poison, nor will it shatter unpoisoned enemies that die to a hit that would have inflicted poison on them. For this effect to shatter an enemy, that enemy must first be poisoned then die to hit damage or a culling strike from a player with this ring equipped (or from their proxy).
Icefang Orbit | Item acquisition | Icefang Orbit can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Icefang Orbit belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Indigon | Description | Indigon is a unique Hubris Circlet. It is a Shaper item & Elder item.
Indigon | High mana cost | Indigon's mana cost multiplier can complicate skill use. This can be mitigated or circumvented in several ways:
Indigon | Damage over time snapshotting | Damage over time effects snapshot their DPS value, which can make Indigon interesting for spells that apply spell damage modifiers to their damage over time (e.g. Essence Drain ). Effects created during Indigon's #% increased Spell Damage bonus will benefit from that bonus for their entire duration, even if the player loses it. The longer the duration of the DoT (scalable by skill effect duration and Temporal Chains ), the less often the player would need to spend mana for Indigon's damage bonus. (This does not apply to duration effects that hit ; these do not snapshot.)
Indigon | Item acquisition | Indigon has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Indigon can only drop from the Uber Elder in The Shaper's Realm.
Indigon | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Indigon | Legacy variants | Indigon has legacy variants.
Indigon | Legacy variants | In addition, pre-3.18 variants have only one influence: The Shaper. The legacy item does not work with Replica Eternity Shroud.
Indigon | Design attribution | Indigon was created by Path of Exile game designer Hrishi. [2]

Inevitability | Description | Inevitability is a unique Cobalt Jewel. It is a threshold jewel that alter the behaviour Rolling Magma.
Inevitability | Mechanics | As a threshold jewel, the full effect of Inevitability can be gained only when it is placed in a jewel socket that meets the threshold requirement.
Inevitability | Mechanics | The damage scales with how many time the orb has chained , instead of same damage for each chain.
Inevitability | Mechanics | Unlike other more/less damage mod, the more/less damage mod on this item does not multiplicative to themselves. The stat ids behind are local_jewel_magma_orb_damage_+%_final_per_chain_with_40_int_in_radius and local_jewel_magma_orb_damage_+%_final_with_40_int_in_radius. The mods are multiplicative to other more/less damage mods, however, since they are not using local_jewel_magma_orb_damage_+%_final as stat id.

Inextricable Fate | Description | Inextricable Fate is a pair of unique Fugitive Boots.
Inextricable Fate | Mechanics and interactions | Grasping Vines
Inextricable Fate | Mechanics and interactions | Grasping Vines on enemies are instantly removed if they teleport (e.g. The Maven or Sirus ). Enemies that move around (e.g. The Searing Exarch ) will gradually lose stacks as they move. While the Less Movement Speed from Grasping Vines is capped at 90% and can never reduce an enemy's move speed to 0, stacking additive sources of Reduced Movement Speed , such as 50% hinder from Stormbind along with 30% maim and 20% from Brain Rattler , or Vaal Rain of Arrows along with a 25% hinder can reduce enemies' movement speed to 0, which will prevent them from losing stacks by moving.
Inextricable Fate | Mechanics and interactions | All Damage inflicts Poison against Enemies affected by at least 3 Grasping Vines AllDamagePoisonsGraspingVinesUnique__1 Intrinsic All Damage inflicts Poison against Enemies affected by at least 3 Grasping Vines : This modifier both allows non- physical /non- chaos damage to poison and guarantees that all hits have 100% chance to poison. This can add a non-negligible amount of damage to builds that can overcome the downsides with little specialization into poison. This mod only affects the wearer and does not affect allies; equipping it on an Animate Guardian will not allow the player or their other minions to poison.
Inextricable Fate | Item acquisition | Inextricable Fate has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from the Eater of Worlds.

Infused Beachhead | Description | Infused Beachhead is a unique Harbinger Map.
Infused Beachhead | Description | Infused Beachhead is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. Its functionality is disabled; it cannot be used in the Map Device.
Infused Beachhead | Layout | The layout is identical to other Beachhead maps. However, the monsters are way more deadly in this version, due to 100% more Monster Life and 100% increased Monster Damage mods, and it being one tier higher than the T15 Beachhead.
Infused Beachhead | Layout | Players can clear the map by running in a clockwise or anticlockwise direction and then backtrack to the boss room. The entrance is apparently always in a three o'clock direction, at the right side of the map.
Infused Beachhead | Boss | The "boss" of this map is a portal that spawns Harbingers and King Harbinger. Harbinger spawns monsters. Killing the spawned monsters damages Harbinger as well as the portal. Completing the map drops a Harbinger Scroll.
Infused Beachhead | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Infused Beachhead | Item acquisition | Infused Beachhead is drop disabled.
Infused Beachhead | Legacy variants | Although Infused Beachhead does not have a legacy variant, there is a new version of it for each map series each league.
Infused Beachhead | Legacy variants | Selling an Infused Beachhead from the old map series to a vendor would convert the map item from legacy map series to the current map series.

Inspired Learning | Description | Inspired Learning is a unique Crimson Jewel.
Inspired Learning | Mechanics | Upon killing a rare monster , the character gains one of its modifiers for the next 20 seconds. As a special exception, this item's on-kill effect works with minions and proxies.
Inspired Learning | Mechanics | This jewel can be considered a low-power version of the similar stat on Headhunter , which provides the ability to gain all monster modifiers from a slain rare enemy. Because stolen modifiers cannot stack, [1] there is no benefit to combining Inspired Learning with Headhunter; it will only reduce the duration of one stolen modifier from 60 seconds to 20.
Inspired Learning | Mechanics | Stolen modifiers can grant buffs (e.g. Precision Aura ) but are not inherently buffs themselves; they have no green-bordered icon, and do not interact with buff-specific stats like Mantra of Flames.
Inspired Learning | Mechanics | The following modifiers can not be gained by characters:
Inspired Learning | Mechanics | The Inspired Learning jewel can only grant its effect when placed in one of the jewel sockets shown in the image, with 4 allocated notable passives inside its radius. It has no effect when socketed in a socket of a Cluster Jewel.
Inspired Learning | Item acquisition | Inspired Learning can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Inspired Learning | Recipes | Inspired Learning can be created from the following recipes:
Inspired Learning | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.

Jaws of Agony | Description | Jaws of Agony is a unique Supreme Spiked Shield.
Jaws of Agony | Mechanics | Jaws of Agony grants a level 25 Bear Trap. The bear trap will be supported by any applicable support gems socketed into the item without requiring any of the sockets to be linked. [1]
Jaws of Agony | Mechanics | Power Charge on throwing a trap : The player has a chance to gain a power charge when a trap is thrown, regardless of whether the trap is triggered.
Jaws of Agony | Mechanics | Necromantic Aegis grants power charges to minions that throw traps. [2]
Jaws of Agony | Item acquisition | Jaws of Agony can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Jaws of Agony | Recipes | Jaws of Agony can be created from the following recipes:
Jaws of Agony | Legacy variants | Jaws of Agony has legacy variants.
Jaws of Agony | Design attribution | Jaws of Agony was created by supporter kentgrav. [3] Jaws of Agony's flavor text contains forms of the words "destroy" and "dominate", which Kentgrav combines to form his in-game character name "Destrominate". Kentgrav's goals in creating the item were to give trap builds a way to gain Power Charges while also strengthening and encouraging the use of the Bear Trap Skill. [ citation needed ]

Jorrhast's Blacksteel | Description | Jorrhast's Blacksteel is a unique Steelhead.
Jorrhast's Blacksteel | Mechanics | Weapons you Animate create an additional copy : Whenever Animate Weapon is used, it will create two copies of the weapon. It cannot bypass the maximum number of Animated Weapons. This effect occurs even if the skill is triggered ; it also affects Animate Guardian's Weapon from Chains of Command. This effect will not work if Jorrhast's Blacksteel is equipped on your Animate Guardian as Animate Guardian's Weapons count as the player's minions.
Jorrhast's Blacksteel | Mechanics | This stat does not apply to The Dancing Dervish or animated weapons from Rampage.
Jorrhast's Blacksteel | Item acquisition | Jorrhast's Blacksteel can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Jorrhast's Blacksteel belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.

Kalandra's Touch | Infobox | Reflects opposite Ring. Mirrored. On one hand, you have a choice. On the other, you have its twin. Acquisition Drop level: 60 This item technically cannot be chanced as it is not possible to obtain a non-unique rarity Ring Ring. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard
Kalandra's Touch | Description | Kalandra's Touch is a unique Ring.
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Reflects your opposite Ring : Kalandra's Touch copies the stats , modifiers (including implicits and enchantments ), and properties of the ring equipped in the opposite ring slot. Kalandra's Touch does not gain any modifiers from this; it provides the character with stat bonuses that are duplicates of those granted by the other ring. [a] There is no particular benefit to equipping a Kalandra's Touch in both ring slots simultaneously, as neither will grant any stat bonuses. [1]
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Kalandra's Touch will not gain a socket by reflecting an Unset Ring , nor will it copy a gem socketed in that Unset Ring. [2] The rarity of Kalandra's Touch is always considered to be unique , regardless of the rarity of the ring it is reflecting. This matters for bonuses that care about the rarity of equipped items (e.g. Hidden Potential ). [3]
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Kalandra's Touch will copy the corrupted/uncorrupted state and influence (s) of the opposite ring [4] , which is relevant for effects that depend on equipment properties such as Shadowstitch and Bound By Destiny. [5]. The corrupted/influence state of Kalandra's Touch itself is ignored.
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | For bonuses that care about which ring slot (left or right) a ring is equipped in (e.g. Sibyl's Lament , Penumbra Ring ), Kalandra's Touch is considered to be equipped in the slot that it actually occupies, and those bonuses will apply or not apply as usual. [b] [3] In other words, the character will not be granted the left or right ring bonus twice; they will be granted the left ring bonus once and the right ring bonus once.
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Kalandra's Touch is inherently mirrored , and therefore cannot be modified. It cannot be anointed , even through the use of a Reflective Oil. However, if the ring in the opposite ring slot has been anointed, Kalandra's Touch will copy the effect of that enchantment. [6]
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Although it is not possible to corrupt a non-corrupted Kalandra's Touch because it is mirrored, it is possible to obtain one that is already corrupted, via methods that cause items to drop corrupted or from divination cards that grant a random corrupted unique. [7] If Kalandra's Touch does gain one or more implicit modifiers, those modifiers will not apply regardless of whether it is reflecting a ring or not, as the ring mechanically cannot grant its own stats. [8] Implicit modifiers that grant skills may function however, though this is inconsistent and potentially a long-standing bug. [9] [ discuss ]
Kalandra's Touch | Mechanics | Kalandra's Touch cannot reflect another ring if equipped in the additional ring slot from The Unseen Hand , nor can it reflect a ring in that slot.
Kalandra's Touch | Item acquisition | Kalandra's Touch can drop anywhere. This item technically cannot be chanced as it is not possible to obtain a non-unique rarity Ring.
Kalandra's Touch | Recipes | Kalandra's Touch can be created from the following recipes:
