Kaom's Binding | Infobox | Requires Level 56. +(25-35) to Strength. +(30-40) to Strength +(300-500) to Armour Take no Burning Damage if you've stopped taking Burning Damage Recently Nearby Enemies Convert 25% of their Physical Damage to Fire. For centuries, he burned in an unending nightmare of lava and flame. but he endured. Acquisition Drop level: 57 Ancestor. Vendor Offer 2x Alchemy Shard 6x Alteration Shard
Kaom's Binding | Description | Kaom's Binding is a unique Heavy Belt.
Kaom's Binding | Mechanics | Take no Burning Damage if you've stopped taking Burning Damage Recently : This modifier makes you unaffected by burning damage if you've stopped taking burning damage recently. This does not remove the source of burning damage (e.g. Burning Ground , Ignite ) itself.
Kaom's Binding | Mechanics | Nearby Enemies Convert 25% of their Physical Damage to Fire : This modifier has a radius of 6 metres. [1] This modifier is not the same as damage taken as modifiers. Instead, it directly converts enemies' physical damage to fire damage, stacking with any conversion inherent to the monster or its skills. This has the added benefit of lowering physical damage dealt in a hit, making armour more effective, and reducing the damage that contributes to Bleeding and Poison. However, this also allows monsters to ignite with the new fire damage on crit.
Kaom's Binding | Item acquisition | Kaom's Binding can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Kaom's Binding | Recipes | Kaom's Binding can be created from the following recipes:

Kaom's Spirit | Infobox | Armour: (242-278). Requires Level 69 , 98 Str. +(50-70) to maximum Life +(20-30)% to Fire Resistance (0.3-0.5)% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life Life Recovery from Regeneration is not applied Regenerate 1 Rage per second for every 200 Life Recovery per second from Regeneration Does not delay Inherent Loss of Rage. Who can tell when whispers are truly from a god?. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard 7x Alteration Shard
Kaom's Spirit | Description | Kaom's Spirit is a pair of unique Titan Gauntlets.
Kaom's Spirit | Mechanics | Regenerate 1 Rage per second for every # Life Recovery per second from Regeneration : Rage generation takes place after modifiers to regeneration rate are accounted for. Life recovery rate does not affect this stat. This allows any build that has access to sufficient Life regeneration to be able to use Berserk or other Rage-related mechanics.
Kaom's Spirit | Mechanics | This stat does not require exact multiples of 200 life regeneration per second to regenerate Rage. For example, a character with 20 life regeneration per second would regenerate 1 Rage per 10 seconds while wearing these gloves. [1]
Kaom's Spirit | Mechanics | Note that this item's Rage regeneration Does not delay Inherent Loss of Rage , meaning that for Rage to be gained, Rage regeneration must either exceed the inherent loss per second (10 by default), or Rage loss must first be delayed in another way (e.g. by gaining Rage on hit, or when hit with Vengeance ).
Kaom's Spirit | Mechanics | This effect cannot be used with Zealot's Oath , [2] which applies life regeneration stats to energy shield instead. It can be used with Vaal Pact , which functions by preventing positive changes to life, not by setting life recovery stats to zero.
Kaom's Spirit | Mechanics | Life Recovery from Regeneration is not applied : does not prevent effects based on the life regeneration stat, such as Pious Path 's energy shield regeneration, but does prevent effects based on applied life recovery, such as the generation of Blood Barrier from Sacrifice of Blood.
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Strong sources of life regeneration:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Ascendancies:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Skills:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Uniques:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Passives:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Other items:
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | It also has some synergy with Dissolution of the Flesh , increasing the efficacy of % life regeneration due to the Max Life multiplier without suffering from losing the regen.
Kaom's Spirit | Interactions | Life Recovery from Regeneration is not applied : This effect prevents life regeneration from recovering life, but doesn't alter its actual numeric value. Therefore, other effects that scale with the amount of life regeneration will work with Kaom's Spirit. The Every 4 seconds, Recover 1 Life for every 0.1 Life Recovery per second from Regeneration Recovery Mastery also has this modifier, and will work with Kaom's Spirit.

Kaom's Way | Infobox | Requires Level 32. +(20-30) to maximum Life. +(10-20) to Strength Regenerate 0.2% of Life per second per Endurance Charge Gain (2-4) Life per Enemy Hit with Attacks +1 to Maximum Endurance Charges 2% increased Area of Effect per Endurance Charge. Kaom guided his Karui across the sea and spilled their blood on foreign soil. Acquisition Drop disabled. Vendor Offer 12x Alchemy Shard 9x Alteration Shard
Kaom's Way | Description | Kaom's Way is a unique Coral Ring.
Kaom's Way | Item acquisition | Kaom's Way is drop disabled.
Kaom's Way | Legacy variants | Legacy modifiers and base items were retained when upgrading unique to their fated counterparts. So because Kaom's Sign has a legacy variant, Kaom's Way effectively does as well.
Kaom's Way | Legacy variants | As of version 3.16 , it is unable to can carry over the legacy 0.4% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life mod (from the legacy (pre-2.0) Kaom's Sign ) to the fated version. [1] GGG patched the fating unique mechanic some time during version 3.X.X (before 3.16.0), so that legacy mods of Doomfletch and The Stormheart cannot carry over to Doomfletch's Prism and The Stormwall as well. The prophecy mechanic is removed in version 3.17.0.
Kaom's Way | Legacy variants | Due to this, it is impossible to acquire a quasi-legacy Kaom's Way that have both the post-3.16 mod 2% increased Area of Effect per Endurance Charge and the legacy mod 0.4% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life together. The game will automatically adding Gain (2-4) Life per Enemy Hit with Attacks to the item and delete the legacy mod 0.4% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life , effectively creating a ring that have all the same mods as current variant. [1]
Karui Charge | Infobox | Requires Level 24. +(20-30) to Dexterity. +(20-30) to Strength (5-10)% increased Attack Speed +100 to Accuracy Rating 30% increased Projectile Speed 10% increased Movement Speed 30% increased Projectile Damage. Karui Charge | Description | Karui Charge is a unique Jade Amulet.
Karui Charge | Item acquisition | Karui Charge is drop disabled.
Karui Charge | Legacy variants | Legacy modifiers and base items were retained when upgrading uniques to their fated counterparts. So because Karui Ward has a legacy variant, Karui Charge effectively gains another variant in addition to its own. However, since Prophecy is removed in version 3.17.0 , legacy Karui Ward cannot be upgraded to Karui Charge anymore.
Karui Charge | Legacy variants | Note that there is a chance to create a quasi-legacy Karui Charge that also lack of the mod 30% increased Projectile Damage and also with the current mod value in #% increased Attack Speed , by using a Divine Orb on the true legacy Karui Charge, or upgrading legacy Karui Ward to Karui Charge, on after version 2.6.0 and before version 3.17.0. However, it has no point to do so (other than collector value) as it is inferior than the current variant.

Kiara's Determination | Description | Kiara's Determination is a unique Silver Flask.
Kiara's Determination | Modifiers | As with all other Silver Flasks , you are granted onslaught during its effect. You are also made immune to chills , freezes , curses , and stuns. While it is not explicitly stated, freezes and curses are removed on sip. While this flask does make you immune to stun locks while active, already-triggered stuns are not cleansed on sip and still must play their animation before you are able to move again.
Kiara's Determination | Item acquisition | Kiara's Determination can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Kiara's Determination | Recipes | Kiara's Determination can be created from the following recipes:
Kiara's Determination | Ruthless mode | Utility Flasks cannot gain charges while active in Ruthless mode. This flask also has a flask duration of (0.80-1.60) seconds instead.
Kiara's Determination | Legacy variants | Kiara's Determination has legacy variants.
Kiara's Determination | Design attribution | Kiara's Determination was created by supporter CKilgannon. [1]

Kikazaru | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Kikazaru | Legacy variants | Kikazaru has legacy variants.
Kikazaru | Trivia | 🙈🙉🙊 "See no evil. Hear no evil. Speak no evil." Kikazaru's namesake comes from the Three Wise Monkeys , a Japanese pictorial maxim representing the Buddhist tradition of not dwelling on evil thoughts. The three monkeys are Mizaru , covering his eyes, who sees no evil; Kikazaru , covering his ears, who hears no evil; and Iwazaru , covering his mouth, who speaks no evil.
Kikazaru | Trivia | The jewel clasp on the 2D item art resembles hands covering one's ears.

Kiloava's Bluster | Description | Kiloava's Bluster is a unique Ironwood Buckler.
Kiloava's Bluster | Mechanics | #% chance for Elemental Resistances to count as being 90% against Enemy Hits ignores your current resistance values including exposure and curses and treats it as being at 90% actual and maximum resistances when the modifier applies. This effect overrides your maximum resistance value, including effects that set your resistances to a fixed value such as Loreweave or Replica Loreweave.
Kiloava's Bluster | Mechanics | This effect does not work against damage over time.

Vendor Offer 1x Regal Orb. Weapon DPS Physical: (635.2-764.6) Phys+Chaos: (635.2-764.6) Total: (635.2-764.6)
Kingmaker | Description | Kingmaker is a unique Despot Axe.
Kingmaker | Mechanics | Equipping this item grants the Kingmaker's Presence buff to nearby allies. It does not affect the user. The effects of the buff depends on the version of the Kingmaker, which the pre-3.6.0 legacy item only grant Culling Strike and increased Item Rarity (IIR).
Kingmaker | Mechanics | Note that the "nearby" range is very small; it is measured to be a 3 metre radius. Using the Meat Shield support will keep an Animate Guardian equipped with the Kingmaker from wandering outside of the player character's range while standing still. When the character walks around however, the Animate Guardian will fall in and out of range to apply Kingmaker's Presence.
Kingmaker | Item acquisition | Kingmaker has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Kingmaker is a vendor recipe item.
Kingmaker | Recipes | Kingmaker can be created from the following recipes:
Kingmaker | Legacy variants | Kingmaker has legacy variants.
Kingmaker | Legacy variants | The legacy mod Nearby Allies have +1 Fortification is a mod that acts as a description of the unique item aura but actually does nothing (the aura is granted by a separate hidden mod ), the item's aura still grants +10 Fortification regardless of if it is the current variant or legacy, unless it is pre-3.6 variants that do not even have the Nearby Allies have +1 Fortification mod.
Kingmaker | Legacy variants | According to datamined data, the pre-3.6 legacy Kingmaker actually has a larger aura radius (5 meter verse 3 meter) for granting Culling Strike and IIR to allies, [a] which match the measurements done by redditor theuberelite. [1] This is despite the buff using the same status icon for both current and pre-3.6 legacy variant buffs; the status icon comes from a buff with internal id: "increased_item_rarity_aura", with the fortification (id: kingmaker_fortify_aura), crit multi (id: critical_strike_multiplier_aura) and culling strike (id: culling_strike_aura) actually coded as separate hidden buffs without status icons. In other words, it is not a bug that the aura from a pre-3.6 legacy Kingmaker does not grant crit multi or fortification despite the status icon saying it does.

Kitava's Thirst | Infobox | Armour: (202-246) Energy Shield: (42-52). Requires Level 44 , 50 Str, 50 Int. 50% chance to Trigger Socketed Spells when you Spend at least 100 Mana on an Upfront Cost to Use or Trigger a Skill , with a 0.1 second Cooldown 15% reduced Cast Speed (70-80)% increased Armour and Energy Shield +(30-50) to maximum Mana. Tukohama, Tawhoa and Kitava went to the lake to fish. Tawhoa weaved a net to catch the fish, Tukohama built a fire to cook the fish, and Kitava swallowed the entire lake, fish and all, with a single gulp. Vendor Offer 7x Alteration Shard
Kitava's Thirst | Description | Kitava's Thirst is a unique Zealot Helmet.
Kitava's Thirst | Strategy | There are several methods that can be used to raise the cost of skills to help Kitava's Thirst trigger its socketed spells more regularly and expand the list of usable skills that can trigger this unique. Rapid cast or attacking abilities are preferred when using Kitava's Thirst to increase the likeliness of socketed spells triggering. Because of the high mana cost needed, several spells and most attack skills struggle to reach the 100 mana threshold, however there are a few ways to increase their cost:
Kitava's Thirst | Strategy | Channelled skills are the fastest skills, but they activate the cast only once per use (if the mana cost is sufficient), so the player has to start and end channelling rapidly, which is tedious but possible.
Kitava's Thirst | Strategy | Fastest non-channelled spells are Unearth and Armageddon Brand. Frenzy is the fastest attack skill. Instant skills can be used, but might be undesirable because of their cooldown.
Kitava's Thirst | Strategy | Mana consumption of a character using Kitava's Thirst offensively is huge. On average, one needs to spend 200 mana for one activation and the cost of the triggered socketed spells also need to be paid. Nexus Gloves can help mitigate the cost.
Kitava's Thirst | Item acquisition | Kitava's Thirst can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Kitava's Thirst | Recipes | Kitava's Thirst can be created from the following recipes:
Kitava's Thirst | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Kitava's Thirst | Legacy variants | Kitava's Thirst has a legacy variant.

Kongming's Stratagem | Description | Kongming's Stratagem is a unique Ancient Spirit Shield.
Kongming's Stratagem | Mechanics | Kongming's Stratagem triggers level 20 Fog of War , which is affected by socketed support gems regardless of whether they are linked. However, since Fog of War is not granted by a skill gem, it cannot be supported by Empower Support , Enhance Support or Enlighten Support. Fog of War creates a smoke cloud in an area when triggered that lasts for a base duration of 3 seconds. It can be compared to a Stibnite Flask. Fog of War can be supported by area and duration support gems such as Increased Area of Effect Support and More Duration Support , while its trigger cooldown can be modified by items like The Flow Untethered.
Kongming's Stratagem | Item acquisition | Kongming's Stratagem can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Kongming's Stratagem | Recipes | Kongming's Stratagem can be created from the following recipes:
Kongming's Stratagem | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Kongming's Stratagem | Legacy variants | Kongming's Stratagem has a legacy variant.

Lavianga's Spirit | Item acquisition | Lavianga's Spirit can drop anywhere. It can be chanced. Lavianga's Spirit belongs to a special group of unique items called league-specific items , which can be obtained from certain divination cards and other sources in addition to their normal acquisition methods.
Lavianga's Spirit | Recipes | Lavianga's Spirit can be created from the following recipes:
Lavianga's Spirit | Legacy variants | Lavianga's Spirit does not have any legacy variants that have different mods.
Lavianga's Spirit | Legacy variants | However, during version 3.13.X, Fateful Meeting can grant a random double-influenced league-specific unique item, including this flask. This bug has been fixed in version 3.14.0, so that influenced version of Lavianga's Spirit are legacy too. There is no actual in-game benefit to use influenced flask, however. Player cannot use Influenced Exalted Orb to create an influenced flask base either.
Lavianga's Spirit | Build of the Week | Lavianga's Spirit was featured in Season 2 Episode 12 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.
Lavianga's Spirit | Alternate artwork | In addition, this flask can be acquired from Voidborn Reliquary , which use different foil color.

Leadership's Price | Description | Leadership's Price is a unique Onyx Amulet.
Leadership's Price | Mechanics | Leadership's Price drops corrupted. A Tainted Catalyst must be used to roll a random catalyst quality as well as a roll from (0-20)% quality. Note that only the Attribute catalyst will have any impact on the mods on Leadership's Price.
Leadership's Price | Item acquisition | Leadership's Price has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Leadership's Price drops from the twin bosses of Contract: The Twins unique contract.

Leer Cast | Description | Leer Cast is a unique Festival Mask.
Leer Cast | Mechanics | Equipping Leer Cast applies a special aura buff " Damage Aura " that affects the player character and nearby allies. This bonus applies to all damage, including that of spells , traps , and totems.
Leer Cast | Mechanics | Like other similar effects (aura granted by item but not an aura skill granted by item), the aura buff won't be affected by any of the player character's aura bonuses, however, the effects will stack with other party members also equipped with Leer Cast. [1]
Leer Cast | Mechanics | However, since the item also has another mod 25% reduced Damage the net effect would be 25% increased Damage for the player character and 50% increased damage for the nearby allies. increase/reduce mods are additive to each other.
Leer Cast | Item acquisition | Leer Cast can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Leer Cast | Recipes | Leer Cast can be created from the following recipes:
Leer Cast | Legacy variants | Leer Cast has a legacy variant.

Legacy of Fury | Description | Legacy of Fury is a pair of unique Wyrmscale Boots.
Legacy of Fury | Mechanics | Nearby Enemies are Scorched : This effect applies the alternate fire ailment Scorch to nearby enemies. The Scorch has a base magnitude of -10%, scaled by effect modifiers for Scorch and non-damaging ailments. The effect's 3 metre radius is not modified by area of effect modifiers.
Legacy of Fury | Mechanics | #% chance when you Kill a Scorched Enemy to Burn Each surrounding Enemy for 4 seconds, dealing 8% of the Killed Enemy's Life as Fire Damage per second : This effect applies a burning debuff to enemies around the killed enemy. Its radius can be scaled with area of effect modifiers.

Legacy of the Rose | Description | Legacy of the Rose is a unique Judgement Staff.
Legacy of the Rose | Mechanics | Summon Shaper Memory : Gaining 10 stacks of Remembrance will summon an untargetable, invulnerable Shaper Memory, similar to the Shaper clones found in the Shaper pinnacle boss encounter or from the modifier Tier 17 maps. It is not a minion.
Legacy of the Rose | Mechanics | Grants Level 20 <random Shaper skill>, which will be used by Shaper Memory : The summoned Shaper Memory will use one of three skills, based on the value rolled for this modifier. A Divine Orb can be used to reroll which skill the Shaper Memory uses.
Legacy of the Rose | Mechanics | All of these skills count as being used by a proxy and will not cause the character to take reflect damage. They scale with the character's offensive stats. They do not count as triggered and are incompatible with Triggerbots.
Legacy of the Rose | Mechanics | Gain 1 Remembrance when you spend a total of 200 Energy Shield with no Shaper Memory Summoned : Unlike other similarly worded stats, it is possible to gain multiple stacks of Remembrance on a single skill use. [1] Energy shield costs of all skills are tracked together.
Legacy of the Rose | Item acquisition | Legacy of the Rose has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.

Leper's Alms | Infobox | Chance to Block: (29-30)% Evasion: (906-1218) Energy Shield: (186-245) Movement Speed: -3%. Requires Level 66 , 85 Dex, 85 Int. +5% chance to Suppress Spell Damage. (500-600)% increased Evasion and Energy Shield +(60-80) to maximum Life 40% increased Duration of Ailments on Enemies +(3-4)% Chance to Block Shared Suffering. One's burden is another's gift. Acquisition Drop level: 77. Vendor Offer 14x Alchemy Shard 6x Alteration Shard
Leper's Alms | Description | Leper's Alms is a unique Mirrored Spiked Shield.
Leper's Alms | Mechanics | Shared Suffering : All Damage from Hits will cause Elemental Ailments you are suffering. Elemental Ailments you inflict are removed from you. [1]
Leper's Alms | Mechanics | Shared Suffering does not transfer ailments. Rather, while your character is suffering any elemental ailment (or counts as suffering with e.g. Vaal Arctic Armour ), hits from you, or from your skills used by proxies , will have a 100% chance to inflict that ailment, and all damage types will contribute to the effect of that ailment. For example, while your character is shocked , physical damage will shock without needing shock chance or a critical strike. This part of the modifier is similar to a Conflux , though conditional on suffering the ailment. For an ailment to be removed from you, you still need to successfully apply the ailment (typically by having sufficiently high damage). [2]
Leper's Alms | Interactions | Vessel of Vinktar : inflicting shock on yourself by using this flask will immediately remove that shock from you.
Leper's Alms | Item acquisition | Leper's Alms can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Leper's Alms | Recipes | Leper's Alms can be created from the following recipes:
Leper's Alms | Legacy variants | Leper's Alms has legacy variants.

Lethal Pride | Description | Lethal Pride is a unique Timeless Jewel.
Lethal Pride | Mechanics | Notable passives in radius of Lethal Pride get additional mods, which depend on the roll the jewel itself has. Two Lethal Pride jewels that share the same number will give the same notable passive augmentations and are therefore functionally identical. This also means that there are 8001 different versions as far as notable passive changes are concerned.
Lethal Pride | Mechanics | Lethal Pride also adds +2 Strength to all travel minor passives (+10 to an attribute) and +4 Strength to all other minor passives in its radius.
Lethal Pride | List of passive skills | The following variants of Lethal Pride will transform any Keystones within the radius into the following new Keystones:
Lethal Pride | Additive nodes | Lethal Pride will modify every notable in its radius to add another stat line in addition to the notable's ordinary effect. Small passives gain +2 or +4 Strength.
Lethal Pride | Additive nodes | The possible bonuses it can grant, depending on the seed, are:
Lethal Pride | Item acquisition | Lethal Pride has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Can drop from the Karui legion in the Domain of Timeless Conflict. Extremely rarely drops from Queen Hyrri Ngamaku in zones.
Lethal Pride | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Lethal Pride | Recipes | Lethal Pride can be created from the following recipes:
Lethal Pride | Recipes | [1]
Lethal Pride | Item skins | The following item skins apply only to Lethal Pride:
Lethal Pride | Legacy variants | Lethal Pride has a legacy variant.
Lethal Pride | Legacy variants | Legacy jewel with the value "Kiloava" would grant the keystone Glancing Blows instead. However, even if the jewel was obtained before version 3.11.0 , using a Divine Orb , the player cannot obtain Kiloava variant anymore. Moreover, using a Divine Orb on legacy jewel Kiloava variant, will delete the "Kiloava" mod value forever.

Lifesprig | Description | Lifesprig is a unique Driftwood Wand.
Lifesprig | Modifiers | Regenerate (6-8) Life over 1 second when you Cast a Spell – The life regeneration property gives a buff called 'Life Regeneration' for one second after casting a spell , this buff does not stack only refreshes upon cast. It does not apply to placing totems , traps or mines , even if they have the spell tag. However, detonating a mine will grant the buff.

Lightbane Raiment | Mechanics | Lightbane Raiment creates a patch of desecrated ground beneath the character when blocking. It has a radius of 16 units (1.6m) and deals 250 chaos damage per second for 8 seconds. [1]
Lightbane Raiment | Mechanics | Damage from desecrated ground can be increased from global damage sources, but is not affected by support gems in the chest piece. [ citation needed ]

"There's nothing like imminent death to galvanize one's purpose in life." - Malachai the Soulless. Vendor Offer 6x Alchemy Shard 8x Alteration Shard
Lightning Coil | Description | Lightning Coil is a unique Desert Brigandine.
Lightning Coil | Modifiers | Lightning Coil has the property " physical damage taken as elemental ". When equipped, portion of physical damage from hits is taken as lightning damage. Because this means a smaller percentage of damage is physical, it makes armour dramatically more effective vs physical hits. The downside is that armour will not mitigate the lightning portion but lightning resistance will. [1] Note that this damage taken as mod does not apply to damage over time , as it specifically states damage from hits.
Lightning Coil | Modifiers | Increased lightning damage taken due to this mod scales the magnitude of shock applied to the wearer. [2]
Lightning Coil | Item acquisition | Lightning Coil can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Lightning Coil | Recipes | Lightning Coil can be created from the following recipes:
Lightning Coil | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Lightning Coil | Legacy variants | Lightning Coil has legacy variants.
Lightning Coil | Design attribution | Lightning Coil was created by supporter Gulruon. [ citation needed ]
Lightning Coil | Build of the Week | This item was featured in Season 2 Episode 3 and Episode 13 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.
Lightning Coil | Foil | They are also available in Voidborn Reliquary , which the area awards Voidborn colored unique by random.
Lightning Coil | Foil | Known Valdo's Maps that award Celestial foil Lightning Coil are "Lost Refuse" Tropical Island Map , "Tranquil Mire" Strand Map , "Vortex Alcove" Toxic Sewer Map , "Cabal Bind" Coves Map , etc.
Lightpoacher | Description | Lightpoacher is a unique Great Crown with two attainable variants.
Lightpoacher | Skills | Spirit Burst
Lightpoacher | Skills | Spirit Burst is a spell, and is disabled while wearing Gruthkul's Pelt.
Lightpoacher | Mechanics | Spirit Charge
Lightpoacher | Mechanics | Spirit Burst
Lightpoacher | Mechanics | Spirit Charges, unlike other basic charges , offer no buffs or benefits by default and only have additional effects when from Lightpoacher or Hale Negator. Sources of generic charge duration like Charge Mastery and Trickster 's Swift Killer will allow Spirit Charges to be retained longer.
Lightpoacher | Mechanics | Gain 5% of Physical Damage as Extra Damage of each Element per Spirit Charge : This modifier grants 5% of physical damage as fire, cold, and lightning damage per Spirit Charge. This can stack extremely high in builds investing into abyssal sockets and Abyss jewels.
Lightpoacher | Strategies | Channelling skills will consume a Spirit Charge when the skill is first activated, but not while channeling. This allows you to maintain most of your Spirit Charges if you are able to channel a skill indefinitely.
Lightpoacher | Strategies | Disabling Spirit Burst will prevent you from consuming Spirit Charges. Trigger support gems (e.g. Cast when Damage Taken Support cannot support Spirit Burst, preventing it from being disabled by the double trigger interaction. There are three known methods to disable Spirit Burst, allowing the character to benefit from the full damage bonus (with or without Shroud of the Lightless ) without expending Spirit Charges:
Lightpoacher | Strategies | 1. Insufficient life : Socketing Lifetap Support and Archmage Support will cause Spirit Burst to gain a Life Cost; this can effectively allow Chaos Inoculation or Low Life builds that have current life lower than the cost to disable Spirit Burst entirely, allowing the character to benefit from the damage bonus without expending Spirit Charges.
Lightpoacher | Strategies | 2. Insufficient mana : Alternatively, The Agnostic 's degeneration can be used to drain mana to 0, preventing Spirit Burst from triggering with Archmage Support only. However, this setup has a few considerations:
Lightpoacher | Strategies | 3. Spells are disabled : Gruthkul's Pelt disables Spirit Burst (as it is a spell); however, this also disables all auras and utility spells.
Lightpoacher | Strategies | Spirit Charges can be maintained by killing worms from The Writhing Jar , though the life and mana recovery from the flask may cause some issues with the first two strategies. If the player opts to invest in flask charge generation, going low life with Coruscating Elixir may synergize with Pain Attunement or Bloodthirst Support for spells and attacks respectively. Alternatively, Penance Mark can be used.
Lightpoacher | Item acquisition | Lightpoacher has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
Lightpoacher | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Lightpoacher | Recipes | Lightpoacher can be created from the following recipes:

Lightpoacher (1 Abyssal Socket) | Strategies | 2. Insufficient mana : Alternatively, The Agnostic 's degeneration can be used to drain mana to 0, preventing Spirit Burst from triggering with Archmage Support only. However, this setup has a few considerations:
Lightpoacher (1 Abyssal Socket) | Strategies | 3. Spells are disabled : Gruthkul's Pelt disables Spirit Burst (as it is a spell); however, this also disables all auras and utility spells.

If the player opts to invest in flask charge generation, going low life with Coruscating Elixir may synergize with Pain Attunement or Bloodthirst Support for spells and attacks respectively.

Lightpoacher (1 Abyssal Socket) | Legacy variants | Lightpoacher has a legacy variant.
Lightpoacher (1 Abyssal Socket) | Legacy variants | Do not Divine the item, despite by doing do will buff the #% chance to gain a Spirit Charge on Kill mod to 100%. The dev has specifically set a new default value of zero for the second legacy mod Recover #% of Life when you lose a Spirit Charge so that Divine Orb will "delete" this mod forever, and not gaining the mod Gain 5% of Physical Damage as Extra Damage of each Element per Spirit Charge. Buy a non-legacy Lightpoacher instead if aiming for Gain a Spirit Charge on Kill mod.

Lioneye's Fall | Description | Lioneye's Fall is a unique Viridian Jewel.
Lioneye's Fall | Mechanics | Any passive whose description includes the keyword melee or the names of any melee weapon types (such as staff or axe ) functions as if that word is replaced with the bow keyword. Examples include:
Lioneye's Fall | Mechanics | In the case of the transformed modifier additional Block Chance with Bows , this will only provide block chance provided the player already has some base block chance from an item, such as Rearguard or The Anvil.
Lioneye's Fall | Mechanics | Occasionally, a transformed modifier may never be useful. For example, any transformed passive pertaining to dual wielding will do nothing because one cannot wield two bows.
Lioneye's Fall | Interaction with Timeless Jewels | Any node within the radius of a Timeless Jewel cannot be modified by other jewels. [1] This interaction is often encountered when a Brutal Restraint Jewel is placed in the Jewel Socket near the Heartseeker notable and Lioneye's Fall is placed in the socket above the Acrobatics keystone; if this is done then the From the Shadows (Dagger) passive wheel cannot be transformed to bow nodes by the Lioneye's Fall Jewel.
Lioneye's Fall | Interaction with Cluster Jewels | Lioneye's Fall does not work for passives on a cluster jewel, even if it was socketed on the jewel socket from within that cluster jewel.

Loreweave | Description | Loreweave is a unique Elegant Ringmail.
Loreweave | Mechanics | The Your Maximum Resistances are (76-78)% modifier is a hard cap on the player's maximum resistance. Players cannot get above the stated roll, even when using a Purity aura , but also cannot drop below the stated value, usually as a consequence of map modifiers. Thus, the player's maximum resistances will always be equal to that number. However, players still have to gather resistances to reach that number, as Loreweave doesn't grant the resistances by itself.
Loreweave | Mechanics | Like with many vendor recipes, the resulting Loreweave's item level is equal to the lowest item level of all the unique rings used to create it. Consequently the item often does not meet the required item level of 50 to have up to six sockets naturally. The mod Has 6 Sockets ignores this limit and grants the item six sockets to begin with.
Loreweave | Item acquisition | Loreweave has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
Loreweave | Item acquisition | This item can be acquired by vendoring 60x Unique Rings.
Loreweave | Legacy variants | Loreweave has a legacy variant.
Lori's Lantern | Infobox | Requires Level 30. +(8-10)% to all Elemental Resistances. +10% to all Elemental Resistances (6-8)% increased Movement Speed when on Low Life 31% increased Light Radius +(20-25)% to Chaos Resistance when on Low Life Damage of Enemies Hitting you is Unlucky while you are on Low Life. By its light, Lori led her young charges to safety and bound up their wounds. Her sword arm was iron, yet her heart gentle and proof against hate. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard 6x Alteration Shard
Lori's Lantern | Description | Lori's Lantern is a unique Prismatic Ring.
Lori's Lantern | Modifiers | While a character equipped with Lori's Lantern is on low life , enemies are unlucky when damaging the character. This only influences damage rolls; it has no effect on an enemy's chance to hit or land a critical strike on the character. [1]
Lori's Lantern | Modifiers | Lori's Lantern provides a bright, white glow cosmetic effect on the hand of the character. The left ring slot corresponds to the right hand of the character, and vice versa. Equipping a Lori's Lantern in each slot will apply a glow effect on each hand. The glow effect can be applied to a different ring using a skin transfer. There is a graphical glitch that causes the glow effect to be misplaced when the character has a bow equipped. The left ring slot causes the tip of the arrow to shine, and the right slot causes the shaft of the arrow to shine. [2]
Lori's Lantern | Item acquisition | Lori's Lantern can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Lori's Lantern | Recipes | Lori's Lantern can be created from the following recipes:
Lori's Lantern | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.

Lost Unity | Description | Lost Unity is a unique Formless Ring.
Lost Unity | Mechanics | Herald of the Hive : Herald skill with a 25% Mana Reservation cost that grants a buff that grants more damage per Herald affecting you. While active, it periodically releases a purple pulse around your character that deals all five damage types to enemies around you. Inflicting ailments causes you to gain Otherworldly Pressure, which increases the frequency of the pulses. You will lose Otherworldly Pressure over time, at a rate which increases the more of it you have. The skill itself cannot apply ailment, cannot be reflected and is not affected by modifiers to spell damage.
Lost Unity | Item acquisition | Lost Unity has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Obtained from a vendor recipe using 5 different unique Breach rings.

Mageblood | Description | Mageblood is a unique Heavy Belt ,
Mageblood | Description | Due to its powerful flask -enhancing effects and extremely low drop rate as a tier 0 unique, it is typically one of the most expensive items in each league, traded amongst players for several hundred Divine Orbs. It is also commonly used as a status symbol.
Mageblood | Flask options | Since Utility Flasks used for Mageblood are usually rolled with a 25% increased effect prefix and a 70% increased effect Enkindling Orb enchantment , both the flask implicit and suffix are scaled by 95%. This, without any additional flask scaling from other items or passives, offers the following stats, of which up to four can be picked:
Mageblood | Flask options | Some useful synergies that can be used with these flask effects include Stormshroud with chance to avoid being shocked (NOT reduced effect of shock or immunity to shock!) and Self-Flagellation with reduced curse effect.

Mahuxotl's Machination | Infobox | Chance to Block: 26% Armour: (146-167) Energy Shield: (30-35) Movement Speed: -3%. Requires Level 46 , 60 Str, 60 Int. Corrupted Soul Divine Flesh Eternal Youth Everlasting Sacrifice Immortal Ambition Vaal Pact. The Banished Architect sought to employ all the darkest secrets of the Vaal. at the same time. Acquisition Drop restricted Can be obtained as a boss drop from the Trialmaster in an Ultimatum encounter. Vendor Offer 36x Alchemy Shard
Mahuxotl's Machination | Description | Mahuxotl's Machination is a unique Steel Kite Shield.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Mechanics | The Corrupted Soul , Divine Flesh , Eternal Youth , Immortal Ambition , and Vaal Pact modifiers grant the same effects as the keystones Corrupted Soul , Divine Flesh , Eternal Youth , Immortal Ambition , and Vaal Pact. Allocating any of these keystones in addition to equipping this item will impart no additional benefit.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Mechanics | Everlasting Sacrifice has the effect When you reach full Energy Shield, Sacrifice all Energy Shield to gain +5% to all Maximum Resistances for 4 seconds. See its respective page for more information.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Mechanics | Some of the effects of these keystones nullify or override others. The effects of each keystone are listed below; those that are nullified when combined with the others are crossed out.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Mechanics | Damage taken as - Mahuxotl's Machination grants Divine Flesh. When combined with Tempered by War , no lightning or cold damage is taken, making it ideal to combine with it. Half of the damage is taken as fire and the other as chaos. This allows the player to completely ignore gearing for Cold or Lightning resistances. See Damage taken as for more information.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Strategy | Everlasting Sacrifice on Mahuxotl's Machination is harder to maintain due to Immortal Ambition disabling ES regeneration and recharge as well as applying a constant ES degen, as well Corrupted Soul raising the amount of ES recovery needed by adding maximum ES based on life. This leaves instant recovery and leech as the only viable strategies for ES recovery.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Strategy | Vaal Pact disables all sources of life recovery besides leech , but Immortal Ambition allows you to recover ES from life leech. Endgame melee characters will usually heal the full 10% of maximum health with each hit instantly with Vaal Pact. If they're on full life to trigger Immortal Ambition, melee characters will only need to hit a few times to fill their ES pool.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Strategy | If the player's energy shield is full and they weapon swap into Mahuxotl's Machination the buff will apply. This allows the player to gain the benefits of Everlasting Sacrifice without having the shield equipped, though this method requires the player to manually weapon swap to use.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Item acquisition | Mahuxotl's Machination has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Can be obtained as a boss drop from the Trialmaster in an Ultimatum encounter.
Mahuxotl's Machination | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:

Manastorm | Description | Manastorm is a unique Lacewood Spirit Shield.
Manastorm | Mechanics | The sacrifice effect occurs every time you cast a spell ( Spell Totems , traps , mines , and triggering do NOT count as casting), there is no cooldown. Each sacrifice produces its own separate effect , but only the strongest one applies at a time. [1] The effect still applies to you after switching weapon set.
Manastorm | Mechanics | Sacrificed mana does not count as spent. It cannot be used with effects like Gain a Power Charge after Spending a total of 200 Mana.
Manastorm | Mechanics | The effect is not considered a buff therefore does not get affected by Ichimonji.
Manastorm | Mechanics | The added maximum lightning damage is not specific to any type of skills and is applied to attacks , spells , and secondary damage.
Manastorm | Mechanics | Brand Recall at level 1 and 0 quality and Phase Run are instant and have a cooldown of exactly 4 seconds, so they can be held down to effectively maintain the buff, as long as the player's mana is full when their cooldowns recover.
Manastorm | Item acquisition | Manastorm has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from Nightmare of Uhtred in Citadel Map.
Manastorm | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Manastorm | Legacy variants | Manastorm has a legacy variant.

Mao Kun | Description | Mao Kun is a unique Shore Map.
Mao Kun | Description | Mao Kun is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Mao Kun | Layout | The map is an island inhabited by pirates that revive as undead when the player interacts with the boss (simply named Fairgraves). They then spawn a number of loot containers called Booty Chests , which drop currency and spawn the boss Fairgraves, Never Dying.
Mao Kun | Layout | Fairgraves will appear a few seconds after opening a Booty Chest. When the first seven booty chests are opened, Fairgraves only uses his basic Spectral Throw with additional projectiles, and will disappear after 10–20 seconds, regardless of whether or not you engage in combat with him. The loot from Fairgraves apparitions is proportional to % of life lost. It is possible to open multiple chests next to each other, spawning multiple copies of Fairgraves, but you can simply run away before they even spawn and return to loot later. He will also despawn after a fixed time even if the player pauses the game. [1]
Mao Kun | Layout | When the final eighth Booty Chest is opened, a large pack of ghost pirates are summoned in a circle around the chest and the final Fairgraves boss appears on top of the chest, using all of his abilities and he can finally be killed.
Mao Kun | Boss | Fairgraves, Never Dying :
Mao Kun | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Mao Kun | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Mao Kun | Item acquisition | Mao Kun can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Mao Kun | Legacy variants | Mao Kun has legacy variants.
Mao Kun | Design attribution | Mao Kun was created by supporter magiceffect. He wanted Tricornes. [ citation needed ]

Martyr of Innocence | Description | Martyr of Innocence is a unique Highborn Staff.
Martyr of Innocence | Mechanics | Grants level 15 Battlemage's Cry skill. This allows you to socket a spell in the staff, and after warcrying, your next attacks will be exerted and cast the socketed spell. This is useful in various setups such as running a cast on crit setup in the body which casts that spell, but also is exerted when used, casting the spell(s) in the staff.
Martyr of Innocence | Mechanics | Note that skill cannot have two trigger. Since Battlemage's Cry is a hybrid skill that also contains Battlemage's Cry Support , socketed spell cannot be use by the player directly and instead only able to be trigger from Battlemage's Cry. Socketing another trigger gem will disable the other socketed spell gem altogether.
Martyr of Innocence | Item acquisition | Martyr of Innocence can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Martyr of Innocence | Legacy variants | Martyr of Innocence has legacy variants.
Martyr of Innocence | Legacy variants | A legacy (pre- version 3.25.0 ) Highborn Staff base can be used to chance a legacy /quasi-legacy Martyr of Innocence, which will retain the old implicit +12 or 18% or 20% Chance to Block Attack Damage while wielding a Staff and will not have the legacy explicit mod.
Martyr of Innocence | Legacy variants | The legacy implicit has a new default value of 20.

Martyr's Crown | Description | Martyr's Crown is a unique Vine Circlet.
Martyr's Crown | Mechanics | The Pain Attunement mod has the same effect as the Pain Attunement keystone. Taking the keystone in addition to equipping this item will impart no additional benefit.
Martyr's Crown | Mechanics | Like other fated item, some mod value from the original item is retained. In Martyr's Crown, the mod (60-80) to maximum Energy Shield (since version 3.19 is +(150-225) ) from the original item is retained, and the prophecy added one more ES mod (110-130) to maximum Energy Shield (before 3.0 it was +(200-220) ). Player need to use advanced mod description (default PC keybind: Alt ) to view the mods however, as it will displays as +(170-210) to maximum Energy Shield. (since version 3.19 it is +(260-355). )
Martyr's Crown | Item acquisition | Martyr's Crown is drop disabled.
Martyr's Crown | Legacy variants | Martyr's Crown has legacy variants. The basetype properties and some explicit modifiers and are retained when upgrading unique to their fated counterparts. So because Crown of Thorns has legacy variants, Martyr's Crown effectively does as well.
Martyr's Crown | Legacy variants | The above table does not take into account players that used legacy (pre-1.2) inferior Crown of Thorns that have lower ES roll (12-24), and deliberately fated it into Martyr's Crown.
Martyr's Crown | Legacy variants | Despite pre-3.0 has a legacy ES mod that have superior mod value ( +(200-220) ) than the current values , the combined range of the two ES mods are at max 260-300 , so that using a Divine Orb on any Martyr's Crown that have not been use the orb on it after version 3.19.0, may improve the ES mod as the new combined range is +(260-355) , at a cost of have more physical damage taken from attack hit (from +5 to +25).
Martyr's Crown | Legacy variants | Double legacy items (the pre-3.0) may have collection value, however.
Martyr's Crown | Legacy variants | The infobox on the right is showing the item as if using Divine Orb , as it displays the current rollable mod value of the item, even the mod value is updated after the item is drop disabled.
Marylene's Fallacy | Infobox | Requires Level 40. +(20-30) to Intelligence. +(80-120) to Accuracy Rating +(210-240)% to Global Critical Strike Multiplier +(80-100) to Evasion Rating (10-15)% increased Light Radius 40% less Critical Strike Chance Critical Strikes have Culling Strike. "Marylene stroked the medallion at her breast before every duel and every cast of the die." - Victario, the People's Poet. Vendor Offer 4x Alchemy Shard 10x Alteration Shard
Marylene's Fallacy | Description | Marylene's Fallacy is a unique Lapis Amulet.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | Marylene's Fallacy is rather straightforward in what it does; a massive amount of critical strike multiplier in exchange for 40% less critical strike chance.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | Note the less critical strike chance modifier will affect effects that override base critical strike chance like Trypanon , but effects that always causes a crit such as in Lightning Tendrils and Plume of Pursuit won't be, therefore negating the item's downsides. Actum also does not receive less critical strike chance.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | The breakeven point in critical strike multiplier for a character to match the increased damage granted by Marylene's Fallacy alone at an equal critical strike chance is at a critical strike multiplier of 274% (150% base + 124% additional critical strike multiplier).
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | In every case where your critical strike multiplier before equipping Marylene's Fallacy is higher than 460% (150% base + 310% additional) using Marylene's Fallacy is a loss of opportunity as you have to invest massively into critical strike chance. Unless you are overcapped on crit chance prior to equipping Marylene's Fallacy, its use will decrease overall damage.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | This, however is changed when Critical Strike Luck from Dance with Death is applied.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | With a base 100% critical strike chance, the critical strike chance with Marylene's Fallacy is 84%, following the Luck formula.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | C r i t C h a n c e L u c k y = 2 × C r i t C h a n c e D e f a u l t − C r i t C h a n c e D e f a u l t 2 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}CritChance_{Lucky}=2\times CritChance_{Default}-{CritChance_{Default}}^{2}
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | (2*0.6)-(0.6^2)=0.84=84%
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | Marylene's Fallacy combined with Luck is most effective when your pre-Marylene's critical strike chance is between 50%-80%.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | As the tables below show, using Luck is extremely beneficial for Marylene's Fallacy, but tapers of as critical strike chances approaches 100% or when your other sources of Critical Strike Multiplier is high.
Marylene's Fallacy | Mechanics | Unstable Catalyst can further improve the Crit Multi at the cost of even less crit chance.

Midnight Bargain | Description | Midnight Bargain is a unique Calling Wand.
Midnight Bargain | Mechanics | Cannot be used with Chaos Inoculation : A character with Chaos Inoculation allocated cannot equip Midnight Bargian. If Midnight Bargain is already equipped, it will become disabled once Chaos Inoculation is allocated.
Midnight Bargain | Mechanics | Reserves 30% of Life : Equipping Midnight Bargain reserves 30% of the character's life. Since the reservation is not originated from a skill, the amount of life reserved this way cannot be reduced by bonuses that increase the (life) reservation efficiency of skills.
Midnight Bargain | Item acquisition | Midnight Bargain can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Might and Influence | Description | Might and Influence is a unique Viridian Jewel. It is a threshold jewel that enhances Dual Strike and its variants.
Might and Influence | Mechanics | As a threshold jewel, the full effect of Might and Influence can be gained only when it is placed in a jewel socket that meets the threshold requirement.
Might and Influence | Mechanics | Each Might and Influence jewel has the Attack Damage modifier and two random weapon-specific modifiers. A Divine Orb will reroll the mod values of these modifiers, but will not change which weapon type are on the jewel.
Might and Influence | Mechanics | Note that the mace modifier also affects sceptres.
Might and Influence | Item acquisition | Might and Influence is drop disabled.
Might and Influence | Legacy variants | Might and Influence has a legacy variant.

Might of the Meek | Description | Might of the Meek is a unique Crimson Jewel.

Militant Faith | Description | Militant Faith is a unique Timeless Jewel.
Militant Faith | Mechanics | Militant Faith adds +5 to Devotion to small passive skill nodes within the jewel radius. Small Attribute travel nodes (+10 to Str / Dex / Int ) are transformed into +10 to Devotion.
Militant Faith | Mechanics | Notable passive skills in radius either receive a +5 to Devotion , or the notable is completely replaced. New Militant Faith notables require at least 150 total Devotion for their effects to activate. Current Devotion total is displayed in the "Misc" tab of the character stat sheet.
Militant Faith | List of passive skills | The following variants of Militant Faith will transform (in-game wording "Conquered") any Keystones within the radius into the following new Keystones:
Militant Faith | List of passive skills | The following passive skill nodes are possible results of transformation.
Militant Faith | Jewel modifiers | Militant Faith has two of these modifiers.
Militant Faith | Item acquisition | Militant Faith has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Can drop from the Templar legion in the Domain of Timeless Conflict. Extremely rarely drops from Cardinal Sanctus Vox in zones.

Militant Faith | Legacy variants | Legacy jewel with the value Venarius would grant the keystone The Agnostic instead. However, even the jewel was obtained before version 3.11.0 , using a Divine Orb , the player cannot obtain Venarius variant anymore. Moreover, using a Divine Orb on legacy jewel Venarius variant, will delete the "Venarius" mod value forever.

Mind of the Council | Description | Mind of the Council is a unique Harlequin Mask. It was introduced in prophecy leagues and subsequently became a drop from core mechanics.
Mind of the Council | Mechanics and interactions | Mind Over Matter modifiers stack additively with the 30% of Lightning Damage is taken from Mana before Life modifier, but only for lightning damage. Taking damage as lightning can be used to shift damage to mana with this modifier.
Mind of the Council | Mechanics and interactions | The help text of +20% chance to be Shocked incorrectly states that Shock increases Damage taken by 15% for 2 seconds. This modifier only grants a chance to be shocked; the magnitude of shock depends on the damage taken from a hit.
Mind of the Council | Legacy item only | Shocks inflicted on enemies trigger the Recover 3% of Maximum Mana when you Shock an Enemy modifier. [1] This is applied once for each enemy shocked, not per cast or attack. The you specifier means that the player must inflict the shock themself.

Look the storm in the eye and you will have its respect. Vendor Offer 14x Alchemy Shard 8x Alteration Shard. Weapon DPS Physical: (160.4-196.1) Phys+Chaos: (160.4-196.1) Total: (160.4-196.1)
Mjölner | Description | Mjölner is a unique Gavel.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Cooldown: When an attack made with Mjölner hits an enemy, socketed lightning spells will be triggered without costing mana , unlike trigger gem which cost mana. The base cooldown of this triggering effect is 250ms, and sources of increased Cooldown Recovery Rate will affect it.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Multiple socketed lightning spells will trigger sequentially, starting with the socket on the top left. The order of the triggered spells is preserved even if a previous spell is already off cooldown. Multiple copies of the same spell share a cooldown. NOTE! This applies on multiple copies of the same spell when dual-wielding, even if they are in different Mjölners.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Lightning Conduit 's This Spell's Cast Time is added to its Cooldown if Triggered modifier adds the cast time to the base 250ms cooldown. Modifiers to cast speed (including Faster Casting Support ) will affect the additional cooldown; however, the base cooldown remains the same. Sources of cooldown recovery rate will affect the final sum of the cooldowns.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Dual wielding: While dual wielding , socketed lightning spells will only cast when a hit is made with the Mjölner they are socketed in.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Maximizing DPS: If only one Mjölner is being worn an attack rate of 3.79 attacks per second is optimal to maximize triggers. For non-crit builds Resolute Technique can be allocated if a sufficient accuracy rating cannot be easily reached.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Achieving a high DPS with Mjölner is hard, since the triggered spell can only be supported by 2 support gems. Consider using other sources of more damage, going crit to amplify your damage output, or using The Squire to add more support gems.
Mjölner | Mechanics | Foulborn: A Foulborn Mjölner can grant the skill Lightning Bolt.
Mjölner | Item acquisition | Mjölner can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Mjölner | Recipes | Mjölner can be created from the following recipes:
Mjölner | Recipes | Mjölner can also be used in Cartographer's Chisel 's vendor recipe.
Mjölner | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Mjölner | Legacy variants | Mjölner has legacy variants.
Mjölner | Design attribution | Mjölner was created by supporter Mizzajl. [1]
Mjölner | Item skins | The following item skins apply only to Mjölner:

Mokou's Embrace | Description | Mokou's Embrace is a unique Ruby Ring.
Mokou's Embrace | Mechanics | All Damage Taken from Hits can Ignite you : This modifier causes damaging hits taken from monsters, environment, etc. from non-fire damage types to contribute towards ignite damage applied to your character and causes all damage types to apply ignite on critical strike.
Mokou's Embrace | Mechanics | Combined with the +25% Chance to be Ignited modifier, sources of self-damage hits can consistently trigger ignites on the character. Additional chance can be obtained via Replica Reckless Defence. Note that reflected damage (e.g. Hiltless ) can never inflict ailments.
Mokou's Embrace | Mechanics | Having a source of Unaffected by Ignite is necessary to negate the ignite's significant damage over time if the character is unable to overcome it with damage mitigation and recovery.
Mokou's Embrace | Item acquisition | Mokou's Embrace can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Moonsorrow | Description | Moonsorrow is a unique Kinetic Wand.
Moonsorrow | Mechanics | The implicit modifier, Cannot roll Caster Modifiers KineticWandImplicit Intrinsic Cannot roll Caster Modifiers , has a hidden stat bonus that that increases the magnitude of caster modifiers by 1%. [1] Removing this implicit modifier by replacing it with a corruption implicit modifier will also remove the hidden bonus.
Moonsorrow | Mechanics | The modifier 10% chance to Blind enemies on hit is a local mod, therefore it only applies to hits from attacks with the weapon.
Moonsorrow | Mechanics | Applicable skill gems socketed in this item are supported by level 20 Blind Support. All support stats are accounted for, including the cost and reservation multiplier.

Mystic Refractor | Description | Mystic Refractor is a unique Pagan Wand.
Mystic Refractor | Mechanics | Projectiles cannot continue after colliding with targets : Projectiles cannot Split, Pierce, Fork, Chain, or Return if they collide with a target.
Mystic Refractor | Mechanics | This disables and prevents all regular projectile behaviours , even if the behaviour would always apply, such as Projectiles Pierce all Targets. This downside has no effect on projectiles that do not continue on collision or do not collide, such as Forbidden Rite , Creeping Frost , or Winter Orb. Most "mortar" type skills share this interaction.

Nebulis | Description | Nebulis is a unique synthesised Void Sceptre. The fractured version is Nebulis.
Nebulis | Modifiers | These modifers do not refer to uncapped or overcapped resistances and instead refer to your capped resistances (up to your maximum resistances). This value is limited by the global 90% max resistance cap, meaning each element can have up to 15% Fire/Cold/Lightning Resistance above 75%, for a total of 45% resistances above 75%. Getting maximum resistances beyond 90% does not further increase the cap, but can provide a buffer against debuffs or stats that reduce your maximum resistances.

Null's Inclination | Description | Null's Inclination is a unique Ranger Bow.
Null's Inclination | Mechanics | Trigger Socketed Minion Spells on Kill with this Weapon : Killing an enemy with Null's Inclination will trigger any minion spell skill gems socketed into it. The enemy must be killed by a hit from Null's Inclination; the minion spells will not trigger if the enemy dies to damage over time.
Null's Inclination | Mechanics | Multiple Spells : Each socketed spell is independent: one kill can trigger multiple spells, and all spells can recover their cooldown(s) independently. However, the number of cooldowns stored is tracked per spell, not per gem, so socketing multiple copies of the same gem will trigger the spell twice, and use two cooldowns to do so.
Null's Inclination | Mechanics | Targeting : Triggered ground-targeted AOE spells (e.g. Absolution ) will target the position where the enemy died, rather than any nearby enemies. Triggered corpse-targeted spells will trigger on the corpse of the killed monster, and will occur before the corpse is consumed by other on-kill effects. If there are multiple copies of a corpse-targeted spell triggered at the same time, and the first consumes the corpse, latter triggers will target the nearest other corpse.
Null's Inclination | Mechanics | Cooldowns : Skills which already have a cooldown (e.g. Summon Flame Golem ) retain that longer cooldown and do not store more than 1 use. They are still triggered on-kill.
Null's Inclination | Item acquisition | Null's Inclination can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

Null's Inclination | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.

Oba's Cursed Trove | Description | Oba's Cursed Trove is a unique Underground Sea Map.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Description | Oba's Cursed Trove is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | This map contains the initial spawn room and six sequentially connected areas, with the first two using the Submerged Passage tileset , the 3rd and 4th using the Crypt tileset , and the last two the Chamber tileset. Each of the first 5 areas contains monsters. Each area must be fully cleared to make the exit to the next area enterable, excluding spiders that spawn in the Crypt tileset areas. The final area contains no monsters and instead contains five Strongboxes :
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | Additionally, the area level increases by 1 each time the player enters the next area, up to +5 in the last room. The map is one of the few unique maps that can potentially reach an area level of 84 (or higher) which is important for finding item level 86 base types, the others being The Coward's Trial , Uber Cortex or Replica Cortex.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | Area becomes fatal after some time : Entering this map starts a timer of 9 minutes when alone, or 6 minutes when entering as a group. After this timer expires, Oba will apply the Doomed debuff and begin a countdown in sync with the debuff timer. This debuff applies a flaming horned skull effect to the player's head, causing the player's head to explode when the timer reaches 0, instantly killing the player. Leaving or dying then re-entering the map will cause immediately apply the debuff again as soon as Grace Period is removed. The debuff timer can be desynced from the chat countdown, but will not affect the time it takes to actually kill the player. The debuff can expire without killing the player if the player enters grace period again (e.g. going through a door), but will be reapplied with the correct remaining duration after grace period is removed. Modifiers to expiry rate will affect the debuff itself, and will reapply if the debuff expires faster than the countdown.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | The player also receives several dialogues, four warnings, and a countdown at set intervals (solo/party):
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | After successfully completing each area up to the fourth area, Oba says: Proceed, exile, and seize what you can, while you can.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Layout | After successfully completing the fifth area, Oba says: Haha! You are a mighty adventurer indeed. Use what I have given you and spread my legacy throughout all of Wraeclast. The timer is then disabled, and the player is free to open the strongboxes or backtrack the map at their leisure.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Item acquisition | Oba's Cursed Trove can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Recipes | Oba's Cursed Trove can be created from the following recipes:
Oba's Cursed Trove | Legacy variants | Oba's Cursed Trove has legacy variants.
Oba's Cursed Trove | Legacy variants | Selling the legacy map to a vendor NPC, will returns a unique map from the current map series AND in the base tier (e.g. T14 may change to the tier to the base tier of Underground Sea Map of the current map series). The legacy mod Monster Level: 79 may carry over, however.

Olmec's Sanctum | Description | Olmec's Sanctum is a unique Bone Crypt Map.
Olmec's Sanctum | Description | Olmec's Sanctum is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Olmec's Sanctum | Modifiers | The Final Boss drops higher Level Items mod means that Olmec, the All Stone drops items with +4 item level. This means in a 81 level (T14) version of the map those items will have level 87 (+2 for being unique map boss, +4 from the mod). The other monsters follow the regular drop rules.
Olmec's Sanctum | Modifiers | Despite atlas reshuffle, this map can drop as T16 since version 3.17.0 (mostly when all 4 voidstones socketed). However, since there is no modifier that required item level higher than 86, there is no point to change the map area level beyond 80 (T13).
Olmec's Sanctum | Modifiers | There is one more area modifier that is not showing in the map item: Area has increased monster variety MapMoreMonsterPackVarietyOlmecs_ Intrinsic Area has increased monster variety.
Olmec's Sanctum | Bosses | The map is shaped like an X, with each wing resembling a type of damage. At the end of each wing there is a boss room with a boss totem. When the boss is killed, the player goes back to the middle of the X by clicking the object and enter the portal. The player can then travel to another wing. When the fourth portal is activated, the final boss, Olmec, the All Stone , appears in the middle of the map and the doors close for the duration of the fight. He is a totem as well, dealing all forms of damage. The four previous totems also reappear and fight by his side, and will respawn if killed before Olmec falls. To fully complete the map, a minimum of 5 bosses have to be defeated.
Olmec's Sanctum | Bosses | The final boss will not appear if one of the side portals is not activated, even if all four side bosses are killed. This can happen if a player returns to town by logging out and back in from a boss room without activating the portal. Simply going back to the boss room and using the portal will make Olmec appear.
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | Each arm first has several rooms with elementally themed monsters:
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | Lightning:
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | Fire:
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | Cold:
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | Chaos:
Olmec's Sanctum | Monsters | When Olmec spawns in the central room, monsters from any of these groups can spawn with him. Additionally, monsters spawned by strongboxes can come from any of the above regardless of which arm the box is in.
Olmec's Sanctum | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Olmec's Sanctum | Item acquisition | Olmec's Sanctum can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Olmec's Sanctum | Recipes | Olmec's Sanctum can be created from the following recipes:
Olmec's Sanctum | Design attribution | Olmec's Sanctum was created by supporter tpapp157. [1]
Olmec's Sanctum | Design attribution | The design of Olmec's Sanctum was influenced by the adventure game show Legends of the Hidden Temple , which aired on Nickelodeon in the 1990s. The flavour text makes reference to the show's six teams, in order: Purple Parrots, Red Jaguars, Green Monkeys, Orange Iguanas, Blue Barracudas, and Silver Snakes.
Olmec's Sanctum | Legacy variants | Olmec's Sanctum has legacy variants.

Oni-Goroshi | Description | Oni-Goroshi is a unique Charan's Sword.
Oni-Goroshi | Mechanics | Uses both hand slots : Oni-Goroshi does not allow a shield or another one-handed weapon to be equipped in the off-hand slot. However, it is still considered a one-handed weapon, and does not count as a two-handed weapon for most purposes ( Energy Blade treats it as two-handed).
Oni-Goroshi | Mechanics | Gain Her Embrace for 3 seconds when you Ignite an Enemy : Oni-Goroshi grants Her Embrace for three seconds when the character ignites an enemy, as long as Oni-Goroshi remains equipped. Unequipping Oni-Goroshi or switching to a different weapon will immediately remove Her Embrace. Her Embrace is a buff that grants the following effects:
Oni-Goroshi | Mechanics | While in Her Embrace, take 0.5% of your total Maximum Life and Energy Shield as Fire Damage per second per Level : Damage taken is based on the player character's combined maximum life and energy shield , multiplied by their level. For example, a level 90 character with 4500 life and 1500 energy shield will take 2700 fire damage per second by default. This damage can be mitigated normally, for example with fire resistance or damage reduction from endurance charges. It is not an ignite. The damage can be stopped by swapping to a different weapon, though this also removes Her Embrace.
Oni-Goroshi | Mechanics | Item has 6 Sockets and is fully linked (Hidden) : Oni-Goroshi has a hidden mod that grants six linked sockets , ignoring item level and the socket limit of one-handed items.
Oni-Goroshi | Item acquisition | Oni-Goroshi has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Oni-Goroshi can only be obtained as a guaranteed drop from Uber Hillock , or by turning in a set of 27 Rebirth divination cards.
Oni-Goroshi | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Oni-Goroshi | Recipes | Oni-Goroshi can be created from the following recipes:
Oni-Goroshi | Ruthless mode | This item cannot be obtained in leagues with Ruthless mode enabled.

Oskarm | Description | Oskarm is a pair of unique Nubuck Gloves.
Oskarm | Mechanics | Corrupting the gloves may grant one of the vaal implicit modifiers Curse Enemies with < Hex > on Hit in order to dual curse the enemy on hit. However, the player must find a way to increase the curse limit, such as Whispers of Doom or Cospri's Will. If the character's curse limit is the default of 1, the implicit curse is applied second and therefore takes priority.
Oskarm | Mechanics | Trigger Level 10 Assassin's Mark when you Hit a Rare or Unique Enemy : See Assassin's Mark.
Oskarm | Mechanics | Assassin's Mark triggered by Oskarm has a cooldown of 0.5 seconds. It cannot apply Assassin's Mark if any monster in the area is currently already marked by you. [1]

Paradoxica | Description | Paradoxica is a unique Vaal Rapier.
Paradoxica | Damage Scaling | Paradoxica has double damage from the weapon's total flat damage. When combined with large amounts of flat added physical damage from non-weapon sources, it can scale damage competitive with high physical damage one-handed rare swords. Some examples of non-weapon sources of flat added physical are:
Paradoxica | Ailments | Double damage only applies to hit damage, not base damage - it does not double damage over time.
Paradoxica | Veiled modifiers | Paradoxica has two veiled modifiers :
Paradoxica | Veiled prefix | Unveiling a Veiled prefix draws from a pool of generic veiled prefixes ( Chosen ).
Paradoxica | Veiled suffix | Unveiling an of the Veil suffix draws from a pool of generic veiled suffixes ( of the Order ).
Paradoxica | Item acquisition | Paradoxica has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from executed Safehouse leaders in Intervention Safehouses. Only obtainable from Safehouses of area level 78 or higher.
Paradoxica | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Paradoxica | Item skins | The following item skins apply only to Paradoxica:
Paradoxica | Ruthless mode | Although veiled rare items cannot be found in Ruthless mode , this item will still drop with veiled modifiers and can be unveiled.
Paradoxica | Legacy variants | Paradoxica has legacy variants.
Paradoxica | Legacy variants | Read List of thrusting one-handed sword modifiers for a list of possible crafted (un)veiled mods. Note that player cannot swap the crafted mod of the legacy item. Player can remove the mod since it is crafted mod, but player CANNOT craft any mod back to this unique item, effectively brick the item. The current version is not a crafted mod thus cannot be bricked by removing the mod.
Paradoxica | Legacy variants | The following legacy (post 3.6 pre-3.14) crafted veiled mod of this item, have numeric value adjusted:
Paradoxica | Legacy variants | The other crafted mods are identical to the current numeric value of crafted mods, but may still inferior to the current non-crafted version of (un)veiled mods.

Pillar of the Caged God | Mechanics | Damage Scaling
Pillar of the Caged God | Mechanics | Strength - Pillar of the caged god can scale increased physical damage modifiers to much higher levels than most builds by having large amounts of strength. Pillar of the Caged God | Design attribution | The flavour text suggests that the staff was "destined to serve the monkey's paw". This draws parallels to the Ruyi Jingu Bang , a magical staff wielded by Sun Wukong , a figure of classical Chinese literature who was trapped for 500 years by Buddha in the story Journey to the West. (A "Caged God".)

Pillars of Arun | Description | Pillars of Arun is a unique Dunes Map.
Pillars of Arun | Description | Pillars of Arun is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Pillars of Arun | Layout | Pillars of Arun is a series of platforms populated by monsters , separated by smaller platforms. Players must use a movement skill (i.e. Flame Dash or Lightning Warp ) to cross between platforms. Each small platform also intermittently spawns a whirlwind which will damage the player while remaining on the small platform.
Pillars of Arun | Layout | Each large platform is surrounded by a proximity shield , so it's not possible to kill the monsters before being on the platform, unless you have a proxy (eg. totem ) to deal damage. Because of this, players should expect to get hit when arriving on each platform.
Pillars of Arun | Layout | The Boss has a chance to drop Gorgon's Gaze.
Pillars of Arun | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Pillars of Arun | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Pillars of Arun | Item acquisition | Pillars of Arun can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Pillars of Arun | Recipes | Pillars of Arun can be created from the following recipes:
Pillars of Arun | Ruthless mode | Due to movement skills being disabled in Ruthless mode and the only viable skill for traversal being Chain Hook and Hydrosphere , this map now has an additional modifier: Grants Level 20 Dash Skill. This modifier allows players to temporarily equip Dash on their skill bar while inside the map.

Poorjoy's Asylum | Description | Poorjoy's Asylum is a unique Temple Map.
Poorjoy's Asylum | Description | Poorjoy's Asylum is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
Poorjoy's Asylum | Map Type | Indoors. Based on The Lunaris Temple Level 1 (Act 3).
Poorjoy's Asylum | Mechanics | Unique Boss drops (10-15) additional Rare <Item Class> : The <Item Class> placeholder is replaced by one of 24 possible item classes , chosen randomly: Amulets, belts, body armours, boots, bows, claws, daggers, rune daggers, gloves, helmets, one-handed axes, one-handed maces, one-handed swords, one-handed thrusting swords, quivers, rings, sceptres, shields, staves, warstaves, two-handed axes, two-handed maces, two-handed swords, and wands. [1] For example, a Poorjoy's Asylum could roll with Unique Boss drops (10-15) additional Rare Amulets , which causes the map boss, Mistress Hyseria, to drop a random assortment of rare amulets in addition to any other loot drops. A Divine Orb can be used to reroll the item class.

Pragmatism | Mechanics | This item can be used with most hybrid gems that are not primarily support gems ; Spellslinger scales particularly well due to gaining increasing cooldown recovery per level, which can then apply to other cooldown skills.
Pragmatism | Mechanics | Brand Recall also scales very well due to having a default maximum gem level of 6, providing significant scaling per additional gem level. [1] Combined with Spellslinger, this will cause Brands to activate instantly 4 times.

Precursor's Emblem | Acquisition | Precursor's Emblem can only be obtained by vendoring a unique meadow, mountain, and valley delve component ring.
Precursor's Emblem | Modifiers | The listed mods are not equally probable. Some of them are significantly less probable than others, some are so rare that they can make the item one of the most valuable unique items. Combinations of the more rare of modifiers are virtually unheard of. The rolls are independent and a mod can appear twice on one item (they will be merged into one line).

Presence of Chayula | Infobox | Requires Level 60. +(10-16) to all Attributes. 30% increased Rarity of Items found +60% to Chaos Resistance Cannot be Stunned 20% of Maximum Life Converted to Energy Shield. Presence of Chayula | Description | Presence of Chayula is a unique Onyx Amulet.
Presence of Chayula | Mechanics | Presence of Chayula's unique modifier % of Maximum Life Converted to Energy Shield behaves similarly to damage conversion. Base life , including +x to maximum Life modifiers from items, strength , and passives, is first converted to energy shield , and then % increased maximum Life and % increased maximum Energy Shield modifiers are combined additively and applied. [1] [2]
Presence of Chayula | Mechanics | If the keystone passive Chaos Inoculation is allocated, this modifier provides virtually no benefit since the player's maximum life is overwritten to 1. [3]
Presence of Chayula | Item acquisition | Presence of Chayula has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Presence of Chayula has a chance to drop from Chayula in the Flawless Breachstone version of encounter
