The Beachhead (High Tier) | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
The Beachhead (High Tier) | Item acquisition | The Beachhead is drop disabled.
The Beachhead (High Tier) | Item acquisition | This map can be acquired (by chance) if a Harbinger's Orb is used on a map between Tier 11 through Tier 15. It is also possible to rarely obtain them from other sources of Harbinger Rewards. The resulting unique map will always be corrupted.
The Beachhead (High Tier) | Item acquisition | The map is sometimes present as Kirac mission option when using Comprehensive Scouting Report.
The Beachhead (High Tier) | Legacy variants | The Beachhead (High Tier) does not have any legacy mods. The game creates a new map series in each expansion (except Delve (3.4), which still uses War for the Atlas map series, or Ultimatum (3.14), which still uses Ritual map series). There is a vendor recipe that will convert a map from an older map series to the current map series.

The Beast Fur Shawl | Description | The Beast Fur Shawl is a unique Vaal Regalia.
The Beast Fur Shawl | Modifiers | #% increased Energy Shield Recovery Rate will increase the recovery rate of energy shield.
The Beast Fur Shawl | Modifiers | This recovery rate applies to the energy shield Recharge rate, but does not reduce the delay time.
The Beast Fur Shawl | Modifiers | The recovery rate also applies to passive energy shield Regeneration when coupled with Zealot's Oath. However, this increased regeneration will not show on the character sheet, as the wording is similar to Patient Reaper. The supporter reached out to GGG and confirmed that is the case. [1] This has been further confirmed with independent testing. [2] [3] [4] [5] The original design intent by the supporter was for use in an energy shield based Righteous Fire build. [6]

The Bringer of Rain | Legacy variants | The Bringer of Rain has legacy variants.
The Bringer of Rain | Legacy variants | A Divine Orb can change the displayed gem levels of Blind and Faster Attacks for items dropped between 0.10.0 to 1.0.X, as well as between 3.5.0 to 3.18.X, but not the versions dropped between 1.1.0 to 3.4.X, as they use different mods than other versions. [1]
The Bringer of Rain | Legacy variants | Except the variants dropped between versions 1.1.0 to 3.4.X, all legacy Bringer of Rain grant the same actual gem level as current variant (level 30) for all 3 supports, as it is only the mod text are legacy, and does not affect the granted support gem level itself.
The Bringer of Rain | Design attribution | The Bringer of Rain was created by supporter Prpnnightmare. [2] [3] [4]
The Bringer of Rain | Design attribution | "Bringer of Rain" is a nickname given to a Greek-Thracian gladiator named Spartacus , [4] [5] in the TV-series of the same name. The flavor text is a quote [6] by Crixus from S1E2.
The Bringer of Rain | Design attribution | The 2D art of the unique item was created by GGG illustrator Filippo Valsecchi, [4] who also prevously did concept art for the Spartacus series.
The Bringer of Rain | Alternate artwork | It also has Voidborn foil variants as well as Celestial foil variants obtainable from Valdo's Map , such as "Festering Cramp" Arena Map and "Mobilised Zone" Colosseum Map.

The Coming Calamity | Description | The Coming Calamity is a unique Destroyer Regalia.
The Coming Calamity | Modifiers | The Mana Reservation of Herald Skills is always 45% modifier prevents the mana reservation of herald skills from being increased or decreased via passive skills, gems, or items. This allows heralds to be linked to support gems without worry of increasing the reservation value.

The Covenant | Infobox | Energy Shield: (313-409) Movement Speed: -3%. Requires Level 49 , 134 Int. Socketed Gems are Supported by Level 29 Added Chaos Damage +(20-30) to Intelligence (210-250)% increased Energy Shield 10% increased maximum Life Skills gain a Base Life Cost equal to 100% of Base Mana Cost. My Soul is your Strength My Price is your Blood. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard 11x Alteration Shard
The Covenant | Description | The Covenant is a unique Spidersilk Robe.
The Covenant | Mechanics | Applicable skill gems socketed in this item are supported by level 29 Added Chaos Damage Support. All support stats are accounted for, including the cost and reservation multiplier.
The Covenant | Mechanics | The Skills gain a Base Life cost equal to 100% of Base Mana Cost causes your skills to cost both Life and Mana to use. Eldritch Battery will allow Energy Shield to be spent instead of Mana, but will still cost Life. Blood Magic and Lifetap Support will remove the base mana cost, causing this mod to have no effect.
The Covenant | Mechanics | Whispers of Infinity 's cost conversion takes priority over The Covenant's added life cost, causing the skill to not gain any additional cost from The Covenant.
The Covenant | Item acquisition | The Covenant can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Covenant | Recipes | The Covenant can be created from the following recipes:
The Covenant | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
The Covenant | Legacy variants | The Covenant has legacy variants.
The Covenant | Legacy variants | The pre-2.6 The Covenant (plus foil version that dropped from Ancient Reliquary ) has Blood Magic mod that grant the keystone of the same name, while the later versions of the legacy items grant the now removed Blood Magic Support gem (the mod wording now known as Socketed Gems Cost and Reserve Life instead of Mana ), but with a 100% cost & reservation multiplier. Blood Magic keystone remove mana but it is global , which affects all other skill gem to cost and reserve life. While Socketed Gems Cost and Reserve Life instead of Mana is a local mod that only affects socketed gems. The price of this legacy items aside, different versions of the legacy items fit different build so that make sure to buy the right version.
The Covenant | Legacy variants | Note that a Divine Orb can usually change a mod value of the legacy item (or made a mod "hidden" if it has a new default value of "zero"), but not adding or removing a mod. So that some of the Energy Shield mod of this legacy item can be improved or make it worse by using a Divine Orb. Also, the #% reduced maximum Life can also be changed to 10% increased maximum Life by using a Divine Orb. Moreover, the dev has used a new mod id for the new Gems in this item are Supported by level # Added Chaos Damage mod, so that using a Divine Orb cannot change the supported gem from level 15 to 29. [1]
The Covenant | Legacy variants | Lastly, a Divine Orb cannot add Skills gain a Base Life Cost equal to 100% of Base Mana Cost to the item as it is an entirely new mod.
The Covenant | Design attribution | The Covenant was created by supporter Redex. [2]
The Coward's Trial | Infobox | Map Level: 74 Map Tier: 7 Guild Character : Å. Contains waves of Monsters Contains additional waves of Undead Monsters <0 to 7 random additional waves modifiers> Area contains additional waves of Ghosts Area contains additional waves of Oriathan Zombies Area contains additional waves of Phantasms Area contains additional waves of Raging Spirits Area contains additional waves of Bone Rhoas Area contains additional waves of Ravager Maws Area contains additional waves of Zombies. For his sins he was hunted in his sleep, judgement wrought of twisted nightmare. The Coward bound his dreams to stone, awaiting another to walk the path of true atonement. Travel to this Map by using it in a personal Map Device. Maps can only be used once. Vendor Offer 12x Alchemy Shard
The Coward's Trial | Description | The Coward's Trial is a unique Cursed Crypt Map.
The Coward's Trial | Description | The Coward's Trial is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
The Coward's Trial | Layout | This map contains the initial spawn room and four sequentially connected floors. Each floor must be fully cleared to spawn the stairs to the next area.
The Coward's Trial | Layout | The second, third, and fourth floors are inspired by the tilesets of the Crypt , the Temple of Atzoatl / Vaal Outpost , and Abyssal Depths / Abyssal City respectively.
The Coward's Trial | Mechanics | Coward's Trial does not benefit from map quality and will not grant additional item quantity. The map can still benefit from the Map Device 's item quantity mod.
The Coward's Trial | Mechanics | The area level increases by 1 each time the player enters the next area starting from the second floor, up to +3 in the last room. The map is one of the few unique maps that can potentially reach an area level of 84 (or higher) which is important for finding item level 86 base types, the others being Oba's Cursed Trove , Uber Cortex and Replica Cortex.
The Coward's Trial | Mechanics | Each floor contains several waves of monsters, with additional waves of specific types of monsters depending on the modifiers on the map itself. The number of magic and rare monsters increase with every wave, and at the end of every wave a rare Necromancer spawns [1]. [ confirmation needed because of 3.7.0 changes ]. The final wave is the exception to this, where Infector of Dreams is accompanied by monster types that can only appear during the final wave.
The Coward's Trial | Mechanics | Several flavour/hint messages appear in the chat window during the map:
The Coward's Trial | Mechanics | Except the mods Contains waves of Monsters and Contains additional waves of Undead Monsters , the rest of the mods are "optional" which can be rerolled with a Divine Orb to switch them "on" or "off" (1 or 0), or dropped naturally as "on" or "off". Thus, the map can have zero additional waves except the base waves of Monsters and waves of Undead Monsters , or have all 7 optional waves ( Ghosts , Oriathan Zombies , Phantasms , Raging Spirits , Bone Rhoas , Ravager Maws and Zombies respectively). There are 2^7 = 128 combinations of map mods. The chance to have zero optional waves or all 7 optional waves, were 1/128 respectively, assuming equal chance to roll the mod value as 0 and roll as 1.
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | In the last sub-area, the map contains several Abyss themed monsters:
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | Other monsters of the map:
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | Note that only the last sub-area contain monsters that leave corpses.
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
The Coward's Trial | Item drops | Infector of Dreams will always drop an additional random item level 100 unique item , regardless of map level. [2] [3]
The Coward's Trial | Item acquisition | The Coward's Trial can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Coward's Trial | Recipes | The Coward's Trial can be created from the following recipes:
The Coward's Trial | Legacy variants | The Coward's Trial has legacy variants.
The Coward's Trial | Legacy variants | Pre- 3.7.0 legacy variants use the mods WaveTimeUndeadSiege and NumWavesUndeadSiege instead. They function the same as the current variant if using Divine Orb to reroll the item. The additional "optional" waves (Ghosts, Oriathan Zombies, Phantasms, Raging Spirits, Bone Rhoas, Ravager Maws and Zombies respectively) are default as "off" ("0") in the legacy variant, however.
The Coward's Trial | Legacy variants | The legacy version has hidden values in the mods: (30-50) for Contains additional waves of Undead Monsters and (22-25) for Contains waves of Monsters , while the new version of the mods, Contains waves of Monsters DisplayCowardsTrialWavesOfMonsters Intrinsic Contains waves of Monsters and Contains additional waves of Undead Monsters DisplayCowardsTrialWavesOfUndeadMonsters Intrinsic Contains additional waves of Undead Monsters , does not have numeric value other than 1. It is purely a cosmetic, as the value (30-50) which was used to be for Area has (30-50) seconds between monster waves and the value (22-25) was used to be for Area has (22-25) waves of monsters , which both mod texts (and stat id behind) were converted to match new version due to 3.7.0 change, but the numeric value were retained. These values are still displayed in the official trade site search result as a relic. Such values will be "deleted" by using Divine Orb and does not affect the function of the map, but the orb will most likely add more waves to a legacy map.
The Coward's Trial | Legacy variants | Player can sell the legacy item to a NPC in exchange for a new "The Coward's Trial" map from the current map series, but retain the aforementioned legacy mods.
The Coward's Trial | Design attribution | The Coward's Trial was created by supporter OverUsedChewToy. [2]

The Crimson Storm | Description | The Crimson Storm is a unique Steelwood Bow.
The Crimson Storm | Mechanics | Adds (100-120) to (150-165) Physical Damage against Bleeding Enemies is a conditional mod, and can therefore add Physical Damage to spells and heralds , provided that the target is bleeding (from an attack).
The Crimson Storm | Veiled modifiers | The Crimson Storm has a veiled modifier :
The Crimson Storm | Veiled modifiers | Unveiling an of the Veil suffix draws from a pool of generic veiled suffixes ( of the Order ).

The Dancing Dervish | Infobox | Two Hand Sword Physical Damage: (142.6-161.2) to (239.2-270.4) Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Attacks per Second: (1.88-1.95) Weapon Range: 1.3 metres. Weapon DPS Physical: (357.9-420.8) Phys+Chaos: (357.9-420.8) Total: (357.9-420.8)
The Dancing Dervish | Description | The Dancing Dervish is a unique Reaver Sword.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | The Dancing Dervish grants level 15 Manifest Dancing Dervishes. Manifest Dancing Dervishes will be supported by any applicable support gems socketed into The Dancing Dervish; it is not necessary to have the sockets be linked together. Manifest Dancing Dervishes will spawn upon reaching 15 consecutive Rampage Kills. The manifested dervishes do not deactivate when swapping to different weapons, however, the weapon slots containing The Dancing Dervish will remain disabled while the Dervishes are active.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | While The Dancing Dervishes are manifested, the player character is considered unarmed and gains Onslaught , therefore effects such as Facebreaker 's, Doryani's Fist 's, or Hollow Palm Technique 's unarmed damage bonus will take effect. As soon as Rampage ends, the manifested weapons will vanish and the character reequips the weapon, re-enabling the weapon slots.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | Melee Hits count as Rampage Kills : This modifier applies only to hits with The Dancing Dervish. It does not apply to the dancing dervish minions. [1]
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | The Writhing Jar , Vaal Breach etc. can be used to sustain Rampage while there are no nearby enemies to kill (or hit), or if the only enemies are invulnerable, such as during certain boss immunity phases. Due to the "Melee Hits count as Rampage Kills" modifier it is possible gain two Rampage Kills per enemy, if you kill the enemy with a melee hit. This includes the worms from The Writhing Jar that are destroyed on hit.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | The minions themselves have 95% less damage taken; it is possible for the minions to get damaged, however they are unlikely to die as long as they have even just a small amount of regen or leech. [2] They can however die from engaging with large packs of enemies in a map with damage reflection , or from being consumed by Summon Reaper. If one or both dervishes are killed, they cannot be re-summoned until rampage is gained again. These minions are damageable and can be targeted by Link skills if the " Link Skills can target Damageable Minions " Minion Defence Mastery node is allocated. Linked players do not die when these minions die due to Rampage's effect ending, despite the item's wording.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | The minions also have a 25% chance to grant the player a Rampage Kill when hitting Rare or Unique enemies.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | Dancing dervish minions are considered manifested minions. Some effects refer to manifested minions, such as those granted by Chains of Command. Dancing dervishes are not considered animated weapons.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | The T17 Map modifier Rare and Unique Monsters in Area are Possessed by X to X Tormented Spirits and their Minions are Touched found in some Valdo's Puzzle Box maps causes the Dancing Dervish minions to be possessed and, depending on the random Grip modifiers it receives, can greatly increase the minions' damage output.
The Dancing Dervish | Mechanics | All modifiers on the The Dancing Dervish item itself apply to the minions once manifested, including enchantments , corrupted implicit modifiers , Scourge modifiers , and Crucible passive trees (except modifiers which allocate player notable passive skills , as minions do not have access to your passive tree).
The Dancing Dervish | Item acquisition | The Dancing Dervish can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Dancing Dervish | Recipes | The Dancing Dervish can be created from the following recipes:
The Dancing Dervish | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
The Dancing Dervish | Design attribution | The Dancing Dervish was created by Grinding Gear Games programmer Rhys. [3]
The Dancing Dervish | Legacy variants | The Dancing Dervish has legacy variants.
The Dancing Duo | Infobox | Two Hand Sword Physical Damage: (142.6-161.2) to (239.2-270.4) Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Attacks per Second: (1.88-1.95) Weapon Range: 1.3 metres. Requires Level 65 , 82 Str, 119 Dex. 60% increased Global Accuracy Rating. (130-160)% increased Physical Damage (25-30)% increased Attack Speed 5% increased Movement Speed Triggers Level 15 Manifest Dancing Dervishes on Rampage Manifested Dancing Dervishes disables both weapon slots Manifested Dancing Dervishes die when Rampage ends Melee Hits count as Rampage Kills Rampage. One more partner by the hand, Let your steps entwine. Spin together, swing as one, And dance with death in trine. Acquisition Drop disabled. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard 13x Alteration Shard. Weapon DPS Physical: (357.9-420.8) Phys+Chaos: (357.9-420.8) Total: (357.9-420.8)
The Dancing Duo | Description | The Dancing Duo is a unique Reaver Sword.
The Dancing Duo | Mechanics | The Dancing Duo grants level 15 Manifest Dancing Dervishes. Manifest Dancing Dervish will be supported by any applicable support gems socketed into The Dancing Duo; it is not necessary to have the sockets be linked together. The manifested sword does not deactivate when swapping to different weapons, however, the weapon slots remain disabled while it's active.
The Dancing Duo | Mechanics | While The Dancing Duo is manifested, the player character is considered unarmed and is affected by Onslaught , therefore effects such as Facebreaker 's, Doryani's Fist 's, or Hollow Palm Technique 's unarmed damage bonus will take effect. As soon as Rampage ends, the manifested weapons will vanish and the character reequips the weapon reenabling the weapon slots. The Minions themselves have a lot of damage reduction while active and will not die that easily. A little bit of minion life regeneration suffices to keep them alive and healthy.
The Dancing Duo | Item acquisition | The Dancing Duo is drop disabled.
The Dancing Duo | Legacy variants | Legacy modifiers and base items are retained when upgrading uniques to their fated counterparts. So because The Dancing Dervish has legacy variants, The Dancing Duo effectively does as well.

The Druggery | Description | The Druggery is a unique Cloth Belt.
The Druggery | Mechanics | 100% of Life Recovery from Flasks is applied to nearby Allies instead of You - This modifier makes characters heal nearby allies with life flasks , at the cost of them not working on the user.
The Druggery | Mechanics | Flask Behavior - Life flasks that normally stop working at full health, will work all the time with the druggery equipped.
The Druggery | Mechanics | Radius - The radius that life flasks can heal nearby allies is 7.5m. [1]
The Druggery | Interactions | The healing applied to allies is affected by your Life Flask Recovery Rate (e.g. Belly of the Beast ), your Life Recovery from Flasks (e.g. Bloodgrip ), your Flask Effect modifiers, and allies' Recovery Rate modifiers. Conversely the healing is not affected by allies' Life Flask Recovery Rate, allies' Life Recovery from Flasks, allies' Flask Effect, or your Recovery rate modifiers.
The Druggery | Interactions | Allies using The Sorrow of the Divine or Supreme Decadence will also recover Energy Shield, alongside life.
The Druggery | Interactions | Blood of the Karui 's " Recover Full Life at the end of the Effect LocalFlaskLifeOnFlaskDurationEndUniqueFlask3 Intrinsic Recover Full Life at the end of the Effect " is applied to you, not to nearby Allies.
The Druggery | Interactions | Master Surgeon causes the flask's healing to not be removed when the allies' life reaches full. Without this, the flask's healing ends for the ally as soon as their life reaches full.
The Druggery | Interactions | Allies with Petrified Blood can recover above half from flasks used by you as normal.
The Druggery | Item acquisition | The Druggery can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Druggery | Recipes | The Druggery can be created from the following recipes:
The Druggery | Design attribution | The Druggery was created by supporter rz2yoj. [1]

Simply the way it must be, so that existence may be. Acquisition Drop restricted. Vendor Offer 4x Alchemy Shard 9x Alteration Shard
The Eternal Struggle | Description | The Eternal Struggle is a unique Onyx Amulet.
The Eternal Struggle | Mechanics | The Eternal Struggle drops with one eldritch implicit modifier from each of the Eater of Worlds and Searing Exarch pools; see List of modifiers for amulets § Eldritch implicit modifiers for the full list. One of the eldritch implicit modifiers will always be either Grand or Exceptional, and the other will always be either Lesser or Greater (all four combinations are possible). If the item is dropped by The Black Star , then the Searing Exarch implicit modifier will be a higher tier than the Eater of Worlds implicit, and vice versa if it is dropped by The Infinite Hunger. [1] These modifiers cannot be re-rolled using Eldritch Embers or Eldritch Ichors.
The Eternal Struggle | Mechanics | Critical Strikes inflict Malignant Madness if The Eater of Worlds is dominant : If the Eater of Worlds implicit modifier is a higher tier than the Searing Exarch implicit modifier, then the character's critical strikes will always inflict Malignant Madness.
The Eternal Struggle | Mechanics | Kill Enemies that have 15% or lower Life on Hit if The Searing Exarch is dominant : If the Searing Exarch implicit modifier is a higher tier than the Eater of Worlds implicit modifier, then hits with character's skills will kill enemies that are left with 15% of maximum life or less. This functions similarly to culling strike.
The Eternal Struggle | Item acquisition | The Eternal Struggle has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
The Eternal Struggle | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
The Eternal Struggle | Design attribution | The Eternal Struggle was created in a collaboration with Dsfarblarwaggle as a reward for taking a prize place in the 3.17 boss kill event. [2] [3]

The Flawed Refuge | Description | The Flawed Refuge is a unique Maple Round Shield.
The Flawed Refuge | Mechanics | You take 100% of Elemental Damage from Blocked Hits : This modifier is additive with other effects that cause you to take a percentage of damage from blocked hits, such as Module Error: Too many passives found with q_where = passive_skills.name="Glancing Blows". With Glancing Blows and The Flawed Refuge, you take 165% of elemental damage from blocked hits, or 65% more damage than an unblocked hit.
The Flawed Refuge | Mechanics | 40% of Elemental Damage from Hits taken as Physical Damage : Combined with Divine Flesh , 90% of elemental damage from hits is taken as physical or chaos damage. This allows for skipping gearing for elemental resistance modifiers for hits and instead use Doppelgänger Guise more effectively. Veil of the Night can also be used to set negative resistances to 0 to help mitigate damage over time , half of which is already taken as chaos damage. [1]
The Flawed Refuge | Item acquisition | The Flawed Refuge has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from enemies native to the Viridian Wildwood or Ritual encounters.

The Fledgling | Description | The Fledgling is a unique Lacquered Helmet.
The Fledgling | Mechanics | Far Shot : Projectile Attack Hits deal 20% less Damage to targets at the start of their movement, dealing up to 60% more Damage to targets as the projectile travels farther
The Fledgling | Mechanics | The Far Shot cannot stack with other source of the modifiers such as the Lioneye's Glare unique bow.
The Fledgling | Mechanics | Projectiles cannot collide with Enemies in Close Range : This modifier prevents collisions of projectiles before they have travelled a distance of 2 metres. This is not an area of effect: projectiles that return after travelling can collide with enemies at the original location. Area hits and effects that do not require a collision are not affected (e.g. Poisonous Concoction , Toxic Rain ).

The Goddess Bound | Design attribution | The Goddess Bound was created by supporter Charan. [1] It was originally called Tipua Kaikohuru , but renamed to the Goddess Bound to establish a connected trilogy of unique one-handed swords that could be upgraded from one to the next using complex vendor recipes. [2] The next swords in the trilogy are The Goddess Scorned and The Goddess Unleashed. However, the trilogy was broken in version 3.1.0 when the vendor recipe used to create the Goddess Unleashed was disabled. Oni-Goroshi was introduced to the game in version 3.1.1c as a replacement for the Goddess Unleashed, although it is not obtained through vendor recipes involving the other swords.

The Goddess Scorned | Description | The Goddess Scorned is a unique Elegant Sword.
The Goddess Scorned | Modifiers | Uses both hand slots – The Goddess Scorned is a one-handed sword , but does not allow a shield or another one-handed melee weapon to be equipped in the off-hand slot.
The Goddess Scorned | Modifiers | Ignites you inflict deal Damage 50% faster – Ignited enemies burn 50% faster with the Goddess Scorned equipped. This means ignites deal 50% more fire damage per second and have ~33% less duration. Expressed arithmetically, fire damage over time from ignites is multiplied by ( 100 + 50 ) / 100 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(100+50)/100} , while ignite duration is multiplied by 100 / ( 100 + 50 ) {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}100/(100+50)}. The total fire damage inflicted by one instance of ignite remains the same after these adjustments, but the duration is shortened. [1] [2]

The Goddess Scorned | Legacy variants | The Goddess Scorned has legacy variants.
The Goddess Scorned | Design attribution | The Goddess Scorned was created by supporter Charan as the second part of a connected trilogy of unique one-handed swords that could be upgraded from one to the next using complex vendor recipes. [4] [5] The first sword in the trilogy is The Goddess Bound and the third is The Goddess Unleashed. However, the trilogy was broken in version 3.1.0 when the vendor recipe used to create the Goddess Unleashed was disabled. Oni-Goroshi was introduced to the game in version 3.1.1c as a replacement for the Goddess Unleashed, although it is not obtained through vendor recipes involving the other swords.

The Golden Charlatan | Description | The Golden Charlatan is a unique Lion Sword.
The Golden Charlatan | Mechanics | Gain a random Shrine Buff for 30 seconds when you Kill a Rare or Unique Enemy : This modifier grants a random regular shrine effect on kill, including Divine Shrine. The kill does not need to be dealt with this weapon; kills from spells or damage over time will also grant shrine buffs. Killing blows by the character 's proxies , minions , or grafts will not grant a shrine buff to the character. Animate Guardian and Animate Weapon can benefit from this modifier if this item is animated. Multiple shrine effects from this modifier can be possessed simultaneously; if the random shrine chosen is one currently already affecting the wearer, the buff duration is refreshed.
The Golden Charlatan | Mechanics | Shrine buffs can be further modified or extended by The Gull , Blunderbore , and modifiers to buff expiration rate like Runegraft of the Warp.
The Golden Charlatan | Item acquisition | The Golden Charlatan has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.

Vendor Offer 12x Alchemy Shard
The Golden Rule | Description | The Golden Rule is a unique Viridian Jewel. In patch 3.26, a sample of 576 Viridian Jewels yielded 23 The Golden Rule, or a 4.0% chance per corrupted jewel.
The Golden Rule | Mechanics | The Golden Rule is especially useful for testing player skills; with 100% chance to poison, a player can accurately measure their hits per second with a given skill by tracking their self-inflicted poison stacks.
The Golden Rule | Mechanics | The reflected poison on the player character, is not affected by the mod #% reduced Poison Duration on you found on rare / magic jewels , instead it is affected by #% increased Poison Duration. [1]
The Golden Rule | Mechanics | Similarly, if the player has allocated Crimson Dance , it is possible to self-inflict up to 8 stacks of bleed. Allocating Lethe Shade on the other hand, will not double the duration of the reflected ailment.
The Golden Rule | Mechanics | Players can use this behavior to their advantage by adding properties to the poison that will be reflected. To maximize the properties' impact, builds relying on this strategy often include Winterweave , so they can debuff the enemy and buff themselves. For example, when a build using The Golden Rule, Winterweave and Icefang Orbit poisons with a skill supported by Bonechill support the player will impart Enemies Chilled by Supported Skills increase Cold Damage taken by Chill Effect from Bonechill Support on the enemy and reverse that effect for themselves.
The Golden Rule | Mechanics | If the character using the Golden Rule does not have a way to circumvent poison damage and is relying solely on minimum damage poison stacks and recovery (e.g. to gain extra Virulence stacks for Herald of Agony ), it is important to avoid granting poison chance to explosions (e.g. Gloom shrine ) or avoid sources of explosions altogether which can reflect massive poisons deal fatal damage almost instantly.
The Golden Rule | Item acquisition | The Golden Rule has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
The Golden Rule | Recipes | The Golden Rule can be created from the following recipes:
The Golden Rule | Legacy variants | The Golden Rule has a legacy variant.
The Golden Rule | Legacy variants | Un-corrupted The Golden Rule are all legacy items and only be obtainable by trading with other players in permanent leagues. Also, corrupted The Golden Rule that have corruption implicit are also legacy.

The Harvest | Description | The Harvest is a unique Jasper Chopper.
The Harvest | Mechanics | All modifiers except (120-140)% increased Physical Damage are global and will apply to any critical strike dealt while The Harvest is equipped.
The Harvest | Mechanics | Gain a Flask Charge when you deal a Critical Strike – With the Harvest equipped, the character will gain one flask charge when dealing a critical strike , which will be assigned randomly to one of their equipped flasks. There is a global limit on how often characters can gain flask charges from critical strikes. No more than one flask charge can be gained from such effects every 100 milliseconds, which means that the character can gain up to ten flask charges from critical strikes per second.

Be careful where you put your finger. Acquisition Drop level: 70. Vendor Offer 8x Alchemy Shard
The Hungry Loop | Description | The Hungry Loop is a unique Unset Ring.
The Hungry Loop | Mechanics | The Hungry Loop consumes socketed uncorrupted support gems when they reach maximum level. Simply socketing a gem that already has the maximum level is not enough, the gem needs to be socketed in the ring before actually leveling the gem (by clicking the "+") to its maximum level for the ring consume the gem. Consequently all consumed gems can only result in a modifier of their natural maximum level; the +1 gem level effect from corruption can not be achieved since the Vaal Orb needs to be used after the gem hit its maximum level. [1]
The Hungry Loop | Mechanics | When a support gem is consumed, the Hungry Loop gains a modifier that supports socketed skill gems with the maximum level of the consumed support. Gem quality is ignored. The Hungry Loop can consume up to four different support gems. Selling The Hungry Loop to a vendor returns any consumed support gems.
The Hungry Loop | Mechanics | Awakened support gems and exceptional gems can be consumed, requiring hitting level 5/3 respectively instead of the usual level 20.
The Hungry Loop | Item acquisition | The Hungry Loop can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Hungry Loop | Recipes | The Hungry Loop can be created from the following recipes:
The Hungry Loop | Legacy variants | The Hungry Loop has a legacy variant.

The Iron Mass | Infobox | One Handed Sword Physical Damage: (148.5-162) to (214.5-234) Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Attacks per Second: (1.48-1.53) Weapon Range: 1.1 metres. Requires Level 60 , 113 Str, 113 Dex. 40% increased Global Accuracy Rating. (175-200)% increased Physical Damage (14-18)% increased Attack Speed Unholy Might Summoned Skeleton Warriors and Soldiers wield this Weapon while in your Main Hand Summoned Skeleton Warriors and Soldiers deal Triple Damage with this Weapon if you've Hit with this Weapon Recently. Weapon DPS Physical: (269.0-303.7) Phys+Chaos: (269.0-303.7) Total: (269.0-303.7)
The Iron Mass | Description | The Iron Mass is a unique Gladius.
The Iron Mass | Buff/Debuff | Recently refers to the last 4 seconds.
The Iron Mass | Interactions | All skeletons wield an exact copy of The Iron Mass and will use it to attack.
The Iron Mass | Interactions | The Hidden Blade can be used to automate the requirement of hitting with the weapon recently, as its skill is always using the main hand weapon.
The Iron Mass | Damage comparison | Summon Skeletons normally has 0.8 attacks time (1.25 APS), and gains base damage as they level up.
The Iron Mass | Damage comparison | The Iron Mass overrides the skeletons' base damage, base attack speed, and base crit chance. An Iron Mass with 18% increased attack speed, 200% increased physical damage, and 30% quality has 1.53 attacks per second and 217.8 average damage per hit. This results in 333.2 DPS. This value does not scale with minion level. Factoring in the triple damage modifier, this is approximately 1000 DPS, converted to chaos damage via Unholy Might.
The Iron Mass | Damage comparison | A level 20 Skeleton minion does ~1021.6 DPS by default, dealing physical damage.
The Iron Mass | Item acquisition | The Iron Mass has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
The Iron Mass | Item acquisition | The Iron Mass can be obtained from Curio Displays in the final reward rooms of any Grand Heist.
The Iron Mass | Legacy variants | The Iron Mass has a legacy variant.

The Jinxed Juju | Description | The Jinxed Juju is a unique Citrine Amulet.
The Jinxed Juju | Mechanics | 10% of Damage from Hits is taken from your Spectres' Life before you : this modifier shifts a portion of damage taken to your spectres ' life. With multiple spectres, the amount is divided evenly. If the spectres have energy shield (from Discipline or Grave Intentions ), damage will still be taken from their life, regardless of Damage Types. [1]
The Jinxed Juju | Mechanics | Damage removes spectre life after your mitigation and Sentinel of Radiance 's similar modifier, but before other resources (e.g. Frost Shield , Guard skills , Energy Shield , Life ). [2] It only interacts with the loss of resources; it does not change 'damage taken' for the purpose of recoup or Cast when Damage Taken Support.
The Jinxed Juju | Item acquisition | The Jinxed Juju can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

The Magnate | Description | The Magnate is a unique Studded Belt.
The Magnate | Mechanics | At 400 Strength, both the Double Damage and Triple Damage modifiers have a chance to activate independently. If a hit deals Triple Damage, it cannot also deal Double Damage. Functionally, you have a 5% chance to deal Triple Damage and 9.5% chance to deal Double Damage (due to 0.5% redundancy); with no other sources of Double or Triple Damage, this is effectively a 19.5% more Damage multiplier for Hits. [1]
The Magnate | Mechanics | Non-damaging ailments , fortification and stuns will still benefit fully from the tripled hit damage.
The Magnate | Item acquisition | The Magnate can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Magnate | Recipes | The Magnate can be created from the following recipes:

The Monastery Bell | Description | The Monastery Bell is a unique Dream Mace.
The Monastery Bell | Mechanics | Count as Blocking Attack Damage from the first target Hit with each Shield Attack : This modifier causes your shield attacks (e.g. Spectral Shield Throw ) to proc block effects as if an attack was blocked by the first target you've hit. Notably, this effect bypasses Shield Shatter 's effect of disabling block while you have the buff and allows it to be triggered repeatedly as there is no cooldown (though the buff will still prevent actual blocking of hits). It also bypasses Cannot Block on The Eternal Apple and allows Retaliation skills to become usable on demand.
The Monastery Bell | Mechanics | The target does not have to be an enemy; non-enemy targets such as Hydrosphere can be used to trigger on-block effects such as those found on Aegis Aurora.
The Monastery Bell | Item acquisition | The Monastery Bell can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Monastery Bell | Recipes | The Monastery Bell can be created from the following recipes:

The Oppressor | Infobox | Chance to Block: (34-38)% Armour: (444-595) Evasion: (444-595) Movement Speed: -3%. Requires Level 70 , 85 Str, 85 Dex. 120% increased Block Recovery. -10% to amount of Suppressed Spell Damage Prevented +(26-32)% chance to Suppress Spell Damage (200-250)% increased Armour and Evasion +(9-13)% Chance to Block You take 20% of Damage from Blocked Hits. The Oppressor | Description | The Oppressor is a unique Elegant Round Shield.
The Oppressor | Mechanics | Blocking#Taking damage from blocked hits
The Oppressor | Item acquisition | The Oppressor can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Oppressor | Recipes | The Oppressor can be created from the following recipes:
The Oppressor | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
The Oppressor | Design attribution | The art for the Oppressor was created by Grinding Gear Games senior concept/3D artist, Dane Brennand. [1] [2] Brennand also created the art for The Squire , though he described the Oppressor as the "cooler shield" visually, without knowing the item's stats. [3]
The Oppressor | Trivia | The Oppressor and The Squire were the first unique Elegant Round Shields to be added to the game in version 3.16.0. One day before the release of the version 3.16.0 update, the community team at Grinding Gear Games posted a teaser of the Oppressor on the Path of Exile subreddit, titled Trash to Trash —a play on words of Trash to Treasure. [4] When one redditor made a comment about how seemingly lackluster the Oppressor is, Chris Wilson replied stating that "[The Oppressor] is amazing at doing what it's supposed to do". [5] Although it was never explicitly stated, there is a strong implication that the developers included the Oppressor in the version 3.16.0 update in order to prevent players from deterministically acquiring the Squire. Without the existence of the Oppressor, the Squire would be the only possible outcome of upgrading an Elegant Round Shield to unique rarity using a Tainted Mythic Orb or using an Orb of Chance with the Trash to Treasure prophecy active.

The Perandus Manor | Description | The Perandus Manor is a unique Chateau Map.
The Perandus Manor | Description | Perandus Manor is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map. Its functionality is disabled; it cannot be used in the Map Device.
The Perandus Manor | Map Type | Indoors. Based on The Lord's Labyrinth.
The Perandus Manor | Strategy | The Perandus Manor contains three hidden boss rooms scattered about the map. Secret switches, of which the tags are hidden unless one's mouse is hovered directly over it, must be pressed in order to enter one of these rooms. Within these boss rooms, there will be more than one Perandus chest , similar to the ones found in the Perandus league (with the exception of them being referred to as Cadiro's rather than just Perandus'). Guarding these chests are packs of Perandus monsters and one of the above-listed bosses, of whom must be defeated in order to open the chest.
The Perandus Manor | Strategy | The map is considered completed by the Atlas' standards as soon as all the bosses are defeated, even if their respective chests remain unopened.
The Perandus Manor | Items found in this area | One of the Perandus chest (more precisely "Cadiro's" chest) of this map is guaranteed to drop one of the Perandus league exclusive items:
The Perandus Manor | Item acquisition | The Perandus Manor is drop disabled.
The Perandus Manor | Legacy variants | The Perandus Manor has legacy variants.
The Perandus Manor | Legacy variants | There is a vendor recipe that (as of 3.15.X), converts the legacy map to "current" (3.15 = Expedition map series) map series. However, there is a bug that the map will reverted to base tier, instead of the "shaped" tier due to the watchstone.

The Poet's Pen | Description | The Poet's Pen is a unique Somatic Wand. [1]
The Poet's Pen | Mechanics | One socketed spell is triggered at the end of an attack. If you have more than one spell socketed in a wand they will be alternately triggered between attacks, in order (top to bottom).
The Poet's Pen | Mechanics | Cooldown Time: When a supported spell is successfully cast, it and all supported spells of the same name are put on cooldown for 250 milliseconds. [2] [3] The resulting optimal attacks per second have certain breakpoints, which are lower than pure calculations would yield, likely due to technical constraints. [4]
The Poet's Pen | Mechanics | Dual wielding : Spells will trigger right after their corresponding wand attack. Spells of the same name share the cooldown even if they are socketed in each wand (Ex: 1 Arc in one Pen and another Arc in the other). Spells in main hand are triggered before spells in offhand.
The Poet's Pen | Item acquisition | The Poet's Pen can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Poet's Pen | Recipes | The Poet's Pen can be created from the following recipes:
The Poet's Pen | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
The Poet's Pen | Legacy variants | The Poet's Pen has legacy variants.
The Poet's Pen | Design attribution | The Poet's Pen was created by supporter Loate. [5] [6]

The Putrid Cloister | Boss | Unique Boss drops divination cards causes Headmistress Braeta to drop divination cards each time she flees/is defeated. These cards can include "boss_drop_card" tagged cards.
The Putrid Cloister | Item acquisition | The Putrid Cloister can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.

The Putrid Cloister | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
The Putrid Cloister | Item drops | Monsters in this area also have a high chance to drop Stacked Decks.
The Putrid Cloister | Ruithless mode | In Ruthless mode , Headmistress Braeta drops several gems upon killing her in the final room. The two transition fights do not drop any additional items. [1]
The Putrid Cloister | Legacy variants | The Putrid Cloister does not have legacy map mods. However, the map has legacy map series as the developers shuffled the atlas in version 3.1.0 , and then almost every league since version 3.5.0. With the exception that Ritual and Ultimatum leagues used the same map series.
The Putrid Cloister | Legacy variants | There is a vendor recipe that convert legacy unique map into the corresponding map of the current map series. However, there is a bug that reverted the "shaped" map (drops when watchstones/ voidstones are socketed) into base tier.
The Putrid Cloister | Design attribution | The Putrid Cloister was created by supporter Antnee. [2]

The Queen's Hunger | Description | The Queen's Hunger is a unique Vaal Regalia.
The Queen's Hunger | Mechanics | Every five seconds, one of the following offering skills is triggered in the following order: Bone Offering , Flesh Offering , Spirit Offering. One or more corpses must be consumed to grant their effects. The offering skills from this body armour will be supported by any socketed gems in this body armour, excluding Empower , Enlighten and Enhance Support. It is possible to overlap all three offering effects on you with Leash of Oblation and sources of skill duration.
The Queen's Hunger | Mechanics | Socketing any supportable trigger gems like Cast while Channelling Support , Cast when Damage Taken Support , Cast On Critical Strike Support and Cast on Melee Kill Support will add another trigger condition to the offerings, effectively disabling the offerings completely.
The Queen's Hunger | Veiled modifiers | The Queen's Hunger has two veiled modifiers :
The Queen's Hunger | Veiled prefix | Unveiling a Catarina's Veiled prefix draws from a pool of both exclusive veiled prefixes ( Catarina's ) and generic veiled prefixes ( Chosen ).
The Queen's Hunger | Veiled suffix | Unveiling an of the Veil suffix draws from a pool of generic veiled suffixes ( of the Order ).
The Queen's Hunger | Item acquisition | The Queen's Hunger has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
The Queen's Hunger | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
The Queen's Hunger | Ruthless mode | Although veiled rare items cannot be found in Ruthless mode , this item will still drop with veiled modifiers and can be unveiled.
The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | The Queen's Hunger has legacy variants.
The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | Legacy versions of The Queen's Hunger unveiled before version 3.26.0 could only roll generic body armour veiled prefixes/suffixes.
The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | Other veil mods are omitted from above table, including non-top tier crafted mods that also have mod value adjusted.
The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | Note that the crafted unveil modifiers can be removed from the crafting bench, but cannot be added back, effectively brick the item.
The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | Incomplete table of the second generation crafted mod on this item, and the current mod value range:

The Queen's Hunger | Legacy variants | Suffix

The Retch | Description | The Retch is a unique Rustic Sash.
The Retch | Mechanics | #% of Life Leech applies to enemies as Chaos Damage : this modifier causes hits to apply chaos DoT with a DPS and duration based on the recovery and duration of that hit's life leech.
The Retch | Mechanics | The Retch's DoT is independent of player recovery: hits will inflict it even if the player is on full life and loses their leech immediately, or when the player is unable to recover life due to Strength of Blood. Increases to player recovery, such as % increased Recovery Rate of Life or the more specific % increased total Recovery per second from Life Leech do not affect the Retch’s damage.
The Retch | Mechanics | Because the DoT is considered reflected damage [1] , it is not modified by the attacker’s offensive stats, only by stats on the enemy. Its duration can be extended by Temporal Chains , dealing the same DPS for a longer duration. Each instance of the DoT can deal damage; damage taken is unaffected by the per-second cap on recovery from leech [2].
The Retch | Mechanics | Ghost Reaver disables this mod completely. The Retch cannot deal damage in areas with Monsters cannot be Leeched from , or against monsters with Life Cannot Be Leeched. It has no interaction with Damage Taken from Hits is Leeched as Life.
The Retch | Mechanics | This modifier can be increased by Noxious and Fertile catalysts , up to 240%.
The Retch | Instant leech | Instant life leech modifiers (such as Bloodseeker or Leech Mastery ) cause this modifier to hit , dealing damage instantly. This can cause the damage to be mitigated differently, for example through chance to Avoid All Damage from Hits or by exiles wearing The Fourth Vow. Instant leech does not result in more total damage, as the Retch’s DoT can stack indefinitely.
The Retch | Interaction with proxies | Skills used by totems , traps , mines , mirages , and other proxies can inflict Retch DoT in the same way as the player. This uses the player’s stats for all calculations, including maximum life. [3] Because dead objects cannot leech, traps and mines can only leech before finishing their skill's attack or cast time.
The Retch | Interaction with proxies | DoT is also inflicted by hits that create leech on the player though mechanics like Bone Barrier or Machina Mitts.
The Retch | Interaction with proxies | Kills by Retch DoT from non-player objects are not attributed to the player, unlike most other forms of damage over time. When Retch DoT from no longer existing objects causes monsters to die, those monsters do not grant experience or flask charges, and their loot is unaffected by player item quantity and rarity [4].
The Retch | Scaling | As the damage applies only to the enemy that is leeched from, the amount of damage applied by each debuff is limited by the percentage of hit damage that is leeched - for instance, if a damaging hit has 2% of damage leeched as life, the debuff will apply 4% of the hit's damage to the enemy. Additionally, the total damage applied per hit is limited by the Maximum recovery per Life Leech stat, normally 10% of the player’s maximum life, whether it’s instant or not. [5] Therefore, in order to scale the Retch's damage, the player can increase the maximum duration of leech through % increased Maximum recovery per Life Leech , increase their maximum life, increase their hit rate, slow debuff expiration by cursing the enemy with Temporal Chains, or cause the enemy to take more chaos damage.
The Retch | Example damage calculation | A character has 8000 maximum life and 100% increased Maximum Recovery per Life Leech , setting the cap for its leech instances at 1600 total life. With 2% damage leeched as life, it caps a leech instance with 80,000 damage per hit, leeching 160 per second for 10 seconds. A 20% quality Retch will cause this hit to also inflict a DoT instance with a base DPS of 160x2.4=384 and the same 10 second duration.
The Retch | Example damage calculation | Against an enemy debuffed to -30% chaos resistance and 120% increased damage taken, this DoT deals 384x1.3x2.2=1098.24 DPS for 10 seconds. If the enemy is also debuffed by Temporal Chains, the same DPS will be dealt for 12.5 seconds instead.
The Retch | Example damage calculation | In this scenario, each 80,000 damage or higher hit will cause the Retch to deal an additional 13,728 damage (over 12.5 seconds).
The Retch | Item acquisition | The Retch has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
The Retch | Recipes | The Retch can be created from the following recipes:
The Retch | Recipes | The Retch itself is not a league-specific item. However, the ingredients of the vendor recipe were tied to Talisman league. There are additional ways to acquire league-restricted items, see the article for the League-specific items for more details. Since version 3.14.0 the ingredients are also included in the core drop table.
The Retch | Alternate artwork | The Retch in one of the Voidborn foil colors also exist in the trade site. It is not certain that it is acquired from Voidborn Reliquary directly, or via vendor recipe of foil in matching color.
The Retch | Alternate artwork | There is no Valdo's Map that award this unique in Celestial foil colors yet.
The Retch | Notes | The addition of a cap on the amount of life leeched per hit in 3.6.0 significantly reduced the power level of this item. Prior to that change, combining a large source of leech (e.g. Vessel of Vinktar ) with a source of instant leech ( Vaal Pact or the instant leech mod found on legacy Vessel of Vinktar) could add a significant multiplier to damage dealt.

The Sundered Will | Mechanics | Increases and Reductions to Chaos Damage also apply to Effect of Auras from Chaos Skills at (10-15)% of their value, up to a maximum of 150% : Depending on the roll, 1500-1000% increased chaos damage is required to cap the effect. The aura effect bonus is capped at 150% if multiple rings are equipped, though each ring will contribute to the scaling up to the cap. The Taming | Mechanics | #% increased Elemental Damage with Hits and Ailments for each type of Elemental Ailment on Enemy only applies once for each of Ignite , Chill , Freeze , Shock , Scorch , Brittle , and Sap on an enemy. Since the effect applies to each type of Elemental Ailment rather than each instance of it, additional stacks of the same type of ailment such as through Emberwake or Warden's notable passive skills Oath of Summer and Oath of Spring does not further increase damage beyond the first stack.

The Three Dragons | Infobox | Evasion: (96-103) Energy Shield: (20-22). Requires Level 35 , 40 Dex, 40 Int. +(26-30)% to all Elemental Resistances Your Fire Damage can Shock but not Ignite Your Cold Damage can Ignite but not Freeze or Chill Your Lightning Damage can Freeze but not Shock. "The ice seared his naked feet As the lightning stilled his heart, But it was the flames upon his lover's face That roused him to vengeance." - From 'The Three Dragons' by Victario of Sarn. Vendor Offer 12x Alchemy Shard 5x Alteration Shard
The Three Dragons | Description | The Three Dragons is a unique Golden Mask.
The Three Dragons | Item mechanics | The Three Dragons modifies the element required to inflict the four elemental Status Ailments : Shock , Ignite , Chill , and Freeze. Normally, the duration or effectiveness of these ailments depend on the amount of respective elemental damage of the hit that triggered them, but with Three Dragons the appropriate damage type for each ailment is changed as detailed in the item's description.
The Three Dragons | Item mechanics | This item does not convert stats or modifiers in any way, including modifiers such as "5% chance to Ignite". For example, if an enemy is struck by fire damage without Three Dragons it has a chance to be ignited based on the fire damage dealt equal to the sum of all "chance to Ignite" modifiers the player has. If the same hit is performed with Three Dragons equipped, the player may inflict a shock ailment based on the amount of fire damage dealt equal to the sum of all "chance to Shock" modifiers. This hit can no longer ignite the target (unless it also deals cold damage) and is therefore not affected by "chance to Ignite" modifiers in any way.
The Three Dragons | Item mechanics | Three Dragons prevents the player from applying Chill with cold damage, but does not allow the player to chill with lightning damage effectively disallowing the player from inflicting chill by dealing damage. You can still inflict chill on enemies by creating chilled ground , by using a source that directly chills enemies such as The Pandemonius or Arctic Armour , or by altering what types of damage can chill, such as with Callinellus Malleus or Dyadus.
The Three Dragons | Item mechanics | If equipped while the character has the Elemental Conflux buff, the damage from the hit will apply all status ailments that are not specifically excluded by the Three Dragons. This means hits of fire damage will shock and chill but not ignite, hits with cold damage will ignite and shock but not freeze or chill, and hits with lightning will chill and ignite but not shock.
The Three Dragons | Item acquisition | The Three Dragons can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Three Dragons | Recipes | The Three Dragons can be created from the following recipes:
The Three Dragons | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
The Three Dragons | Build of the Week | The Three Dragons was featured in Season 2 Episode 5 of the Build of the Week series released by Grinding Gear Games.

The Twilight Temple | Infobox | Map Level: 71 Map Tier: 4 Guild Character : ^ Item Quantity: +(75-100)% Item Rarity: +(125-175)% Monster Pack Size: +(25-50)%. The Twilight Temple | Description | The Twilight Temple is a unique Moon Temple Map.
The Twilight Temple | Description | The Twilight Temple is in the current map series (Keepers) for the Atlas of Worlds.
The Twilight Temple | Layout | The map is a large indoor area, with the right side being daytime at Solaris Temple and the left side being night time at Lunaris Temple. (Sometimes it is swapped however, where right is Lunaris and left is Solaris)
The Twilight Temple | Layout | Each side contains 3 rooms with a button of the sun or moon in the middle. Walking over it triggers an ambush of monsters. The northmost area contains two buttons which activate a portal, leading to either Helial, the Day Unending on the sun side or Selenia, the Endless Night on the moon side. In order to activate either of the buttons, you must first trigger the buttons in the 3 rooms on that side first. This portal cannot be changed once either boss is activated; you can kill either of the two bosses in a map. Note that only the buttons on one side of the map need to be pressed in order to complete the map.
The Twilight Temple | Layout | Helial, the Day Unending will always drop Invictus Solaris and Selenia, the Endless Night will always drop Vix Lunaris.
The Twilight Temple | Boss | There are 6 sidebosses. The Celestial orb forms of Helial and Selenia (untargetable, beat them by clearing all adds), as well as Aloris, the First Light , Nebria, the Last Light (based on unique Solar/Lunar soldiers (themselves are converted Blackguards ) from Solaris / Lunaris Concourses ), Opid, Helial's Herald and Arteth, Selenia's Herald (based on Harbingers of Solaris / Lunaris ).
The Twilight Temple | Boss | Completing all three fights on one side lets the player access the respective boss. If both sides are completed, the player can choose either of the bosses. The selection is triggered by standing on the relevant pressure plate (blue = Selenia, red = Helial).
The Twilight Temple | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
The Twilight Temple | Item acquisition | The Twilight Temple can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Twilight Temple | Recipes | The Twilight Temple can be created from the following recipes:
The Twilight Temple | Recipes | Divided Hideout can be found in the map by chance.
The Twilight Temple | Legacy variants | The Twilight Temple did not have a legacy variant in terms of explicit modifier. However, since 3.5 the map was on a new map series at the start of each league and since 3.9, it has variable tier based on watchstone/voidstone.

The Vigil | Description | The Vigil is a unique Crimson Jewel. It is a threshold jewel that enhances Vigilant Strike.
The Vigil | Mechanics | As a threshold jewel, the full effect of The Vigil can be gained only when it is placed in a jewel socket that meets the threshold requirement.
The Vigil | Mechanics | This jewel doubles the base duration of Fortify from 4 seconds to 8 seconds. Fortify duration is increased normally by Vigilant Strike , Fortify Support , as well as by any other x% increased Fortify duration modifiers. The final duration of this fortify applies to nearby allies for the full duration. Fortify with a shorter duration will not override this effect. It is unknown if a stronger fortify with more x% increased effect of Fortify on You will override a fortify with more duration.
The Vigil | Mechanics | When using General's Cry with Vigilant Strike , the summoned minions apply Fortify to the player and nearby allies. Using The Writhing Jar with vigilant strike will give fortify outside of combat that lasts a long time.
The Vigil | Snapshot | The fortify buff Snapshots on use. This means you still gain the benefits of fortify after unequipping Vigilant Strike and/or The Vigil. The simplest way to do this is by weapon swapping.
The Vigil | Item acquisition | The Vigil is drop disabled.
The Vigil | Legacy variants | The Vigil has legacy variants.

The Vinktar Square | Description | The Vinktar Square is a unique Courtyard Map.
The Vinktar Square | Description | The Vinktar Square is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map.
The Vinktar Square | Layout | The shape of the map is a large square. In each corner, there is a guardian boss of a cardinal direction. After all four guardians are killed, a gate in the center of the map will open. The gate leads to a room with the final boss, the Avatar of Thunder.
The Vinktar Square | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
The Vinktar Square | Item drops | Notably, the shock proliferation variant ( Vessel of Vinktar ) appears to be exclusive to Tier 12+ The Vinktar Square maps only, while the other four variants can drop at any tier. It is also noticably rarer than the other variants, at an estimated 5% drop rate compared to approximately 24% drop rate for the other variants. [1]
The Vinktar Square | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.

The Vinktar Square | Legacy variants | The Vinktar Square has legacy variants.
The Vinktar Square | Legacy variants | Note that there is a vendor recipe to convert map to the current series. The level override modifier is retained on legacy maps converted to a map series from Archnemesis onwards.

The Will of Esh | Description | The Will of Esh is a unique Synaptic Ring.
The Will of Esh | Mechanics | Increases and Reductions to Lightning Damage also apply to Effect of Auras from Lightning Skills at (10-15)% of their value, up to a maximum of 150% : Depending on the roll, 1500-1000% increased lightning damage is required to cap the effect. The aura effect bonus is capped at 150% if multiple rings are equipped, though each ring will contribute to the scaling up to the cap. Note that only modifiers to global lightning damage apply (i.e. lightning damage with attacks do not apply). Modifiers to elemental damage do not apply. Large cluster jewels and The Light of Meaning can provide large quantities of this stat.
The Will of Esh | Mechanics | The effect can apply to auras from support gems supporting a lightning skill (e.g. curse auras ( Blasphemy Support ) or High-Impact Mine Support ) [1].

The Winds of Fate | Description | The Winds of Fate is a unique Foul Staff.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Maximum Critical Strike Chance is 50% : This modifier applies a global cap to your crit chance for all skills. If a skill would have over 50% critical strike chance, it is considered as 50%. It does not prevent effects that cause a skill to always deal a critical strike.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Lucky applies separately from crit chance, allowing for the player to effectively have a 75% chance to crit instead by rolling for crits twice. [1] Sources of lucky crits include the Your Critical Strike Chance is Lucky while Focused veiled modifier and crafted modifier and the Dance with Death Keystone.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Plume of Pursuit can synergize well with this unique on a character with Battlemage such as from Instruments of Virtue ; Plume of Pursuit's Spell Skills always deal Critical Strikes on final Repeat bypasses the 50% crit chance cap, allowing the final repeat of a Spell Echoed spell to always crit. This combination can also make good use of Marylene's Fallacy 's crit multi. Hydrosphere in particular synergizes with all parts of this interaction, and bypasses the Chaos conversion from Winds of Fate due to conversion order.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Lightning Tendrils also has good synergy with this weapon with Battlemage , as you can guarantee a crit every 3 hits.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Glacial Hammer of Shattering is another skill that guarantees a crit every 3 hits, and as such has good synergy with this weapon. Since damage conversion on skill gems take priority over all other conversion sources, all the physical damage will be converted to cold.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Alternatively, this weapon can be used as an attack weapon for things such as Shockwave Support or Perfect Agony ignite builds. This weapon has the highest weapon DPS of any unique item.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Destructive Link 's Linked target's Main Hand Critical Strike Chance is equal to yours overrides the 50% crit chance cap if the caster is using a weapon with a base crit chance superior to 50% like Trypanon.
The Winds of Fate | Mechanics | Binds of Bloody Vengeance allows you to always critically hit as long as you've taken a Critical Strike in the last 4 seconds.
The Winds of Fate | Item acquisition | The Winds of Fate has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from Lycia, Herald of the Scourge when The Second Sacrament is applied to a Sanctum with a level 83 Forbidden Tome.
The Winds of Fate | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
The Winds of Fate | Legacy variants | The Winds of Fate has legacy variants.

The Wise Oak | Description | The Wise Oak is a unique Bismuth Flask.
The Wise Oak | Mechanics | This flask provides a defensive buff to whichever resistances share the lowest uncapped value, and a penetration buff to whichever resistances share the highest uncapped value. If any or all of your resistances match, they will share benefits.
The Wise Oak | Mechanics | For example:
The Wise Oak | Mechanics | In this scenario, you gain the penetration bonus to Fire and Cold because they are tied for the highest value. Lightning will get the defensive buff.
The Wise Oak | Mechanics | In this scenario, you are in fact getting the offensive and the defensive buff to every element. [1]
The Wise Oak | Item acquisition | The Wise Oak can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Wise Oak | Recipes | The Wise Oak can be created from the following recipes:
The Wise Oak | Ruthless mode | Utility Flasks cannot gain charges while active in Ruthless mode. This flask also has a flask duration of 5.50 seconds instead.
The Wise Oak | Design attribution | The Wise Oak was created by supporter ToBeSomebody [2] The design was inspired by Pokemon, where Professor Oak gives the player of 3 starter Pokemon who countered each other; Fire, Water & Grass. It is said the bandit lord Oak instead mashed these Pokemon into a tasty beverage for exiles.
The Wise Oak | Legacy variants | The Wise Oak has legacy variants.

To achieve godhood, they would cast off all that god has gifted us. Mortality, compassion, even the sanctity of flesh. - High Templar Voll. Right click to drink. Can only hold charges while in belt. Refills as you kill monsters. Acquisition Fishing. Vendor Offer 6x Alteration Shard
The Writhing Jar | Description | The Writhing Jar is a unique Hallowed Hybrid Flask.
The Writhing Jar | Mechanics | The Writhing Jar allows players to create enemies on demand, primarily to obtain on- hit and on-kill effects, as long as flask charges are available. Despite being a hybrid flask, this flask can be used even while on full health and mana.
The Writhing Jar | Mechanics | The Writhing Worms spawned by The Writhing Jar are normal enemies, granting 1 warcry power each. They do not generate flask charges or souls for Vaal skills or Soul Eater. [1] [2] Writhing Worms last approximately 10 seconds, or 1 hit, and do not leave corpses (even when killed by non-hit damage or duration).
The Writhing Jar | Mechanics | Despite having very low maximum life, approximately 700-800 in a level 83 area [3] , they have an ailment threshold similar to a level 83 normal monster. This prevents them from being overly advantageous targets for effects like Cruelty or Fortification.
The Writhing Jar | Mechanics | Because worms are destroyed on hit, on-hit ailments have no opportunity to proliferate from them. Elemental ailments can be inflicted on them and spread with on-kill effects like Berek's Respite. (For unclear reasons, this is not the case for non-elemental ailments.)
The Writhing Jar | Item acquisition | The Writhing Jar can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
The Writhing Jar | Recipes | The Writhing Jar can be created from the following recipes:
The Writhing Jar | Trivia | The Writhing Jar was created by Rory Rackham. Rory loves the flask, while Mark hates it, leading to an endless cycle of Wormblaster nerfs.

Titucius' Span | Modifiers | The +25% additional Block Chance against Projectiles modifier applies to only attack projectiles and stacks additively. [1] In order to get 'chance to block projectile spells', 'x% of block chance applied to spells' is needed. For example, without any block passives, when using Titucius' Span and Stone of Lazhwar the resulting chance to block projectile spells is 24% ((23% + 25%) * 0.50).

To Dust | Description | To Dust is a unique Cobalt Jewel. It can only be obtained by corrupting a Cobalt Jewel. In patch 3.26 a sample of 576 cobalt jewels yielded 29 To Dust, or a 5.0% chance per corrupted jewel.
To Dust | Item acquisition | To Dust has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced.
To Dust | Recipes | To Dust can be created from the following recipes:
To Dust | Legacy variants | To Dust has legacy variants.
To Dust | Legacy variants | Not corrupted To Dust are all legacy items and only be obtained from trading with other players in the permanent leagues. Also, corrupted To Dust that have corruption implicit are also legacy.
To Dust | Legacy variants | The double legacy item (pre-3.0) use the mod (3-5)% increased Skeleton Movement Speed, (7-10)% increased Skeleton Attack Speed, (7-10)% increased Skeleton Cast Speed MaximumMinionCountUniqueJewel1 Intrinsic (3-5)% increased Skeleton Movement Speed (7-10)% increased Skeleton Attack Speed (7-10)% increased Skeleton Cast Speed , which the mod #% increased Skeleton Attack Speed inherited the numeric value from pre- 3.0.0 mod text +2 to Maximum number of Skeletons but not the function. The old mod have a hidden value of 0 for maximum number of Spectres and maximum number of Zombies thus the 3.0.0 change made the legacy jewel has a default value of 0 for #% increased Skeleton Cast/Movement Speed. New version use 3 mods each for Skeleton Attack/Cast/Movement Speed.
To Dust | Legacy variants | Using a Divine Orb can "add" the mods text (7-10)% increased Skeleton Cast Speed and (3-5)% increased Skeleton Movement Speed back to the item, as well as change the numeric value to (7-10)% increased Skeleton Attack Speed.

Tombfist (1 Abyssal Socket) | Legacy variants | Note that the mod value of #% increased Attack Speed from a double legacy item can be change to 5-10% when using a Divine Orb and at the same time removing #% increased maximum Life as the dev has set a new default value of zero for that mod.
Tombfist (1 Abyssal Socket) | Legacy variants | It may be possible to restore bricked double legacy variant that have hidden 0% increased Attack Speed mod due to the use of Divine Orb previously (during 3.6.0-3.20.X) by using a Divine Orb on the item again. However, by doing so it will "remove" the #% increased maximum Life , making the bricked double legacy variant identical to current variant. Which current variant would be cheaper than legacy and double legacy variants, and bricked double legacy variant are visually identical to legacy variant unless in adv mod description mode. So that no point to use Divine Orb on any legacy Tombfist.

Tremor Rod | Description | Tremor Rod is a unique Military Staff.
Tremor Rod | Modifiers | Applicable skill gems socketed in this staff are supported by level 10 Blastchain Mine Support. All support stats are accounted for, including the cost and reservation multiplier.
Tremor Rod | Modifiers | The Mines can be Detonated an additional time modifier 're-arms' the initial mine after the first detonation, allowing for a second detonation. It does not create an additional mine. [1] This can be problematic when using skills with longer durations such as Pyroclast Mine , as they cannot be detonated again until the novas stop casting. It is also important to note that the 're-arm' of the mines does not reset the mine duration timer. The mine laid by the character even after 're-arming' will have the original mine duration of 16 seconds.
Tremor Rod | Modifiers | The Efficient Explosives notable which grants "Mines have a 15% chance to be Detonated an Additional Time" has no effect with Tremor Rod, similar to effects which grant a chance to deal double damage having no effect beyond 100%.
Tremor Rod | Modifiers | Spells with cooldown such as Cold Snap cannot be detonated again until after a cooldown. For movement spells such as Flame Dash and Lightning Warp , the mine will be relocated to the targeted location before its second detonation.
Tremor Rod | Item acquisition | Tremor Rod can drop anywhere. It can be chanced.
Tremor Rod | Recipes | Tremor Rod can be created from the following recipes:
Tremor Rod | Foulborn modifiers | This unique item can be Foulborn with one or more of the following Foulborn modifiers replacing the corresponding regular modifiers.
Tremor Rod | Legacy variants | Tremor Rod has legacy variants.

Twisted Distant Memory | Description | Twisted Distant Memory is a unique Synthesised Map. The base map may have variable tier, but the area level of the unique map is override as 81.
Twisted Distant Memory | Description | Twisted Distant Memory is not part of the Atlas of Worlds. There is no bonus objective for this map. However, this map can be witnessed by the Maven as part of the prerequisites of Maven's Invitation: The Forgotten.
Twisted Distant Memory | Description | Despite not being on the atlas, this map can still be affected by atlas passive skills that affect Synthesised Maps. Note that atlas passive skills cannot affect maps from Kirac's atlas missions.
Twisted Distant Memory | Modifiers | The map has two pools of map modifiers, one draw from "synthesis global" pool (mod domain = 19), one from "synthesis local" pool (mod domain = 18). Synthesis map modifiers are not visible on the map itself, and these modifiers are only revealed upon entering the map.
Twisted Distant Memory | Layout | The map uses the Park Map tileset. The area contains several wagons and lamps along the path. The boundaries of the map are surrounded in blue memory decay typical of a Synthesis zone, but cannot be directly interacted with. A portal to the boss room is located at the top left.
Twisted Distant Memory | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop from this monster.
Twisted Distant Memory | Item drops | The following drop-restricted items can drop in this area.
Twisted Distant Memory | Item acquisition | Twisted Distant Memory has restrictions on where or how it can drop. It cannot be chanced. Drops from T14+ Map Bosses, and sometimes sold by Kirac or Tujen.

Umbilicus Immortalis | Description | Umbilicus Immortalis is a unique Leather Belt.
Umbilicus Immortalis | Mechanics | Flasks you Use apply to your Raised Zombies and Spectres : Flasks apply to zombies and spectres instead of the player's character. This allows the use of Enkindling Orb enchantments on these flasks without any downside; as the flasks are not affecting the player, the penalty of Gains no Charges during Effect does not apply (they apply to the minions, but flask charges from minion kills are always allocated to the player) and will allow the player to gain flask charges.
